volume.h1: BOMBAY-KARNATAK INSCRIPTIONS.[= South Indian Inscription Vol. 20]

inscription.head:
No. 1.
(B.K. No. 123 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i:
On the right and left sides of the Nāga image fixed in the right prākāra-wall
of the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p: The record is dated in the 12th regnal year, Hēmanta, 7th fortnight, 1st day.

This short inscription in Prakrit language records the gift of a Nāga, a tank and
a vihāra by Mahābhuvī the king's daughter Sivakhadanāgasiri (Śivaskandanāgaśrī)
whose sons were alive and who was the mother of the prince (yuvarāja). The
minister Khadasāti was the superintendent of the work. The Nāga was made by
Naṭaka, pupil of [I]damōraka, the Sajayataka (inhabitant of Saṁjayantī).

This is published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XXXIV, pp. 239-42; cf. also Ind. Ant.,
Vol. XIV, pp. 331 ff. and Lu7ders' List, No. 1186.

inscription.head:
No. 2.
(B.K. No. 101 of 1935-36.)
HIRĒBIDRI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: On a stone fixed in the gate-way of the Kallēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
The inscription, in characters of about the 7th century A.D., records the gift of
land by Mārākona of Guppara (Gupta ?) family.

inscription.head:
No. 3.
(B.K. No. 39 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: On a slab fixed in the left wall of the śaṁkha-basti at Basti-Baṇa.

inscription.p:
It registers a gift of 500 nivarttanas of land to the north of Puligere-nagara, for
the worship of and offerings to the God Śaṁkha-Jinēndra, by Durgaśakti, son of
Kundaśakti and grandson of Vijayaśakti, of the Sēndra lineage.
Published in Ind. Ant., Vol. VII, pp. 106-07, text lines 51-61.

edition.p:
1. This and Nos. 4-7 below are engraved in characters of about the 10th-11th cent. A.D.
They appear to be later copies of earlier copper-plate grants.

inscription.head:
No. 4.
(B.K. No. 37 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: On a slab built into the right wall of the Śaṁkha-basti at Basti-Baṇa.

inscription.p: The record is dated Śaka 608, 5th regnal year of the king, Pausha, Paurṇimā.

It records the grant, by the king, of the village Haḍagile situated in Beḷuvala-300
for the repairs, worship and offerings to the God of Śaṅkha Jinālaya, and also for
a dāna-śālā, at the request of Dhruvadēvāchārya of Mūla-saṅgha and Dēva-gaṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 5.
(B.K. No. 34 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: On a slab built into the right wall of the Śaṁkha-basti at Basti-Baṇa.

inscription.p:
This is dated in Śaka 645, the 28th regnal year of the king, Bhādrapada, Paurṇimā,
lunar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 723, August 20, which was a Friday.

It records the gift of the village of Seṁboḷal, to the temple of Jina-Bhaṭṭāraka
at Purikara-nagara by Bikki Rāṇaka, at the request of a certain Vijaya, son of
Drōṇamma. The gift was made over to Jayadēva-paṇḍitāchārya of Mūla-saṁgha.
The gift village is said to have been situated to the south of Purikara-nagara.

inscription.head:
No. 6.
(B.K. No. 35 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the right wall of the Śaṁkha-basti at Basti-Baṇa.

inscription.p:
The record is dated in Śaka 651, 34th regnal year of the king, Phālguṇa, Paurṇimā,
corresponding to A.D. 730, February 7, which was Tuesday.

It records the gift of the village of Kaddama to the south of Purikara-nagara for
the worship of Saṁkha-Jinēndra and for the maintenance of a dāna-śālā in the temple.
The gift was made by the king at the instance of his preceptor Niravadya-paṇḍita.

inscription.head:
No. 7.
(B.K. No. 40 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒS11VAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: On a slab fixed into the left wall of the Śaṁkha-basti at Basti-Baṇa.

inscription.p:
The inscription is dated in Śaka 656, second regnal year of the king, Māgha,
Paurṇimā, corresponding to A.D. 735, January 13, which was Thursday.

It records the gift, by the king, of 50 nivarttanas of land, at the request of
Bāhubaliśrēshṭhi, for repairs and offerings to Dhavaḷa-Jinālaya, and also for mainte-
nance of a dāna-śālā. The gift was entrusted to Vijayadēva-paṇḍitāchārya of
Mūla-saṅgha and Dēva-gaṇa.
Text published in Ind. Ant., Vol. VII, pp. 106-07, text lines 62-82.

l.ka:
tārṇṇavaṁ dakṣiṇōnnatadaṁṣṭrāgraviśrāntabhuvanaṁ vapuḥ || śrīmatāṁ sakaḷabhuvana-
saṁstūyamānamānavyasagōtrāṇāṁ hārītiputrāṇāṁ saptalō-

inscription.head:
No. 8.
(B.K. No. 96 of 1935-36.)
SIḌĒNŪR, HIREKERUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: On a hero-stone set up near the Kallēs11vara temple.

inscription.p:
The record, in characters of about the 8th century A.D., refers to some attack and
states that Siṅga-Vaddagi Rāpamman made the Sirivāḷḷa.

inscription.head:
No. 9.
(B.K. No. 115 of 1938-39.)
ĀḌŪR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: On a stone standing on the top of a hill (survey No. 146).

inscription.p:
The record, in characters of about the 7-8th century A.D., contains two parts.
The first part, registers gift of a field for the dāna-s11ālā of a Jinālaya constructed by a
gāmuṇḍa. The epigraph was erected by Srīpāla, grandson of Dharma-gāmuṇḍa, and
disciple of Prabhā[chandra ?] of Paralūra-gaṇa.

It further records a grant of wet land to the west of Karmmagere (?), for the
benefit of the Jinālaya, by Dōṇa-gāmuṇḍa and others with the permission of Mā-
dhavatti when Sindarasa was governing Gaṅgi-Pāṇḍivura. Prabhāchandra-gurava of
Paralūra-gaṇa was the recipient of the gift.

Published in Ind., Ant., Vol. XI, pp. 68-• See also Karnataka Inscriptions,
Vol. I, pp. 4 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 10.
(B.K. No. 94 of 1935-36.)
SIḌĒNŪR, HIREKERUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the temple of Kallēśvara.

inscription.p:
This damaged hero-stone refers itself to the reign of Dōrapparasa (Dhruva) and
states that when Mārakka-arasa was governing Banavāsi and Binaēti (Binaēti) Abbe
was administering the town (probably Siḍēnūr itself), a hero (name not clear) died
in a fight. Reference has been made to Siḍiyanūr and probably to a fight with the
Gaṅgas.

l.ka: © svasti śrī dōrapparasar pṛthivīrājyaṁgeye mārakka·arasar banavāsi-
nāḍāḷe bina·eti·abbe ·ūrāḷe muḍugāra toṇṇindara maganuṁ porigoṇṇindamma-

l.note: The letters ra and ḍi are written below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 11.
(B.K. No. 16 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒS11VAR, SHIRATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken stone built into the wall of the Taluk-office.

inscription.p:
This record is not dated but is assigned to A.D. 793 by Dr. Fleet who has
edited it in Ep. Ind., Vol. VI, pp. 163 ff.

It refers itself to the reign of Srīvallabha and records a grant in the form of
proportionate quantity of the goods turned out by the weavers. The grant was made
by the headman of the guild of the weavers of nūruṁkēri of Purigere i.e., the present
Lakshmēs11var.

inscription.head:
No. 12.
(B.K. No. 113 of 1938-39.)
GUḌIGĒRI, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone near the Parvatadēva temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription in characters of about the 8th century A.D., refers
itself to the reign of a king named Mārassaḻba.

It mentions one Daḍigarasa as governing nāḍu and also a certain Śubhachandra-
paṇḍita. Mention is also made of a ṁēṇṭi of gōsāsa of Indamma.
The inscription is published in Ep. Ind., Vol. VI, p. 257.

inscription.head:
No. 13.
SAUNDATTI, PARASGAD TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.
(B.K. No. 58 of 1935-36.)

p.i: On a slab set up in the Basti-guḍi.

inscription.p:
The inscription in Sanskrit and Kannaḍa language is dated in Śaka 797, Manmatha,
but is engraved in characters of about the 11th or 12th century A.D. The Śaka and
the cyclic year correspond to A.D. 875-76.

It records a grant of eighteen nivartanas of land by Mahāsāmanta Pṛithvīrāma,
the son Mēṛaḍa, to a Jinēndra-bhavana got built by himself. The donee is stated to
be the disciple of Indrakīrtisvāmin who in turn was the disciple of Guṇakīrti-
munīśvara. The latter again was the disciple of Muḷḷabhaṭṭa of Maiḷāpatīrtha and
Kārēyagaṇa.

Lines 1 to 4 record a later grant, made by Kanna, of six nivartanas of land in
the sīvaṭa Muḷgunda in Sugandhavartti-12. Lines 18-19 state that the grant made
by Pṛithvīrāma was renewed by Kārttavīrya and (his wife) Bhāgalāṁbikā on the
occasion of a solar eclipse.

The donors of all the grants are of the Raṭṭa family, Mērada being the first
member.

edition.h4: Text. The inscription is published in JBBRAS, Vol. X, pp. 196 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 14.
(B.K. No. 100 of 1935-36.)
HIRĒBIDRI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up in the compound of the Kallēśvara Temple.

inscription.p: The record seems to refer to the date [Śaka] 800.

This damaged inscription seems to state that a certain Nāgati, son of
Duggatigauḍa died in a cattle-raid, at Sālatti. Makara-Ijja was the governor.

inscription.head:
No. 15.
(B.K. No. 93 of 1935-36.)
SIḌENUR, HIREKERUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in front of the temple of Kallēśvara.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 826, Ratyakshiti, which is apparently a mistake
for Raktākshi. The year given coincided with A.D. 904-05.

The inscription states that, at that time, Lōkaṭe was governing Banavāsi Twelve-
thousand and refers to his attack on Pombuḷcha (modern Huṁcha) and the conquest
of the fort of that place when Nāgavarma was administering Siḍiyanūr. Lōkayya
(i.e., Lōkaṭe) made some grant to a gāvuṇḍa, whose name is lost. Satyaḷge Seventy
was under the control of Bijja. The latter portion of the record is badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 16.
(B.K. No. 37 of 1938-39.)
ASŪTI, RAMDURG TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken pillar in front of the Kalmēśvara Temple.

inscription.p:
The record refers to the year Vikrama which probably is the one that fell in
Indra III's reign and which coincided with A.D. 920-21.

This record refers to the reign of Indraballala and states that in the cyclic year
Vikrama, Saḍagarara Tiḻtamma made a gift of gōsahasra.

inscription.head:
No. 17.
(B.K. No. 61 of 1937-38.)
RŪGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Two sides of the elephant-stone (āne-kallu) set up in front of the
wall of the Hanumān Temple.

inscription.p: The record is dated Śaka 863 corresponding to A.D. 941-42.

This inscription is very severely damaged. The letters Amō in line 11
suggest that the inscription belongs to Amōghavarsha.

inscription.head:
No. 18.
(B.K. No. 57 of 1935-36.)
SAUNDATTI, PARASAGAD TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i: On a slab set up in the Basti-guḍi.

inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 902, Vikrama, Paushya, śu. 10, Thursday, Uttarāyaṇa-
Saṅkrāuti. The Saṅkrānti occurred on Thursday, December 23, A.D. 980. The tithi
was, however, śu. 14 on that day and not śu. 10 as stated in the record.

The inscription registers the grant of 150 mattars of land by the Raṭṭa chief
Śāntivarmarasa to the Jaina monastery (basadi) caused to be constructed by him at
Sugandhavarti (i.e., modern Saundatti). The genealogy of this chief is given. The
gift was entrusted to Bāhubali-bhaṭṭāraka. It also records another gift of 150 mattars
of land by the chief's mother Nījiyabbarasi to the same monastery.
The record is published in JBBRAS. Vol. X, p. 204.
1. Continuation is worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 19.
(B.K. No. 59 of 1937-38.)
RŪGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing near the front wall of the Hanumān temple (front and right sides).

inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 936, Ānanda, Phālguṇa, Amāvāsya, Sunday, Saṁkrānti, corres-
ponding to A.D. 1015, February 20, Sunday, f.d.t. ·56. The Mīna saṁkrānti occurred
on the following day.

The record gives a genealogical account of the Chālukya dynasty in the begin-
ning commencing from Raṇarāga, son of Jayasiṁha I, and ending with Vasudhai-
kamalla Ayyaṇadēva.

It registers a gift of 60 mattars of land, three gardens, one oil-mill and three
shops, by Mahāsāmanta Dēvaṇarasa described as the brother of the king, to god
Nagarēśvara. Moṟaḍiya Chāmuṇḍayya, the Sēnabōva, wrote the record. It mentions
Chandraśēkharāchārya of the Nagarēśvara temple and also certain other individuals.

l.note: Some space is left blank after

l.ka: sya tasya tadā phalaṁ || svadattaṁ pa[ra]-

inscription.head:
No. 20.
(B.K. No. 92 of 1935-36.)
SIḌĒNŪR, HIREKERUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: On a slab set up close to the temple of Kallēśvara.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 937, Rākshasa, Uttarāyaṇa, [Paurṇamāsi]
corresponding to December 24, A.D. 1015.

It seems to record certain gifts made by several Gāvuṇḍas of Siḍiyanūru, when
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kali Kāṭimayyarasa was governing Banavāsi-12,000 and his
subordinate Vā[vaṇa]rasa was administering Sattaḷige-70.

l.ka: mire svasti sakanripakāḷātītasaṁvatsarasataṁga 93[7] neya rākṣasa saṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 21.
(B.K. No. 58 of 1937-38.)
RŪGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing near the front wall of the Hanumān temple (back and left sides).

inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 941, Siddhārthin, Paushya, ba. 10, Thursday, Uttarāyaṇa-
saṁkrānti corresponding regularly to December 24, A.D. 1019.

It records that while the king was ruling from Ētagiri, and his son Mahāsāmanta
Dāsarasa was governing the eighteen agrahāras including Rūvige, situated in
Tarddavāḍi-nāḍu, Mahāsāmanta Dhanasaṅgrahayyarasa of the Seḷaṟa family made
a gift of land to God Nagarēśvara of Āneya-Rūvige, for worship and offerings as also
for the maintenance of the ascetics of the place. The gift was entrusted to
Bāḷachandrapaṇḍita.
The king is stated to have been ruling from his capital (nelevīḍu) Ētagiri.

inscription.head:
No. 22.
(B.K. No. 26 of 1936-37.)
GŌLLAGĒRI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in front of the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 9[50], Pra[bhava], Vaiśākha śu. [8], Monday,
corresponding to A.D. 1027, April 16, the weekday, however, being Sunday and not
Monday. The Śaka year was current.

It registers a grant of land to the temple built by Bāchagāvuṇḍa, by
Mahāsāmanta Daṇḍanātha Rebbarasa. The gift land was situated in his mānya at
Gollagēri.

inscription.head:
No. 23.
(B.K. No. 25 of 1936-37.)
DĒVŪR, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the house-site to the left of the Rāmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 951, Śukla, Pushya, ba. 1, Wednesday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti,
corresponding regularly to A.D. 1029, December 24.

It records a gift, by the queen Suggaladēvi. of certain lands to Brahmarāsipaṇḍita
of the Pāśupata school, for worship and offerings to god Mārasinghēśvara of Dēvapura
and also for maintaining ascetics and students.

l.ka: da baṭṭeyiṁ teṁka hipparageya sīmeyiṁ mūḍa
haḷḷadiṁ mūḍaṇa tribhōga suṁka tēja sāmya dēvargge

inscription.head:
No. 24.
(B.K. No. 116 of 1938-39.)
ĀḌŪR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing in a field to the west of the village.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 956, Bhāva, Saṁkrānti, Sunday. In the
given year, corresponding to A.D. 1034-35 no Saṁkrānti occurred on a Sunday. The
date cannot as such be verified.

It records that when Mahāmātya [Perggaḍe Mādimayya] was administering
Banavāsi 12,000 (including Sāntaḷige 1000) and Mahāmṇḍalēs11vara Mayūrvarmma was
governing the Pānumgal-500, gifts of land and garden, etc., were made to the temple
of Banasaṁkaradēva by certain individuals, whose names are not clear. It gives the
genealogical account of a certain Barma and seems to describe the glory of Vāmadēva
who was the disciple of Dēvarāsi Jīyya and sthānādhipa of the Svayaṁbhū temple.
This Vāmadēva was possibly the donee.

inscription.head:
No. 25.
(B.K. No. 73 of 1937-38.)
TAḌAVALGA, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone now used as door frame of the central shrine of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
The record is badly damaged, the ends of all the lines being mutilated. There
are two dates, but both are incomplete (1) • • • • • pushya, Amāvaśyā, eclipse.
(2) • • • Īśvara, Saṁkrānti. The latter date, falling in the reign period of the king
corresponds to A.D. 1037-38.

The record contains the eulogy of a subordinate chief (name lost). It mentions
Rēvaṇa-Bhaṭṭa and seems to record a gift of land on the latter date.

p.i: On the left bar of the door-frame.

edition.h4: On the right bar of the door-frame.

inscription.head:
No. 26.
(B.K. No. 92 of 1936-37.)
BIJĀPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab No. 47 in the Archaeological Museum.

inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 962 Vikrama, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti corresponding to
A.D. 1040, December 24 which was Wednesday.

It registers gift of land, house-site, oil-mill and garden to God Mūlasthāna-
Mahādēva at Benakanakaviṭṭage situated near Sindige-12 which was a Kaṁpaṇa, by
Perggaḍe Mēlayya, in the presence of Manneya Nāyimarasa and Mahāsāmanta
Mūkarasa. The latter, who is styled Banavāsipuravarēśvara, is stated to have been
administering the mēlāḷke of Pagariṭe-nāḍu.

l.ka: biṭṭa keyyariya nela matta 50 manneya nivē-
yasya yasya yadā bhūmistasya tasya ta-

inscription.head:
No. 27.
(B.K. No. 55 of 1936-37.)
KANNOḶḶI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the left wall of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 962, Vikrama, Māgha, ba. 9, Thursday, corresponding to A.D.
1041, January 29.

It records a gift of land to Vāmaśaktipaṇḍita, for the worship of god Bhōgēśvara
of Kaṇṇavaḷḷi by Mahāsāmanta Gōpālarasa who was administering Kaṇṇavaḷḷi. Mention
is made of the neleviḍu of Poṭṭaḷakeṟe.

inscription.head:
No. 28.
(B.K. No. 76 of 1938-39.)
KAMḌOḶḶI, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing in the Siddhēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This badly worn out record refers itself to the reign of Jagadēkamalla Jayasiṁha
and to a Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara, Prachaṇḍa-daṇḍanāyaka (name lost). The date and other
details of the record are lost. Only the imprecatory portion is extant.

l.note:
After this about 25 lines are damaged and worn out. Then follow the imprecatory
svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā, etc.

inscription.head:
No. 29.
(B.K. No. 88 of 1938-39.)
KUNDGŌL, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Broken slab lying in a field called Brahmadēvara-hola,
about two miles from the village.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 965, Tāraṇa, Chaitra, śu. 5, Tuesday,
corresponding to A.D. 1044, March 6, Tuesday, f.d.t. ·15, the Śaka year being
current.

The inscription seems to record gifts of land, house-site, etc. It mentions
Bhāḷachandrēśvaradēva.

l.ka: ga 9[6]5 neya tāraṇa [saṁ]vatsarada [cai]tra suddha paṁcami maṁ • • • • •
gaṁ vāraṇāsi kurukṣētra prayāge gaṁge gaṁgāsāgara [śrī] • • • • •
bī tīrtthasthānadoḷe sāsira kavileya kōḍuṁ koḷagu • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 30.
(B.K. No. 6 of 1936-37.)
ĀLMĒL, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the temple of Pāvaḍi Basappa.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 966, Tāraṇa, Pushya, śu. 2, Monday,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti corresponding to A.D. 1044, December 24.

It refers to a Mahāsāmanta (name lost) who is described as the Lord of Tagara-
nagara, as ruling at Eḷamēla and records a gift of land to the temple of Uttarēśvara
It mentions Brahmarāśi.

inscription.head:
No. 31.
(B.K. No. 117 of 1938-39.)
ĀḌŪR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing near the Kalamēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 966, Tāraṇa, Saṁkrānti, the other details being lost.
It corresponds to A.D. 1044-45.

It records a gift of land to god Kāḷēśvara, probably by Barmagāvuṇḍa, when
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Mayūravarma of the Kadamba family was governing Pānuṁgal-
500 division and Daṇḍanāyaka Ēchayyabhaṭṭa was administering Pāṇḍiyūr. The
record gives the goaealogical account of the donor.

inscription.head:
No. 32.
(B.K. No. 93 of 1938-39.) See No. 118 below.
KUYIBĀL, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up near the mosque close to the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
The extant details of the date of this badly damaged record are Śaka 967,
Pā • • • • •, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti. The intended date might be A.D. 1045, December 24.

It seems to record a gift to a basadi at Kuyyabāḷa, by certain gāvuṇḍas It
mentions Guṇa[bhadra] Siddhāntidēva and his female disciple Mōnimati kanti. The
details are all lost.

inscription.head:
No. 33.
(B.K. No. 56 of 1935-36.)
SAUNDATTI, PARASAGAD TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab fixed in the left wall of the Aṅkēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 970 corresponding to A.D. 1048-49 A.D.,
all other details of date, etc., being lost. It falls in the reign-period of Sōmēs11vara I
who is perhaps the king mentioned in the beginning. It refers to a feudatory.

l.note: This line is engraved on the top of the slab in smaller characters.

inscription.head:
No. 34.
(B.K. No. 73 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up near the temple of Sōmēśvara.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 975. Vijaya, Pushya, Puṇṇame, Sunday, Saṁkrānti. The
details are irregular. The full moon day occurred on Tuesday, December 28, A.D.
1053, and the Saṁkrānti (i.e. Makara Saṁkrānti) occurred on Friday, December 24.

The inscription records a gift of toll-income to god Sōmēśvara of Kalkere, for
offerings and burning oil-lamp, by the several officers of Kuppuṭūraṭhāṇe and the
Tiḷivaḷḷiya ṭhāṇe at the command of Mahāmātya Sōmanāthayya, the perggaḍe of
Vaḍḍarāvuḷa Mahamātya Vāvaṇayya, the perggaḍe of Perjjuṁka and Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Kīrtivarmmadēva.

l.ka: śrīmat vaḍḍarāvuḷada perggaḍe sōmanāthayyaṁgaḷa besade kuppuṭūraṭṭhāṇeya-
hitaṁ śrīmanmahāmaṇḍaḷēśvaraṁ kīrttivarmmadēvara besade tiḷivaḷḷiya ṭṭhāṇeya-

inscription.head:
No. 35.
(B.K. No. 69 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the temple of Basavaṇṇa outside the village.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 977, Manmatha Pushya śu. 5, Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa
Saṁkrānti. The Saṁkrānti occurred on Monday, 25th December, A.D. 1055, when the
tithi was śu. 4 and not śu. 5.

The inscription records gift of lands to God Sōmēśvara, for offerings, oil-lamp,
etc., by Barmma-gāvuṇḍa when Chālukya-Ka[li]-Gaṅga-Permānaḍi-Vikramādityadēva
bearing the epithets Konguḷivarmma-Dharmma-mahārājādhirāja, Kōḷāḷapuravarēśvara
and Nandagirinātha was governing the Gaṅgavāḍi-96000, Mahāmātya Nāraṇayya was
perggaḍe of Banavāsi-12000, Mallarasa was nāḻgāvuṇḍa of Bāsavūra-140 and
Barmmayya, son of Sōvagāvuṇḍa was the ūrggāvuṇḍa of Beṅgēri. The gift was
entrusted to Pasupatidēva.

inscription.head:
No. 36.
(B.K. No. 10 of 1936-37.)
BAḶAGĀNŪR, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the left outer wall of the Chanḍēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
The available details of date of this damaged record are Śaka 97[8], Durmukhi-
Bhādrapada, Sunday, corresponding to A.D. 1056-57.

It refers to a certain Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara (name lost) and seems to record
a gift of land, etc., by certain individuals. The record is badly worn out and the
details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 37.
(B.K. No. 17 of 1937-38.)
CHAḌCHAṆ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Two faces of a rectangular stone, standing in front of the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated Śaka [9]79, [Hēma] lambi, Chaitra, puṇṇame,
Sunday, lunar eclipse and Mēsha-saṁkrānti corresponding to A.D. 1057, March 23.

Records a gift of land and money by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Bijjarasa, described as
‘Lord of Kālañjarapura’, to a temple (name lost). Also records a gift of land
probably to the same temple by Dharmaśiva-Bhaṭāra.

inscription.head:
No. 38.
(B.K. No. 91 of 1938-39.)
KUYIBĀḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab standing close to the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 980, Vilambi, Pushya, śu • • Sunday-
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti. The saṁkrānti occurred in A.D. 1058, December 24, when
the week-day was Thursday and the tithi was śu. 7.

The inscription records a gift of land to god Jōgēśvara (?) by Mahāsāmanta
Indrakēsiyarasa who was governing over Purigeṟe-300 division as Rāshṭrakūṭaka,
and others. It mentions Tantrapāḷa Chāvuṇḍamayya who was also perhaps a donor.
The gift was made over to Lōkābharaṇa-paṇḍita.

The second part of the record registers a gift of land to the same god at
Kuyillabāḷa, by certain gāvuṇḍas whose names are lost. Lōkābharaṇa-paṇḍita was
the recipient of this gift. This is dated in the month of Śrāvaṇa, śu. 11, Monday,
other details not being given. The date cannot be verified.

inscription.head:
No. 39.
(B.K. No. 23 of 1936-37.)
DĒVŪR, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Slab built into the ceiling at the main entrance into the
Rāmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 986, Krōdhi, Pushya, śu. 13, Friday, Uttarāyaṇa-
saṁkrānti corresponding regularly to A.D. 1064, December 24.

The record registers a gift of 60 mattars of land to god Mārasiṁgēśvara of Dēva-
pura, for worship and for the benefit of ascetics and students, by Mahāsāmanta (Vā)-
rarasa who obtained the gift-land from Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Trailōkyamalla-Noḷamba-
pallavapermānaḍi-Jayasiṅgadēva who was governing at Taldavāḍi-nāḍu. The gift was
entrusted to Jñānārāśi-paṇḍita of pāśupata school.

l.ka: yasya yadā bhūmi tasya tasya tadā phalaṁ ||
svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasundharā ṣaṣṭirvva-

inscription.head:
No. 40.
(B.K. No. 43 of 1937-38.)
MĀRASANHAḶḶI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the Patil's house near Lakshmī temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 988, Parābhava, Pushya, śu. 5, Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-
saṁkrānti corresponding regularly to A.D. 1066, December 24.

It registers the gift of the village Mārasiṁganapaḷḷi for feeding the ascetics and
students of the maṭha attached to Uttarēśvara temple of Eḷemēla and also for their
medicines and charity house, by the prince Trailōkyamalla-Noḷamba-Pallava-Permāḍi-
Jayasiṁhadēva, who was governing Tarddavāḍi-1000. The gift was entrusted to
Sōmēśvara-paṇḍita of Pāśupata school.

l.ka: yasya yasya yadā bhūmistasya tasya tadā phaḷaṁ [||*]
svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harēta vasundharāṁ

inscription.head:
No. 41.
(B.K. No. 129 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i:
Slab set up to the left of the Vīrabhadradēva shrine in the compound of
the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 990, Kīlaka, Chaitra, śu. 13, the other details being
lost. It corresponds to A.D. 1068, March 19, when the week-day was Wednesday.

The first 17 lines of the record are published in Epigraphia Indica, Vol. XVI,
pp. 353 ff. It refers to Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kīrtivarmadēva governing Banavāsi 12,000.
The subsequent portion, which is badly, damaged, seems to record a gift to
Kalla-dēgula.

l.ka: va(ṁ)maṇṇana baṁṭaṁ vairiniḥkaṁṭaṁ tailana siṁghaṁ sāhasōttuṁgaṁ
990 neya kīlaka samvatsarada caitra śuddha trayōdaśi

• • • • • na viṣaṁ viṣa • • • dēvaśvaṁ viṣamucyatē viṣa-

inscription.head:
No. 42.
(B.K. No. 70 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the temple of Basavaṇṇa outside the village.

inscription.p:
This damaged record engraved below No. 35 refers to a subordinate of
Trailōkyamalla, whose name is lost, who was governing the Perjjuṅka of a division
probably Banavāsi 12,000. It seems to record a gift for oil-lamp and incense to
a God. The details are lost.

l.note: mendu is written below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 43.
(B.K. No. 31 of 1936-37.)
JĀLVĀḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the Śaṅkaraliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record belongs to the reign of Trailōkyamalla and is assigned
to Sōmēśvara I on palaeographic grounds. It seems to register a gift of land. The
date and other details are all lost.

inscription.head:
No. 44.
(B.K. No. 62 of 1937-38.)
RŪGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone leaning against the front wall of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 991, Kīlaka, Amāvāsyā, Sunday,
Dakshiṇāyana [Saṁkrānti]. This date is irregular. The cyclic year corresponding to
the given Śaka year was Saumya and not Kīlaka. The intended date seems to be
A.D. 1069, June 21 when the tithi was amāvāsyā and the week-day was Sunday. The
Saṁkrānti, however, occurred three days later.

The inscription records the gift of certain money incomes made by Mādhavayya
Nāyaka with the permission of the king for the benefit of the temple of Nakharēśvara
at Rūvige when Pāyyarasa was governing Tardavāḍi division.

The inscription also refers to another gift of oil made by the Fifty Telliga
tenants for a lamp in the temple of Kēdārēśvara of the place (i.e., Rūvige) in the
year Śaka 967, Pārthiva, Pushya śu. 5, Sunday Uttarāyaṇa-Saṁkrānti. This date
corresponds to A.D. 1045, December 15, Sunday, f.d.t. 64. There was no Saṁkrānti
on this date. It appears that this gift was revived as in the case of No. 45 below.

edition.p: The top portion is completely effaced.

l.ka: manaḷida dōsā sārguṁ || sakavarṣa 9[6]7 neya pārtthivasaṁ-
ḷasa 1 yeṇṇe yellā • kālakkaṁ naḍevaṁtāgi biṭṭaru |

inscription.head:
No. 45.
(B.K. Nos. 65 and 65A of 1937-38.)
RŪGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing close to the front wall of the Hanūmān temple.

inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 99[3], Virōdhakrit, Ashādha, ba. 11, Sunday, Dakshiṇāyana-
saṁkranti corresponding to A.D. 1071, June 26.

It records a gift of income from certain taxes for worship, offerings and
burning perpetual lamp before God Nagarēśvaradēva by Perggaḍe Kannapayya,
Sēnabōva Māḷapayya-nāyaka and the Karaṇa when Mahāmanḍalēśvara Pāyiyarasa
was governing Tardavāḍi-1000 division.

It contains the renewal of the gift of oil to God Kēdāradēva by Telliga Gōṁ-
kayya and others made in the year Śaka 967, Pārthiva, Pushya, śu. 7, Sunday, Uttarā-
yaṇa-saṁkrānti. The details of this date are irregular. The tithi corresponded to
A.D. 1045, December 18, Wednesday and the saṁkrānti occurred on December 23. See
No. 44 above.

edition.p: Nine lines in the beginning are worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 46.
(B.K. No. 110 of 1938-39.)
GUḌIGĒRI, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the store-room in the Jaina basadi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 994, Paushya, śu. 11, Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-Saṁkrānti
corresponding regularly to A.D. 1072, December 24.

The inscription records the revival of the endowment of the village Guḍigeṛe,
formerly made by Kuṁkumamahādēvi, the younger sister of the Chālukya king
Vijayāditya, to Ānesejjeya-basadī which was caused to be constructed by her a
Puligere. The gift is stated to have been made by queen Kaṁchalamahādēvi who
was governing from the headquarters at Muḷugunda and others in the presence of the
king, with the consent of Bhāskara-bhaṭṭōpādhyāya on the occasion of his victorious
return from the northern expedition. It is also stated that Mahāpradhāna Daṇḍa-
nāyaka Rudrabhaṭṭōpadhyāya was administering Guḍigeṛe and Daṇḍanāyaka Īśvara-
bhaṭṭa was in charge of all the departments. The donee was Nandi-paṇḍitadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 47.
(B.K. No. 29 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVARA, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing in front of the Bīradēva temple at Hire Baṇa (suburb).

inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 996, Ānanda, Chaitra, śu. Prathamanandīśvara-ashṭami,
Sunday, corresponding to A.D. 1074, March 9, Sunday, f.d.t. ·20.

Records that Mahāsāmanta Jayakēśiyarasa, son of Indrakēśiyarasa of Maṇala
family, who was the Rashṭrakūṭaka of Purigeṟe-300 division, visited Permāḍi Basadi
at Purigeṟe, made it into a pura at the request of Tribhuvanachaṁdrapaṇḍita,
disciple of Gaṇḍavimukta—Bhaṭṭāraka of Mūla-samgha and Baḷātkāra-gaṇa
and remitted certain tax-incomes to the same. Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Lakshmarasa was
then governing the divisions of Purigere-300 and Beḷvola-300.

inscription.head:
No. 48.
(B.K. No. 97 of 1938-39.)
SAMSI, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing in front of the Siddhēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
The record is badly damaged and worn out. It is dated Śaka 995, Ānanda,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti, the other details being lost. It corresponds to A.D. 1074
December 24, the Śaka year being 996 and not 995.

The inscription seems to register a gift of oil and income from certain taxes.
It mentions the Four Hundred Mahājanas and refers to Telligas and Bāḻagaṇa.

l.ka: raṁ saluttamire || svasti sa • • • 9[9]5 neyānanda samvatsaradutta-

inscription.head:
No. 49.
(B.K. No. 75 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up near the temple of Sōmēśvara.

inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 998, Rākshasa, Pushya, Amāvāsya, Sunday, Hasta
nakshatra, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti. The details of this date are irregular. The Śaka
year 998 corresponded to the Cyclic year Anala when the said saṁkrānti occurred
on Saturday, the tithi being ba. 11 of the month Pushya and the nakshatra
Viśākhā. In the cyclic year Rākshasa the saṁkrānti occurred on Friday the tithi
being śu. 15 and the nakshatra Punarvasu. The intended date might be 1076 A.D.,
December 24, Saturday.

The inscription registers the gift of the village Pullavaḷa situated to the south
of the river Varadā in Bāsavura-140, a sub-division of Banavāsi-12,000, to God
Svayaṁbhū Sōmēśvara of Kalkeṟe, for worship and offerings to the deity and for
feeding the ascetics, etc., by Bhuvanaikamalla-Vīra-Noḷamba who was governing
Noḷṁbavāḍi, Pānuṁgallu-nāḍu and Banavāsidēśa. The gift was made, in conjunction
with his wife Māḷaladēvi, at the request of Perggaḍe Divākara and was entrusted to
Dēvasiṅga Jīya. The king was at that time camping at Baṅkāpura. The record
contains an eulogy of the donor and his wife.

inscription.head:
No. 50.
(B.K. No. 61 of 1935-36.)
SAUNDATTI, PARASGAD TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
The bottom of the stone is broken and lost. Refers to Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Kārttavīryarasa of Lattalūrapura and contains the genealogical account of his family.
Mentions Arhaṇandi-munīndra and Kaṇḍūr Gaṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 51.
(B.K. No. 67 of 1936-37.)
KUḶĒKUMAṬGI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the left wall of Chandraśēkharadēva temple.

inscription.p:
The record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 2, Piṁgala, Pushya, śu. 7, Sunday,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti, corresponding to A.D. 1077, December 24. The tithi, however,
was śu. 2 and not śu. 7.

The inscription records gifts of land for the benefit of the temple of Chaṇḍēśvara
at Kumbiṭṭage, by Mahāsāmanta Siṁgarasa of Siyaḷāra family and Udāyadityarasa.
It also records gifts of land, garden and oil to the same temple and also to the
temple of Vināyaka and Ādityadēva by Kētaladēvi, queen of Tribhuvanamalladēva.
The latter is said to have been ruling from his camping place at Ponuguppe.

l.ka: ṟaṭana kōlalu biṭṭa keyi mattaroḷage dēvaraṁ trikā-
ḷaṁ pūjisuva māṇigaḷge matta 2 paṟekāṟarggaṁ hāḍuva-
māḍi ·oṟaṭana kōlalu biṭṭa keyi matta 14 yavaṟoḷage
nūra baṭṭeyiṁ tenkaṇa gaddemaṁ ·ā gaddeyiṁ paḍu-
jegaḷuṁ mūliganuṁ svāna gārddapa cāṇḍāḷaru gaṁgātīrado-
sētunripāṇāṁ kāḷē kāḷē pāḷanī[yaḥ] bhavadbhiḥ sarbbānētā-
bhāginaṁ pārttiva bhūyō bhūyō yūcatē rāmabhadra |
svadattāṁ paṭhadattāṁ vā yō harēti vasundharāṁ ṣaṣṭirvvarṣa-
sahasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi || śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 52.
(B.K. No. 6 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒS11VAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This record, written in Sanskrit and Kannaḍa languages, is dated Śaka 969
(in words), Chālukya-Vikrama year 2, Piṅgala, Āshāḍha śu. 12, Wednesday corresponding
regularly to A.D. 1077 July 5. There is a second date in the record the details of
which are Śaka 1000, Kālayukta, Madhumāsa (Chaitra) Amāvāsya, Sunday. This
corresponds to A.D. 1078, April 14. Saturday, on which day the tithi ended at ·90.

This inscription records the death of Śrīnandi-Paṇḍitadēva of Sūrastra-gaṇa
by the rite of Sallēkhanā observed for one month at Anesejjeya-basadi at Purikara-
nagara. It also records the death on the second date of Bhāskaranandi-Paṇḍita
who is said to have been the elder brother of the former.

inscription.head:
No. 53.
(B.K. No. 4 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the backyard of the Taluk office.

inscription.p:
The record is dated the cyclic year Durmati, Pushya, ba. Bhṛigu-shashṭhi, Uttarā-
yaṇa-Saṁkrānti corresponding to A.D. 1081, December 24.

The inscription, which is incomplete, registers a grant made by Dōṇa, the
younger brother of Mahāsāmantādhipati Eṟemayya who was administering the
Purigeṟe-300, for the Jaina cult in Purikara. The gift was entrusted to Narēndrasēna
(II), of the Sēna-gaṇa in the Mūla-saṅgha, the disciple of Nayasēna Sūri who in his
turn was the disciple of Narēndrasēna (I). It is stated that at the time of the record
the king's younger brother Yuvarāja Jayasiṁha (III) was governing the two
Six-Hundreds (viz. Beḷvola-300 and Purigeṟe-300), Banavāsi-12,000, Sāntaḷige-1000
and Kandūr-1,000.
Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XVI, pp. 58 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 54.
(B.K. No. 80 of 1936-37.)
SINDAGI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken piece of a slab built into the front wall of the Raṅgamaṭha.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 6, Durmati, the other details being
lost. It corresponds to A.D. 1081-82. The portion containing the details of the gift
etc., is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 55.
(B.K. No. 12 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the backyard of the Taluk office.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 6, Dundubhi, Vaiśākha, śu. 5,
Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1082, April 6. The weekday, however, was
Wednesday.

The inscription registers a gift of land made by the Five-Hundred members
headed by Śānti-seṭṭi of Purigere, after purchasing it from the Brāhmaṇas with
the money which had accumulated with interest, granted, formerly to Taḻara
Nāgavarman, a subordinate of Vāvaṇa-nṛipati, to Lakkaṇa-seṭṭi, father of Śānti-seṭṭi,
for the benefit of drawing water by picotta (ēta) from the well called Rāṭaṇada-bāvi
when Trailōkyamalla Vīra-Noḷamba-Pallava-Permanaḍi-Jayasiṁhadēva was governing
Purigeṟe-300, Beḷvola-300, Banavase-12000 and Kandūr-1000. It refers to one Jayakēsi
of Sagara family as the subordinate of Jayasiṁha.

inscription.head:
No. 56.
(B.K. No. 68 of 1936-37.)
KUḶĒKUMAṬGI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the right wall of Chandraśēkhara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 7, Dundubhi, Āshāḍha, amāvāsye
Tuesday, corresponding regularly to A.D. 1082, June 28.

The bottom portion of the inscription is chiselled out. It mentions Mahā-
sāmanta Siṁgarasa of Siyaḷāra family, Udayāditya and Perggaḍe Chaṇḍimayya who
was the Prabhu of Kumbiṭṭage.

l.ka: maṁgaḷavāradaṁdu kuṁbiṭṭageya prabhu perggaḍe caṁḍimayyana kayya

inscription.head:
No. 57.
(B.K. No. 38 of 1938-39.)
HADLI, RAMDURG TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing in front of the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 9, Raktākshi, Chaitra, śu. 1, Monday,
corresponding to A.D. 1084, March 10, the weekday being Sunday.

The inscription records a gift of land to god Gavaṟēśvaradēva by the sage
Jñānaśakti-paṇḍita after purchasing it from the 420 Brāhmaṇas of the agrahāra
Paldala. The record was composed by Karpparasa who was the son-in-law
of Mahāsandhivigrahi, Daṇḍanāyaka Kāḷidāsa-Bhaṭṭa and the son of Chandra and
Chāmāṁbike. The engraver was Kāvōja.

inscription.head:
No. 58.
(B.K. No. 12 of 1936-37.)
BEKKINAḶ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone built into the raised platform of the dargā outside the village.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 10, Krōdhana, Paushya, śu. 5, Monday,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkramaṇa corresponding to A.D. 1085, December 24. The weekday was,
however, Wednesday.

The inscription, which consists of two fragments, seems to record the renewal of
an earlier gift for a water-shed, students, etc., which had lapsed, by Mahā-
maṇḍaḷēśvara Kaliyammarasa, with the consent of the king who is stated to have
been ruling from his capital Kalyāṇa.

l.ka: śvaraṁ kaliyammarasar cāḷukyavikramavarṣada 10 neya krōdhana saṁ • •
rada pauśya suddha paṁcami sōmavāra ·uttarāyaṇa saṁkramaṇadaṁdu rāja •
• • • • • || ślōka || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasunddharā śaṣṭhi • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 59.
(B.K. No. 83 of 1936-37.)
TAVARKHĒḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing in front of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 10, Krōdhana, Phālguṇa, śu. 1, Sunday.
The tithi corresponds to A.D. 1086, February 17, the weekday being Tuesday.

The inscription records a gift of 100 mattars of land at Eḷamēla by Manḍalika
Nāyimarasa. The record falls in the reign-period of Vikramāditya VI.

inscription.head:
No. 60.
(B.K. No. 76 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up near the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 13, Vibhava, Paushya, ba. 1, Sunday,
lunar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1088, December 31. The eclipse occurred on
the previous day.

The inscription, which is damaged, records the renewal of a gift of income from
certain taxes, for the worship and offerings in a temple (name lost), by Mahāmātya
Perggaḍe Chaṅgadēvayya and others when Daṇḍanāyaka Śrīdharayya and Daṇḍanā-
yaka Āsapayya were administering Vaḍḍarāvuḷa and other taxes.

inscription.head:
No. 61.
(B.K. No. 56 of 1936-37.)
KANNOḶḶI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the right wall of Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 19, Bhāva, Chaitra, śu. 1, Sunday,
corresponding to A.D. 1094, March 19, Sunday, f.d.t. ·14.

This badly damaged inscription seems to register a gift of land, garden, and oil-
mills by Paṭṭamahādēvī Maiḷalamahādēvi, queen of Vikramāditya. She seems to have
been administering Kannavaḷḷi while the king was ruling from his capital Kalyāṇa.

l.ka: śraya śrī prithvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja paramēśvara paramabhaṭṭārakaṁ sa-
ya kōlalu biṭṭa mattaru • • • • • • • • • • mattaru | yippatteraḍu | ·a-
ntu sama • • • • • sarvvanamasya mattaru 100 pūrvvada tōṁṭaveraḍu
svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō ha • • • • • viṣṭhāyāṁ jāyatē krimi ||

inscription.head:
No. 62.
(B.K. No. 59 of 1935-36.)
SAUNDATTI, PARASAGAD TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the Basti-guḍi.

inscription.p:
The record is dated Vīra-Vikrama, i.e., Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 21, Dhātu, Pushya
ba. 13, Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti. The details given are irregular. The tithi
corresponds to January 14, Wednesday, A.D. 1097. But in the next year, i.e. A.D.
1098, the tithi corresponds to January 3, when the weekday was Sunday. In neither
case did the said Saṁkrānti occur. The latter date, viz., A.D. 1098, January 3 might
be the intended date.

The inscription records a gift, by the king, of land in Kāreyabāgu village to the
temple of Jinēndra built by Kāḷasēna of the Raṭṭa family. It further records another
gift of land by the Raṭṭa chief Sēna II to the same temple. It also refers to a gift of
land made by the Raṭṭa chief Kannakaira II to his guru Kanakaprabhasiddhāntadēva.
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kārtavīrya is mentioned as the feudatory of the king.
Published in JBBRAS, Vol. X, pp. 195-98.

inscription.head:
No. 63.
(B.K. No. 90 of 1936-37.)
YARGAL (BHUJRUKH), SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of Rāmaliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the cyclic year Bahudhānya, Āśvayuja, puṇṇame, Monday,
falling in the reign of Tribhuvanamalla and regularly corresponding to A.D. 1098,
September 13.

The inscription is badly damaged and effaced. It seems to register a gift to
god Svayaṁbhū-Rāmēs11vara, and others. It refers to the trade-guild of the 500
svāmis of Ayyāvoḷe.

l.ka: tē dvāpara kaliyuga modalāge brahmavipṇumahēśvarama[ṭhaṁ] nelaṁ pa[suṁbe]-

inscription.head:
No. 64.
(B.K. No. 72 of 1936-37.)
MALGHAṆ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab leaning against the right wall inside the Basavaṇṇa temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 25, Vikrama, Pushya, ba. 6, Monday,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti corresponding to A.D. 1100, December 24.

It records a gift of land and house-site by Antaḥpura-verggaḍe and Daṇḍa-
nāyaka Vāmadēvayya for worship and offerings to god Ādityadēva, Kēśavadēva and
Vāmēśvaradēva which were installed by him at Mallagāṇa which was situated in
Kummasi-30 of Tarddavāḍi-1000 division and which was a sarvvanamasya-agrahāra
of Purāṇada Divākarabhaṭṭōpādhyāya. The king is stated to have been camping
on the Bhīmarathi while proceeding to the west against Bhōja.

vanamalladēvaru bhōjana mēle paścimadiśāvarakkettidalli bhīmaratiya tīradappaya-

inscription.head:
No. 65.
(B.K. No. 73 of 1936-37.)
MALGHAṆ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Same slab as in No. 64.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 25, Māgha, śu. 3, Friday, the name
of the year being lost. If the cyclic year is Vikrama as in No. 64, the details of the
date would correspond to A.D. 1101, January 4.

This slightly worn out record seems to register a gift of garden and land to god
Traipurushadēva made over to Divākara-bhaṭṭōpādhyāya by Vāmadē[vayya] at the
time of the construction of a well.

inscription.head:
No. 66.
(B.K. No. 24 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 27, Chitrabhānu. Āshādha, śu. 5,
Sunday, corresponding to A.D. 1102, June 22.

The inscription registers a gift of income from taxes to god Muddēśvara in the
temple of Svayaṁbhu-Sōmēśvara at Puligere by Daṇḍanāyaka, Achchupaṁnāyadadhi-
shṭhāyaka Mādhava-bhaṭṭa who was a subordinate of Mahāsāmantādhipati, Mane-
verggaḍe Bhīvaṇayya who was administering the achchupannāya tax of Palasige-12,000.
The gift was entrusted to Mahēndrasōma-paṇḍita, the āchārya of the temple.

p.i: Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XVI, pp. 31 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 67.
(B.K. No. 59 of 1936-37.)
KOṆḌGŪḶI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab fixed into the left outer wall of Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 32, Sarvajit, Chaitra, Amāvāsyā,
Monday, solar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1107, March 25, f.d.t., ·36. But there was
no eclipse on that day.

This badly damaged inscription registers a gift of land to the temple of Jyōtīś-
vara for worship, offerings and repairs and for the maintenance of the priest of the
temple. It mentions the Sarvanamasya-agrahāra Koṇḍaguḷi.

l.ka: ttu dēvarige biṭṭa [sīme] sarvvamānyavāgi bōvaṁge biṭṭa ma-

inscription.head:
No. 68.
(B.K. No. 62 of 1936-37.)
KOṆḌGŪḶI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: On the face of a broken pillar set up on the bund of the well outside the village.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 32, Sarvajit, Ās11vayuja, Amāvāsyā
solar eclipse, Wednesday, corresponding to A.D. 1107, October 16, Wednesday, f.d.t.
·98. No eclipse, however, occurred on that day.

The inscription is badly damaged and seems to record a gift of toll-incomes, for
the benefit of god Sōmanātha, by Suṅkaverggaḍe Mādhava-Sēnādhipati, Heggaḍe
Mādeyanāyaka and others.

inscription.head:
No. 69.
(B.K. No. 61 of 1936-37.)
KOṆḌGŪḶI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: First face of a broken pillar set up on the bund of the well outside the village.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 33, Sarvajit,
Mārggasira, Āmāvāsyā, Monday, solar eclipse, regularly corresponding to A.D. 1107,
December 16.

This inscription is in two fragments and registers a gift of land, measured by
the Bandhuchintāmaṇi pole, by Mahāpradhāna, Daṇḍanāyaka Kēsirājarasa of the
Vāji-kuḷa to the temple of Sōmēśvara constructed by him at Koṇḍaguḷi.

inscription.head:
No. 70.
(B.K. No. 104 of 1938-39.)
YELIVĀḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into a wall of Kalamēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 33, Sarvadhāri, Āshāḍha, Amāvāsyā,
Wednesday, solar eclipse. The solar eclipse occurred on Jyēshṭha amāvāsyā accord-
ing to which the corresponding date would be A.D. 1108, June 10, Wednesday,
f.d.t. ·15.

This damaged inscription seems to record a gift of three stalls to a deity (name
lost) by Kēsiyaṇṇa in the presence of the Thousand Mahājanas of Kuṁduṁguḷa.

inscription.head:
No. 71.
(B.K. No. 1 of 1937-38.)
AGARKHĒḌ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the compound of Bhairappa temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 34, Virōdhin, Mārggaśira, Puṇṇame,
Monday, lunar eclipse, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti. The details are irregular. The saṁkrānti
occurred on Saturday, December 25, A.D. 1109, when the tithi was Pushya,
śu. 2. The eclipse occurred on November 9, Tuesday when the tithi was Kārttika
puṇṇame. The intended date might be A.D. 1109, November 8, Monday, f.d.t., ·53.

The inscription records a remission of taxes on the land (aruvaṇa) of the temple
of Svayaṁbhudēva at Khēḍa in the One Thousand division, by Lakshmīdhara-
Bhaṭṭōpādhyāya, who was the preceptor of Yuvarāja Mallikārjunadēva, the eldest son
of the king, to the same temple. The latter was then governing over the Tarddavāḍi-
1000 division. It further registers gifts of money income, taxes, etc., to the same
temple by Mahāpradhāna, Permāḍiyarasa, Mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Gōvaṇarasa of the
Siḷāra family whose genealogy also is given and by the Five Hundred Svāmis of
Ayyāvoḷe and the Eight nāḍus.

l.note: The letter jā is engraved below the line.
The letter va is engraved below the line.

[sa]niṁ baḷiya maruḷuviṭṭana vaṁśajaṁ piriya gōvaṇanagrajaṁ mahāmaṇḍaḷēśva-
yayyaṁ maṇḍaḷēsvaraṁ [gōṣṭa]da kavilāsana magaṁ mahāmaṇḍaḷēsvaraṁ ḍākarasa-
nōdaṁ sanivārasidhdhi manneyavallabhaṁ [dha]negana siṁga[ṁ] śrīmadyuvarāja ma-

inscription.head:
No. 72.
(B.K. No. 93 of 1936-37.)
BIJAPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab (No. 48) in the Archaeological Museum.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 36, Khara, Vais11ākha, Puṇṇami,
Monday, lunar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1111, April 24. Monday, f.d.t. ·62. The
eclipse occurred on the next day.

The inscription records a gift of land and house-site as Bhaṭṭavṛitti for reading
the Purāṇa made by Perggaḍe Kīrttirāja when Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kumāra Jaya-
karṇadēva was governing the mahāgrahāra Sindige. The king is stated to be ruling
from Jayantīpura.

l.ka: yyiṁ baḍaga biṭṭa piriya kōla matta [5] maneya nivēśana 1

kālē kālē pālanīyō bhavadbhiḥ sarbbānētā[n bhā]vinaḥ pārtthivēndrān
bhūyō bhūyō yācatē rāmabhadra ||

svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēta vasundharā ṣaṣṭirvvarṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭhāyāṁ
jāyatē krimi ||

inscription.head:
No. 73.
(B.K. No. 71 of 1937-38.)
SHIRKANHAḶḶI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up near the Jaṭṭiṅgarāya temple.

inscription.p:
This record which is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 37, Khara, Pushya, Puṇṇame,
Friday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti, stops abruptly after giving the date. The details of the
date are irregular. The given tithi corresponds to A.D. 1111, December 17, Sunday,
whereas the saṁkrānti occurred on Monday, December 25, the tithi being ba. 8.
The record falls in the reign-period of Vikramāditya VI.

inscription.head:
No. 74.
(B.K. No. 2 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 37, Nandana, Kārttika, Puṇṇame,
Monday, lunar eclipse. The details correspond to A.D. 1112, November 4, Monday,
f.d.t. ·70. But there was no eclipse on that day.

The inscription records a gift, by Daṇḍanāyaka Anantamayya, for the mainte-
nance of the quarter for dancing girls (sūḷe-gēri) constructed by him, attached to the
temple of Sōmēśvaradēva at Purigere when his brother-in-law Śrīpatiyarasa was
governing the Two-600 division, viz., Beḷvola-300 and Purigere-300. The gift-land
was purchased from the Mahājanas of the nagara (i.e. Purigere) and the Gāvuṇḍas.

inscription.head:
No. 75.
(B.K. No. 49 of 1936-37.)
KALKĒRI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in a ruined temple in Survey No. 6.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the cyclic year Hēmaḷambi, Vaiśākha, śu. 5. Sunday, in the
reign of the king. This corresponds to A.D. 1117, April 8, Sunday, f.d.t. ·07.

The inscription states that Amṛita-Paṭṭavarddhana-Vāsudēva-Ghaḷisāsa and
his wife Chāmiyakka consecrated the image of Kēśavadēva with the consent of the
Mahājanas of Kalkere which had been made into a sarvvanamasya-agrahāra by
Mahāpradhāna, Daṇḍanāyaka Kāḷimarasa and endowed a gift of land to the same.

l.ka: © śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī
hi daṇḍanā[ya*]ka kāḷimarasaru || biṭṭa sarvvanamasyadagrahāra kalkeṟeya ·a-
ḷige cāmiyakkanuṁ śrī kēśavadēvara pratiṣṭheyaṁ mā-
ḍi biṭṭa bhūmi samasta baḷi [sahi]taṁ biṭṭa vritti ·eraḍu ·i dha-

inscription.head:
No. 76.
(B.K. No. 64 of 1935-36.)
BĀḶAMBIḌ, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the Rāmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 41, Hēmaḷaṁbi, Phāḷguṇa, s11u, 5,
Sunday, corresponding to A.D. 1118, February 27, Sunday, f.d.t. ·91.

The inscription records a gift of land and garden for the benefit of the temple
of Grāmēśvara by Barmmagāvuṇḍa of Baḷḷareyabīḍu, who was the subordinate of
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Tailahadēva of the Kadaṁba family governing Banavāsi-12000
and Pānuṁgal—500 and by the other prajes of the place. The gift was entrusted
to Amṛitarās11i-paṇḍita, the āchārya of the temple. It further records other gifts of
oil and income from taxes to the same temple by the 1000 taṁbuligas and others.

inscription.head:
No. 77.
(B.K. No. 2 of 1937-38.)
AGARKHĒḌ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the compound of Bhairappa temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 43, Viḷambi, Bhādrapada, śu. 6,
Va[ḍḍavāra*] corresponding to A.D. 1118, August 24, Saturday.

The inscription, which is engraved in continuation of No. 71 above, registers a
gift of taxes by the tax-officer Sāmiseṭṭi to the temple of Svayaṁbhudēva for
offerings and perpetual lamp. It also records other gifts to the same temple.

inscription.head:
No. 78.
(B.K. No. 11 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 43, Viḷambi, Pushya, śu. 11,
Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkramaṇa corresponding to A.D. 1118, December 25. The week-
day was, however, Wednesday.

The inscription records a gift of income from certain taxes to the temple of
Rāmēśvaradēva of Rājadhāni Puligere by Sādaverggaḍe Sōvarasa at the instance of
Daṇḍanāyaka • • charasa who was governing Puligere-300. The gift was
entrusted to Sāmavēdi-Paṇḍita, the āchārya of the temple. It further records
other gifts by the aivattokkalu and other individuals for the same temple.

l.ka: vikramavarṣada 43 neya vi[ḷaṁbi] saṁvatsarada puśya su 11
jisutaṁ dhare baṇnise negardda saccaritaṁ śrīsāmavēdipaṇḍi-
• • • kālaṁ kacci sarvvanamaśyavāgi biṭṭaru || mattaṁ pu-
ka muruḍara kāviseṭṭi mādiseṭṭi mukhyavāgi samasta [ta]-
ḷadavaruṁ | gavaṟe rājaseṭṭi ·ōleya caṭṭiseṭṭi cuṁga[ra kā]-
ḷiseṭṭi cuṁgara rēviseṭṭi gandhiga rēviseṭṭi mukhyavāgi mu[ṁ]mu-
manaḷida [paṁca]mahāpātakanakku || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō ha[rē]-
ti vasundharā śaṣṭhirvvarṣasahaśrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē kṛmi[ḥ ||]

inscription.head:
No. 79.
(B.K. No. 65 of 1936-37.)
KŌRVAR, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab built into the wall at Mēlina-maṭha.

inscription.p:
The extant portion of this fragmentary record gives the details of the date as
[Vi]kāri, Vaiśākha, Amāvāsye, [Tuesday]. These details are irregular. For the cyclic
year falling in the reign of Tribhuvanamalla, the tithi corresponds to A.D. 1119,
May 11, Sunday.

The inscription seems to record a gift of land and garden to a deity (name lost)
made after purchase.

inscription.head:
No. 80.
(B.K. No. 94 of 1936-37.)
BIJAPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab (No. 49) in the Archaeological Museum.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 46, Śārvvari, Āśvayuja, Amāvāsyā,
Monday, solar eclipse corresponding to A.D. 1120, October 24, the weekday being
Sunday.

The inscription records a gift of land to the temple of god Mūlasthāna, built by
Daṇḍanāyaka Sōmarasa, the prabhu of Sindige, by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kumāra Jaya-
karṇadēva and Manneya Siṅgarasa.

inscription.head:
No. 81.
(B.K. No. 15 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 4[7], Plava, Phālguṇa,
Amāvāsyā, Sunday, Saṁkramaṇa, solar eclipse. It corresponds to A.D. 1122, March 10.
The weekday was, however, Friday and no Saṁkramaṇa occurred on that day.

The inscription records a gift of land to god Sōmēśvara by Daṇḍanāyaka
Nāgavarmmayya after purchasing it from the 120 mahājanas of Puligere. One
sēnāpati Sōvidēva is mentioned.

l.ka:
ccāḷukya vikramakālada 4[7] neya plava saṁvatsarada phālguṇadamāvāsye
ādivāra saṁkrama-

bhūmiya pramā-

inscription.head:
No. 82.
(B.K. No. 71 of 1938-39.)
BEṬADŪR, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in front of Kalamēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated Śaka 1045, Subhakṛit, • • • • • Amāvāsye, Friday, solar
eclipse, the name of the month being lost. The date is irregular. In the cyclic year
Śubhakṛit which, however, corresponds to Śaka 1044, no solar eclipse occurred
whereas in the previous year Plava, a solar eclipse occurred in the month of
Phālguṇa when the weekday was Friday. The intended date may be, therefore,
A.D. 1122, March 10, Friday.

The inscription which falls in the reign-period of Vikramāditya VI, registers a
gift of land, garden and money to god Mūlasthāna of the agrahāra Kisuvare by
several individuals whose names are specified. In the beginning it seems to refer
to [Jaya]kēśidēva of the Kadaṁba family who, it is known, was a feudatory of
Vikramāditya VI.

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 83.
(B.K. No. 27 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 48, Śōbhakṛit, Āshāḍha, Amāvāsye,
Monday, Dakshiṇāyana-saṁkramaṇa corresponding to A.D. 1123, June 25. The
Saṁkramaṇa occurred on the next day and the month was adhika Āshāḍha.

The inscription records a gift of land to Agastyapaṇḍitadēva, the disciple of
Sāmavēdipaṇḍita for feeding and maintenance of the teacher who was teaching
Kaumāra-vyākaraṇa and his students in the maṭha attached to the temple of
Rāmēśvaradēva at Puligere, by Daṇḍanāyaka Nāgavarmayya, after purchasing the
gift-land.

l.ka: kramadiṁ baṁda caḷukyarājyalategītaṁ tānaḍarppādaneṁdu manaṁgoṁḍeḷe ba-

inscription.head:
No. 84.
(B.K. No. 67 of 1935-36.)
BĀḶAMBĪḌ, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in Baladēvara Guḍi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 47, Śōbhakṛit, Paushya, śu. 5, Monday,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti, solar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1123, December 24. But
there was no solar eclipse on the preceding Āmāvāsyā.

The inscription records a gift of land, etc., for offerings and worship to god
Brahmēśvara by Bammagāvuṇḍa who had the temple constructed at Baḷḷāreya-bīḍu
which was under his administration. The gift was made over to Vimaḷas11akti-
paṇḍita who was the disciple of Nirvvāṇaśaktidēva, the āchārya of the Maṭha attached
to the temple of Chikkēśvara. The latter is said to have been the rājaguru of
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Tailapadēva who was governing Banavāsi-12000 and Pānuṁgal-500
provinces.

inscription.head:
No. 85.
(B.K. No. 35 of 1937-38.)
HIRĒ-BĒVINŪR, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in Siddharāmēśvara Temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 49, Krōdhi, Chaitra ba. 5, Thursday,
corresponding to 1124 A.D., April 5, the weekday being Saturday and not Thursday.

The inscription records a gift of land, money, etc., to Dēvaśakti-paṇḍita on the
occasion of the consecration of the image of Bhōgēśvaradēva at Hiriya-Bēvinūr by
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Ḍākarasa of Tarddavāḍi belonging to the Siḷāhāra family. It also
registers other gifts of taxes to the same temple by the Seṭṭis, Mummuridaṇḍas, and
Ubhaya-nānādēśis of the 36 villages and also by the Five Hundred of the place.
The king is stated to have been ruling from Jayantīpura.

l.ka: varegaṁ pūrvvadriyiṁdattalastamahēṁdraṁbaraṁ samaṁtu nelanaṁ niḥkaṁṭakaṁ mā-
ḍi vikrama[diṁ] visvanarēsvaraprabhuteyaṁ tāḷdirdda cāḷukya cakri mahōtsāhadinā-
gōvaṇarasaṁ ḍākarasa kiriya gōvaṇarasa dharmapratipāḷakaniṁ[da]rasana maga
namasyamāgi biṭṭaru || svasti śrīmatu cāḷukya vikramavarṣada 49 ttane-
ya krōdhi saṁvatsarada caitra bahuḷa paṁcami brihaspativāradaṁdu hi-

riya bēvinūra rēvikavvegavuḍiya puramaṭhada bhō[gē*]svaradē[va*]rgge || © || svasti

ṇaveraḍu nandādīvigege ·alliya maṭada catrakeṁdu sallageyuma yiṁtinitumaṁ sarvvana-

inscription.head:
No. 86.
(B.K. No. 38 of 1936-37.)
KAḌLĒVĀḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab lying in the Svāmiliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya Vikrama year 50, Vis11vāvasu, Pushya Śu. 6, Sunday,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkramaṇa. The details given are irregular. The tithi corresponds to
A.D. 1125, December 3, Thursday whereas the Saṁkramaṇa occurred on Friday, the
25th December.

The inscription records the gift of the village Seṁbevāḍa for the benefit of
the temple of Svayaṁbhu Sōmēs11vara of Kaḍalevāḍa, by the king who was ruling
from Jayantīpura and his subordinate Mahāmaṇḍalēsvara Siṁgarasa of the Sīyāḷa
family. The gift village is stated to have been situated in the kaṁpaṇa of
Kummasi-30 included in the division of Tarddavāḍi-1000.

inscription.head:
No. 87.
(B.K. No. 74 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up near the temple of Sōmēśvara.

inscription.p:
This record, lower portion of which is lost, seems to register a gift to god
Sōmēśvara of Kalkere by Jayasinga, a subordinate of Daṇḍanāyaka Gōvindarasa who
was administering Banavāsi—12,000 and the Vaḍḍarāvuḷa taxes under Mahāpradhāna
Daṇḍanāyaka Anantapālayya who was governing the Two 600 districts, Banavāsi-
12,000, Palasige-12,000, and the Vaḍḍarāvuḷa and Achchu-pannāya taxes of the
Seven and a half lakh country. It contains a description of Gōvindarasa. The
details of the date are all lost.

inscription.head:
No. 88.
(B.K. No. 131 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i:
Slab set up to the left of the Vīrabhadra temple in the compound of
Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription refers to the king's feudatory Kīrtidēvarasa of the
Kadaṁba family as ruling the Banavāsi twelve thousand province.
Published in Ep. Ind. Vol. XVI, pp. 355 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 89.
(B.K. No. 5 of 1936-37.)
ĀLME3L, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab lying in the Īśvara temple.

inscription.p: Only the king's name is preserved in this fragmentary record.

inscription.head:
No. 90.
(B.K. No. 14 of 1936-37.)
CHĀNDAKAVAṬE, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab lying near Basavaṇṇa-bhāvi.

inscription.p: Only the king's name is preserved in this fragmentary record.

inscription.head:
No. 91.
(B.K. No. 17 of 1936-37.)
CHAṬṬARKI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in front of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p: The date portion, except the mention of Sunday, is lost in this damaged record.

The inscription states that Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gōvaṇadēvarasa attacked the
sarvanamasya-agrahāra Cheṭṭurage, while administering the country in conjunction
with twelve nāyakas from his capital at Kannavaḷḷi; killed the dāyādas there and
established his uninterrupted rule. It records a gift of income from taxes due to
his family, i.e. Seḷara-kula, from that village to the mahājanas of that place.
Gōvaṇadēvarasa is mentioned as the servant of Jagadēkamalla.

l.note: The letter 'ra' is written above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 92.
(B.K. No. 58 of 1936-37.)
KOṆḌGŪḶI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in front of Basavaṇṇa temple.

inscription.p: In this badly damaged and effaced record only the name of the king is preserved.

inscription.head:
No. 93.
(B.K. No. 71 of 1936-37.)
MALGHĀṆ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the left wall of Rāmaliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This undated inscription is in Kannaḍa and Sanskrit languages. It records
that the king granted several lands out of the daśavanda and other lands set apart
for himself, for the renovation of, and worship in, the temples of Divākarēśvara and
Sōmēśvara, for a water-shed, a tank, a well and an educational institution for children,
attached to the temple of Divākarēśvara at Mallagāṇa which he had given away to
Divākarabhaṭṭōpādhyāya as a sarvvanamasya gift. The latter was the son of
Śaṅkarabhaṭṭa and grandson of Vāsudēvadvivēdi-bhaṭṭa, and Dēvalakabbā was his
wife. The record contains an eulogy of the king.

[tabhuva]naṁ vapuḥ || gadya || svasti samastajagatprasūtabhagavatō brahmaṇaḥ putra

edition.p: 1) The rest is worn out and effaced.

inscription.head:
No. 94.
(B.K. No. 40 of 1937-38.)
KHĒḌGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab built into the back wall of Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription records a gift of land and house-site to god
Kaṁkēśvara of Khēḍage by Lakshmādēvi, wife of Tribhuvanamalla, and daughter of
Kaṁ kōja and his wife Masaṇikavve-nyakiti. Contains the praśasti of a Śiḷhra chief
(name lost).

l.ka: • © namaḥ tasya(smai) varāhāya līlayā caratē mahīṁ khurāntaragatēyya(tō ya)-

l.note: The slab is broken after this.

inscription.head:
No. 95.
(B.K. No. 55 of 1938-39.)
JAMKHAṆḌI, JHAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Broken piece of a slab from Mādhava-hola at Kaḍkōḷ now kept in the house of
Mr. W. T. Apte.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record in characters of about the 12th century seems to record
certain gifts by Tribhuvanamalla. It mentions the four hundred mahājanas of the
agrahāra of Jaṁbukhaṁḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 96.
(B.K. No. 87 of 1938-39.)
KUNDGŌḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of a slab built into a wall of Gaṅgādharēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record refers to the reign of a Chālukya king • • • • •
malladēva. The details of the date, etc., are lost. It mentions a gift to Kāḷōja, son
of Bammōja who is called the Sūtradhāri of God Svayaṁbhū.

l.ka: • • • tē mahī khurāntaragatō yasya mēruḥ khaṇakhaṇāyatē

inscription.head:
No. 97.
(B.K. No. 95 of 1938-39.)
SAṀSI, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up near the Kalamēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
The inscription is very badly damaged and effaced so that the purpose of the
record cannot be made out.

inscription.head:
No. 98.
(B.K. No. 35 of 1936-37.)
KAḌLĒVĀḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in the Svāmiliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 2, Plavaṁga, Pushya, śu. 5, Sunday,
Uttarāyaṇa-Saṁkrānti, corresponding regularly to A.D. 1127, December 25.

The inscription states that the king who was ruling from his capital at Jayanti-
pura, paid a visit to the temple of Svayaṁbhū Sōmanātha at Kaḍalevāḍa and made
a gift of income from Mēlāḷke of the village Hiṁchagēri situated in Bāgavāḍi 30
a kaṁpaṇa of Alande-1000 Division. The gift was entrusted to Gaṅgarāsidēva.

l.ka: racāravē ttraiḷōkyanagarāraṁbha mūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē || svasti sama-

edition.p: 1) The imprecatory passages which follow are effaced.

l.note: Some space is left after kuḷa

l.ka: kavaruśada yereḍeneya plavaṅgasaṁvatsarada pusya suddha paṁcami-
bhūlōkamalladēvaru kaḍalevāḍada śrī svayaṁbhūsōmanāthadēvara samīpakke bi-
sirada kaṁpaṇaṁ bāgavāḍimūvattaṟa baḷiya bāḍaṁ hiṁcagēriya mēlāḷkeyaṁ

inscription.head:
No. 99.
(B.K. No. 3 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒSVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the backyard of the Taluk office.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Bhūlōkamalla year 53, (wrong for Chālukya-Vikrama
year), Kīlaka, Śrāvaṇa, s11u. 5, Sunday, lunar eclipse. The date is irregular. The tithi
corresponds to A.D. 1128, July 4, Wednesday, but there was no eclipse either on the
preceding or following Pūrṇimā.

The inscription registers a gift of land for the benefit of an educational institution
(ghaḷige) attached to the temple of Svayaṁbhū Dakshiṇa Sōmanātha at rājadhāni
paṭṭaṇa Puligere, by Mahāmaṇḍalēs11vara Jayakēs11idēva.

matta svasti śrīmaccāḷukyabhūlōkamallavarṣada 53 neya kīlaka saṁvatsarada

inscription.head:
No. 100.
(B.K. No. 95 of 1936-37.)
BIJĀPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab No. 49 in the Archaeological Museum.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 3, Kīlaka, Pushya ba. 10, Thursday,
corresponding regularly to A.D. 1129, January 17.

The inscription records a gift of gold for worship and offerings to god Mūlasthāna.
of Sīndige by Daṇḍanāyaka Vayijānāthayya, the Karaas, Manneya Sōvarasa and
Kāḷamarasa of the Manneya of Sindige-12, Kummase-30, and Aṁkulage-50, when
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Permāḍidēva of Taṛikāḍu was governing Tarddavāḍi-1000 province

inscription.head:
No. 101.
(B.K. No. 120 of 1938-39.)
KĀMADHĒNU, KALGHATGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in front of the Kallēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 54, Saumya, Chaitra s11u. 2, Monday.
Saṁkrānti. The tithi corresponds to A.D. 1129, March 23 which was Saturday.
The Mēsha-saṁkrānti occurred on the next day.

The inscription records a gift of land and money to god Kalidēvasvāmi of Sāsalu
by Malliseṭṭi of Hulivara when Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Jayakēsidēva of the Kadamba
family was governing Koṁkaṇa-900 and Palasige-12000 divisions. The gift land
which was purchased by the donor from Sōmēśvara-bhaṭṭa, the son of Nārāyaṇa-
bhaṭṭa, the ūroḍeya of Sāsalu, and two others, was entrusted into the hands of
Tējōrāśi-paṇḍita, the disciple of Dēvarāśi-paṇḍita, the āchārya of the temple. The
record gives a genealogical account of the donor.

inscription.head:
No. 102.
(B.K. No. 48 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Karibasappana-maṭha.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 7, Paridhāvi, Jyēṣhṭha, puṇṇame,
Wednesday, corresponding to A.D. 1132, May 31. The weekday was, however
Tuesday.

The inscription states the rules for constructing houses (in the temple site)
made by Indrakīrti-paṇḍita, the āchārya of Māhāsthāna Goggiya-basadi, perggaḍe
Malliyaṇṇa, the dēvaputrakas and the aruvattokkalu headed by Lakkiseṭṭi. It
also records a gift of land to the pūjāri of the basadi by Hēmadēvabādya.

l.ka: vatsa[rada] jyēṣṭhada puṇnami budhavāradaṁdu śrīmahāsthānaṁ goggi-
ṇanu mukhyavāgi samasta dēvaputrakaruṁ lakkiseṭṭi modalāgi ta-
ḍiyaṁ koṇḍu biḍuvaralliṁ mēle māḍisidavarggā mane paricchēdavaṁgaḍi mane sāmya-
neyakke bīḷsidhāyamaṁ tiṟuvaru mūṟuvarṣadiṁ baḷikkoḍeya[re baṁdu] kā-
ḍidarappaḍe śrī ballavarasarāṇe piriyarasiyarāṇe || © || śrī go[ggi]ya
vāgi koṭṭa keyi mattaru 2 || nakharaṁgaḷa ·ayinūrbbara samakṣadalli koṭa-

l.note: The numeral1 is engraved below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 103.
(B.K. No. 9 of 1937-38.)
ATHARGĀ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in Survey No. 87.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year (lost), Paridhāvi, Māgha, [śu.] 5,
Sunday, the details corresponding to A.D. 1133, January 13, Friday, instead of Sunday.

The inscription records a gift of land by Daṇḍanāyaka Vaijanāthayya, the
perggaḍe of Tarddavāḍi-nāḍu and Daṇḍanāyaka Kalidēvayya, the prabhu of
[Taḍavalage ?] for a tank.

inscription.head:
No. 104.
(B.K. No. 74 of 1936-37.)
MALGHĀṆ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the front wall of Teggina dēgula.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 8, Pramādi, Śrāvaṇa, Amāvāsye,
Solar eclipse, Monday, corresponding to A.D. 1133, August 2. The weekday was,
however, Wednesday.

The inscription records a gift of land and also income from taxes to god
Svayaṁbhūdēva of the agrahāra village Mallagāṇa for worship and offerings, by
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kumāra Sōvarasa of the Śiḷāra family, for the merit of his father
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Siṅgarasa, who was the subordinate of the king's father Tribhu-
vanamalladēva.

l.note: Some space is left after this letter.

l.ka: nōḍuvaru || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁ-
[miḥ] mattaṁ maṁḍaḷēśvara kumāraṁ sōvarasaru srī svayaṁbhudēvargge
siṁdigeya baṭṭeyi[ṁ] mūḍa mallagāṇada sīmeyiṁ teṁka maṁgaḷūra pa-
ḍuvaṇa holadalu dēvarige biṭṭa māṇikēsvara ma 20 ||

inscription.head:
No. 105.
(B.K. No. 42 of 1937-38.)
MAṆṆŪR, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Slab standing in front of the Bhīmēśvara temple on the hillock
called Bhīmēśvara-beṭṭa.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1056, Ānanda, Āshāḍha, Amāvāsye, Solar eclipse,
corresponding to A.D. 1134, July 23, Monday.

The inscription records a gift of land and house to god Svayaṁbhū Bhīmēśvara-
dēva by certain gāvuṇḍas whose names are lost. It refers to a Daṇḍanāyaka and
to a prabhu of Maṇiyūr. The king is stated to have been ruling from his capital at
Kalyāṇa.

l.ka: maṁ kōṭi kavileymaṁ taṁma svahastadiṁ koṁda pāpamaṁ da[kku]varu
svadattaṁ paradataṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁdharā ṣaṣṭirvva-

inscription.head:
No. 106.
(B.K. No. 46 of 1937-38.)
NĀDA (KHURD), INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing near the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 9, Ānanda, Kārttika, śu. [6]
Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1134, October 25.

The inscription records a gift of land, oil-mill, garden and house-site to the
temple of Chaṇḍēśvara, after consecrating the image, by Chaṇḍimarasa of Chikka-
nāḍu. It also records a gift of toll income from the Mummuridaṇḍas to the same
temple.

l.ka: vanāśraya śrī prithvīvallabhaṁ mahārājādhirājaṁ paramēśvara pa-
ḍada baḷiya bāḍaṁ cikkanā[ḍa] • • prabhu caṁḍimarasaru śrī
dhārāpūrbbakaṁ māḍi biṭṭa mattaru 9 gāṇa 1 tōṁṭa ma-
ttaru 1 dēvālyayadiṁ paḍuvalu 10 kayya ·agala nīḷa 15 nivēśana
nāḍu muṁmuridaṁḍaṁgaḷu biṭṭaru hoṁge koṭṭalli mā-

inscription.head:
No. 107.
(B.K. No. 1 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated in the king's regnal year 13, Kālayukta, Mārggaśira,
puṇṇami, Monday, lunar eclipse, saṁkramaṇa. The date is irregular. In the cyclic
year Kālayukta, the given tithi fell on Friday, November 18, A.D. 1138. There was
neither lunar eclipse nor saṁkramaṇa on this day. In the previous year Piṅgaḷa,
the tithi fell on Monday, November 29, A.D. 1137, but the other details do not
agree.

The inscription registers a gift of the kiṛukuḷa taxes and the gold income due
from the dēvabhōga of the village Niṭṭūru, made in favour of the god Svayaṁbhu
Dakshiṇa-Sōmanāthadēva at Purikara, by Jayakēsidēvarasa of the Sagara family, in
conjunction with the One Thousand, headed by Tantrapāḷa Chaṭṭimayya-nāyaka,
to the sthānāchārya Jñānasōma-paṇḍitadēva in the presence of Daṇḍanāyaka
Chiddarasa, Mahādēvarasa and the Mahājanas of rājadhāni-paṭṭaṇa Purigeṛe, etc.

l.ka:
daḷanaṁ ki[rmmī]rasaṁhārapraḷeyaṁ gūrjjaratarjjanaṁ ḍahaḷadōrddarppāpahaṁ
pāṇḍyanirddaḷanaṁ kēvaḷamē[ṁ] babāppa-

nupamaṁ śrī vikramōrvvīśvara[ṁ] || tatputraṁ || ka || madavetti naḍeva rāyaga[ḍa]ṇa
manudyatpratāpata-

panātapadiṁ naḍe koragisuvuddhatikege paḍiyāvōṁ vīrarāya sōmēśvaranoḷ
|| vṛtta || ·ānatōrvvīpāḷaraṁ ma-

inscription.head:
No. 108.
(B.K. No. 188 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab lying near the south prākāra of Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record refers to a Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara probably of the
Kadamba family. All details are lost.

l.note: The next line is damaged and the rest of the inscription is

inscription.head:
No. 109.
(B.K. No. 110 of 1935-36.)
KATAKANHAḶḶI, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Mallikārjuna temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the cyclic year Siddhārthi, Kā[rttika], Puṇṇami, Monday,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkramaṇa. The details are irregular. The tithi corresponds to A.D.
1139, November 7, Tuesday. and the said saṁkramaṇa did not occur that day.

The inscription records a gift of land to god Mallikārjuna at the agrahāra
village [Ka]takanahaḷḷi by Tārkika-Chūḍāmaṇi Kēs11ava-paṇḍita and Mādhava-
paṇḍita. The gift was entrusted to Āditya-Ghaisāsa.

inscription.head:
No. 110.
(B.K. No. 114 of 1938-39.)
GUḌIGĒRI, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Two fragments of a slab standing in the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This record is fragmentary. The details of the extant portion of date are the
cyclic year [Siddhārthi], Pushya, śu. 5, Thursday. This corresponds to A.D. 1139,
December 27, the weekday, however, being Wednesday.

This inscription seems to register a gift of land to god Nāgēśvaradēva. It
mentions Kētōja and Nāgōja and also two Jaina preceptors Nandi-bratīs11a and
Tējōnidhi-bratīśa.

l.ka:
• • • • • • • • • • du svasti śrīmatu pratāpacakravartti jagadēka

inscription.head:
No. 111.
(B.K. No. 21 of 1936-37.)
DEVARNĀVADGI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing in Mallikārjuna temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated the 2nd regnal year, Siddhārthi, Pushya, Paurṇamāsyā,
Sunday, Lunar eclipse, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti. The date is irregular. The tithi
corresponds to A.D. 1140, January 6, Saturday, and the saṁkrānti occurred on
December 25 of the previous year, the weekday being Monday. The said eclipse
did not occur on either of the days.

The inscription, which is badly effaced, because of a thick coat of oil, records a
gift of land, etc., made by Mahāmaṇḍalēs11vara Sōvidēvarasa of the Seḷara family
who was the manneya of Eḷamēla-nāḍu, at the instance of Perggaḍe Dvarāja to
the temple of Mūlasthāna Mallikārjunadēva built by the latter at Nāvidige in
Eḷamēla-ṇāḍu-30. It gives a genealogical account of both these persons.

inscription.head:
No. 112.
(B.K. No. 96 of 1938-39.)
SĀṀSI, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying near the house of Gurubassappa Kumbi.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated in the regnal year of the king which is lost.
The extant details of the date are Pushya, Amāvāsyā, Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti,
solar eclipse. During the reign of this king, the eclipse occurred on the said tithi,
in A.D. 1144, December 22, which might be the probable date of this record. The
weekday was, however, Tuesday.

The inscription contains a second date, the only available details of which
are Āshāḍha, Amāvāsyā.

The inscription seems to record a gift of land, house-sites, and shop to god
Gavaṟēśvaradēva of the agrahāra of Savasi (also referred to as Tapasi-grāma) by the
Four-Hundred Mahājanas who are praised in the beginning. It also records a gift
of income from certain taxes by the Five-Hundred Svāmis of Ayyāvoḷe and the One
Thousand Taṁbuligas to the same deity, made over to Īśānaśakti-paṇḍita, an āchārya
of the Kāḷāmukha sect. The record which contains an eulogy of these svāmis seems
to register another gift on the second date. But the details are all lost.
