inscription.head:
No. 113.
(B.K. No. 85 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the Sōmēs11vara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 9, Akshaya, Āshāḍha, ba. 11, Sunday,
corresponding to A.D. 1146, July 6. The weekday was, however, Saturday.

The inscription states that Kammāṛa Sāntōja fought and died following the
death of Nāḷprabhu Kētiseṭṭi of Kalkere at Sirivoḷal in a fight with the people of
Kāgenele.

l.ka: ya kētiseṭṭiyaru sirivoḷalalu kāgeneleyavaroḍane kādi svargga-
stanādalli kammāṟa sāṁtōja jagaviditavāgi kādi suralōkaprāptanāda

inscription.head:
No. 114.
(B.K. No. 87 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 9, Akshaya, Āshāḍha, ba. 11, Sunday,
corresponding to A.D. 1146, July 6. The weekday was, however, Saturday.

The inscription is incomplete and seems to record the death of a son of Kēta
following the death of Nāḷprabhu Kēti [seṭṭi] of Kalkere at Sirivoḷal during a fight
with the people of Kāgenele. Cf. No. 113 above.

l.ka:
© svasti śrīmaccāḷukya pratāpacakravartti jagadēkamallavarṣada 9 neya ·akṣaya
saṁvatsarada ·ā-

śāḍa bahuḷa ·ēkādaśi ·ādivāradaṁdu śrīmannāḷprabhu kalkeṟeya kētiseṭiyaru
sirivoḷala-
lu kāgeneleyavaroḍane kādi svarggasthanādali sēṇiga[ja]keyana kētana maga

inscription.head:
No. 115.
(B.K. No. 89 of 1936-37.)
YĀRGAL (BHUJRUKH), SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into a wall in the Rāmliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 9, Akshaya, Pushya, śu. 6,
Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti. The date is irregular. The given tithi corresponds to
A.D. 1146, December 10, Tuesday, but the saṁkrānti occurred on December 25,
Wednesday.

The inscription registers a gift of land, garden, oil-mill, shop, house-site and
income from herijuṅka to the temple of Svayaṁbhu-Rāmēs11vara, built at Eraṅgal by
the three brothers, Kēsirāja, Dāsimarasa and Viṭṭarasa. The gift was made by
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Sōyidēvarasa of the Siḷāṟa family, Apparasa, Nandimarasa and
herggaḍe Nākarasa. It gives an account of the family of Apparasa who was a
Prabhu of Eraṁgal, and also of the three brothers, mentioned above. Cf. No. 116
below.

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(B.K. No. 88 of 1936-37.)
YĀRGAL (BHUJRUKH), SINDAGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in Rā0maliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 9, Akshaya, Pushya, śu. 6, Monday,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti. In the given cyclic year corresponding to A.D. 1146, the given
tithi fell on December 10, Tuesday, while the saṁkrānti occurred on December 25,
Wednesday, the tithi being ba. 5. The intended date might be A.D. 1146, December
26, Thursday, when the tithi would be ba. 6.

The inscription records a gift of land for the upkeep and repairs of a tank,
constructed by Sāntiseṭṭi, by Mahāmaṅḍalēśvara Sōyidēvarasa of the Siḷāra family,
Apparasa, the prabhu of Eraṅgal, Nandimarasa and perggaḍe Nākarasa. It gives
a genealogical account of Apparasa and Nandimarasa. The king is stated to be
ruling from Kalyāṇa. Cf. No. 115 above.

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(B.K. No. 5 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall in the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
The record is dated in the king's regnal year 10, Prabhava, Ashāḍha śu, 12
Thursday, Dakshiṇāyana-saṅkramaṇa. śu. 12 seems to be a mistake for ba. 12
because the said saṅkramaṇa occurred on the latter day. Then the date of the
record would regularly correspond to Thursday, 26th June, A.D. 1147.

The inscription registers certain gifts of lands in Kunduravaḷḷi and Huligeṟe
made by Daṇḍanāyaka Mēcharasa when Daṇḍanāyaka Kēsimayya was governing
Belvola-300, Huligeṟe-300, Halasige-12,000 and Hānungal-500 and when Jayakēśi-
dēva of the Maṇala family was the manneya of Huligeṟe-nāḍu.

The record was composed by Karnnāṭasukavi Mukhya-paṇḍita and engraved
by Chāvōja, the eldest son of Jinnōja of Puligeṟe.
Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XVI, p. 44.

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(B.K. No. 93-A of 1938-39.) In A.R.S.I.E. for 1938-39 the number given is 93. But it contains two records, one of
Trailōkyamalla (Sōmēśvara I) and the other of this king. Here the former is given the No. 93 while
the latter, No. 93-A, for the sake of convenience.
KUYIBĀḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up near the mosque close to the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated in the king's regnal year 11, Vibhava, Chaitra,
śu. 15, Sunday, lunar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1148, April 4, Sunday, f.d.t. ·98.
The eclipse, however, did not occur on that day.

The inscription records a gift of paddy (?) by the Gāvuṇḍas, Aṟuvattokkalu and
others. It further records a gift of income from taxes, to the basadi at Kuyyabāḷa by
herggaḍe Mādirāja and Ādityanāyaka at the instance of an officer (name lost) who
was a Mahāpradhāna and Daṇḍanāyaka.

l.note: This is engraved in continuation of No. 32

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(B.K. No. 69 of 1936-37.)
MALGHĀṆ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab fixed in the front wall of Mallikārjuna temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 11, Vibhava, Pushya, śu. 13
Thursday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti, corresponding to A.D. 1148, December 25. The
weekday was, however, Saturday.

The inscription records a gift of land, house-site, and income from taxes for
worship and offerings in the temple of Mādhavēśvara, built by Mādhava-Ghaisāsa a
the agrahāra Mallagāṇa, situated in Kummasi-30, a kampaṇa included in Tarddavāḍi-
1000 province, by the ūroḍeya Gaṅgeya-Mahādēva-Bhaṭṭōpādhyāya and the
prabhus and Mahājanas of the place.

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(B.K. No. 14 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall in the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This damaged record, in characters of about the 12th century A.D., refers to the
Chālukya king with the title Pratāpachakravarti but his name is lost. He was
probably Jagadēkamalladēva II who had this title. The inscription seems to record
gifts of several vṛittis of land to the individuals mentioned as also to some temples.
All details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(B.K. No. 81 of 1938-39.)
KUNDGŌḶ, KUNDGOL MAHAL, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone standing in front of the Hanumantadēva temple.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record, in characters of about the 12th century A.D., seems
to record the gift of a garden. All details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 122.
(B.K. No. 46 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up near the Bilva tree in the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 2, Śrīmukha, Āshāḍha, Puṇṇame,
Monday, lunar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1153, July 6, Monday, f.d.t. 5.6. The
eclipse occurred on the next day.

The inscription records a gift of money to the temple of Svayaṁbhu Dakshiṇa-
Sōmanātha of rājadhāni-paṭṭaṇa Pugigere by Mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Vīra-Pāṇḍyadēva
who was the hiriya-manneya of Huligere-300, Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Jayakēsidēvarasa
and the One Thousand body (parigraha) of the place for their own welfare. The gift
was made over to Jñānasōma-paṇḍitadēva, the āchārya of the temple. Vīra-
Pāṇḍyadēva is said to have belonged to Yadu-vaṁśa.

l.ka: saṁvidhānadalu kālaṁ karcci hiṭṭaṁ hūsi kuḍe haḍedu
yō harēta vasuṁdharā saṣṭirvvarśasahasrāṇā mriṣṭāyāṁ jāya-

inscription.head:
No. 123.
(B.K. No. 58 of 1938-39.)
KALABĪḶGI, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 6, [Bhāva], Pushya, śu 24, Friday,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti. The tithi occurred on A.D. 1154, December 20, Monday
whereas the saṁkrānti occurred on December 25, Saturday.

The inscription refers to the Mahājanas of [Go]ṭṭe, a sarvanamasya-agrahāra.
The details are lost as the record is built in.

l.note: About five lines above this are worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 124.
(B.K. No. 83 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 3, Viśvāvasu, Mārgaśira
śu 9, Monday, corresponding to A.D. 1185, December 2, Monday.

The inscription records the death of Seṭṭima-nāyaka, a servant of Chaladaṁ-
karāma Mādiseṭṭi of Kalukere during the cattle-raid made by Tailahadēva of
Dēvaṅgēri. Cf. Nos. 125 and 126 below.

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(B.K. No. 86 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 3, Viśvāvasu, Mārgaśira, śu 8,
Monday, corresponding to A.D. 1185, December 2.

The inscription records the death of Boppaṇa, a servant of Chaladaṅkarāma
Mādiseṭṭi during the cattle-raid of Kalukere by Tailahadēva of Dēvaṁgēri. Cf. No. 124
above and No. 126 below.

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(B.K. No. 84 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the temple of Sōmaliṅga.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the regnal year 4 of Tribhuvanamalla Vī0ra-Sōmēśvara,
Parābhava, Chaitra s11u 8, Monday, corresponding to A.D. 1186, March 30. The
weekday was, however, Sunday.

It commemorates the death of the hero Saṅkeya-sāhaṇi, a servant of Mādiseṭṭi,
in a fight at Kundugoḷa, when Mādiseṭṭi raided the latter fort.

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(B.K. No. 34 of 1936-37.)
KAḌLEVĀḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the Svāmiliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1108, Parābhava, Chaitra, Paurṇimā, Friday, lunar
eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1186, April 5. The weekday, however, was Saturday.

The inscription records a gift of income from taxes, etc., of the
mēlāḷke-maṁneya of the village Nāvidige situated in the kaṁpaṇa of Kummasi-30 in
Tarddavāḍi-1000 division, to god Svayaṁbhu Sōmanātha of Kaḍalevāḍa for worship,
offerings, etc., by the king, who was camping at Gōkāge, in conjunction with mahā-
pasāyita Kumāra Bommidēvarasa, his maternal uncle Mahāpradhāna, Daṇḍanāyaka
Gōyindamayya, and Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Ānemarasa of Siḷāra family, who was governing
Yeḷamēla. The gift was entrusted into the hands of Sōvarāsi-paṇḍita, the āchārya
of the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 128.
(B.K. No. 36 of 1936-37.)
KAḌLĒVĀḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the Svāmiliṅga Temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 4, Parābhava, Chaitra, Puṇṇami,
Sunday, lunar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1186, April 5. The week-day was,
however, Saturday.

The inscription records a gift of income from taxes, of manne and mēlāḷke
by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kopparasa and others to god Svayaṁbhu-Sōmanātha of
Kaḍalevāḍa for worship, offerings, etc. The gift was entrusted to Sōvarāsidēva,
the āchārya of the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(B.K. No. 113 of 1935-36.)
KUMAṬGI, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Slab built into the front wall of the deserted Hanumān temple in the old
site of the village.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 6, Plavaṁga, Chaitra, ba. 1,
Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1187, March 26, Thursday, f.d.t. ·08.

The inscription records that the image of god Chenna-Brahmēśvara was recon-
secrated and a gift of land was made for the deity by the Mahājanas, Mūliga,
Eṇṭu-Hiṭṭu and others headed by Sōmaya of the agrahāra of Kummaṭage.

l.ka: neya plavaṁga saṁvatsarada caitra ba 1 brihaspativāradaṁdu śrī-

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(B.K. No. 42 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: On a pillar in the utsava hall in the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated in the king's regnal year 2, Plavagma, Pushya,
Puṇṇame, Thursday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti, lunar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1187,
December 17. There was no eclipse on this day and the saṁkrānti occurred on
December 26.

The inscription registers a gift of oil by the Ayvattakkalu to the temple of
Kattamēśvaradēva and also a gift of betel-leaves and money to the temple by the
Five-Hundred. The gift was made over to Aghōraśaktidēva.

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(B.K. No. 111 of 1935-36.)
KUMAṬGI, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Slab built into the front wall of the deserted Hanumān temple in the old site
of the village.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record seems to register a gift of land, oil and income from
taxes on betel-leaves to the temple of Mūlasthāna-Bhōgēśvara, by the Mahājanas
and others of Kummaṭage.

l.ka: • svarau || svasti śrīmatu cāḷukya cakravartti tribhuvanamalla
• ḷḷadalli tōṭa mattaru gāṇakke paḷḷige hēṟiṁge 50

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(B.K. No. 75 of 1938-39.)
KAMḌOḶḶI, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying near the Rāmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged and effaced record refers to a Western Chālukya king. All
details are lost.

l.note: The rest of the record is completely effaced.

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(B.K. No. 21 of 1937-38.)
CHAḌCHAṆ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the prākāra wall of Paramānanda temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 988, Sarvadhāri, Phālguṇa, s11u. 5 Saṁkrānti. The
details regularly correspond to A.D. 1067, February 21, Wednesday. The cyclic year,
however, was Parābhava.

The inscription seems to register the gift of the village Chaṭe (Chaṭetāṇa) by a
subordinate officer of the king who obtained it from the latter. Mentions prabhu
Dāsa, son of prabhu Rājachaṭṭa of the rājadhāni Chaṭetāṇa and a certain Muddarāja.
The king is stated to have been ruling from Mangaḷivēḍa.

ya dāriṁ mūḍa benakana tōṭadiṁ teṁka dēvaraṁga bhōga • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(B.K. No. 66 of 1938-39.)
TADALBĀGI, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone beam of the Īśvara temple called Dēgula outside the village.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 12, Saumya, Chaitra ba, 13, Monday,
Solar eclipse. The date is irregular. The cyclic year Saumya which occurred
during the reign of Permāḍi coincided with A.D. 1129 and the solar eclipse in Chaitra
of that year occurred on Saturday, April 20.

The inscription registers a grant of land made by Śaṁkara-bhaṭṭōpādhāya,
the son of Chaṁgadēva-bhaṭṭōpādhyāya, who had obtained the gift land. The latter
was the prabhu of Taddalabāge.

The grant was made for the worship and offerings of God Sōmanātha at
Taddalabāge. Grants of flower-garden, oil-mill, land and money, made by the sthānas
and baṇañjigas are also recorded.

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(B.K. No. 50 of 1938-39.)
CHIKKALGI, JAMKILANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the Siddhēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated in the cyclic year Īśvara, Kārttika, śu. 9, Monday,
corresponding to A.D. 1157, October 14.

The inscription refers to the joint rule of Bijjaḷa and prince Maiḷugi and records
a grant of land to god Mūlasthāna Kalidēva. The donors of the grant were
Mādirāja of Chikkalige, Rājamatiyavve and Chaṭṭarasa. The Eṇṭu-Hiṭṭu are also
mentioned.

l.ka: ra saṁvatsarada kārttika suddha [9] mi sōmavāradaṁdu mū-
lasthānada kalidēvarige 24 mattaru keymaṁ 24 kayi nivēśa-

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(B.K. No. 45 of 1936-37.)
KAḌLĒVĀḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in front of the temple of Svāmiliṅga.

inscription.p:
This damaged inscription is dated in the Tribhuvanamalladēva year 3, Bahudhānya,
Āśvija, śu. Pūrṇimā, Friday, lunar eclipse. The details correspond to A.D. 1158,
October 9, which was a Thursday and not Friday as stated in the record. There
was a lunar eclipse on that day.
1. The stone is chipped off after this.

The inscription seems to record a gift to the temple of Svayambhū-Sōmanātha-
dēva of Kaḍalevāḍa situated in Kummasi-30. Mentions [Rāma]dēvayya-nāyaka, the
suṅkaverggaḍe of Tarddāvaḍi-1,000 province, Dāsimayya-nāyaka and taḷara-heggaḍe
Nandimarasa. Also refers to a Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara (name lost) who is described as
the ‘lord of Tagarapura’.

l.ka: • ku(ṁ)mmasi mūvattaṟa baḷiya bāḍaṁ kaḍalevāḍada śrī sva[yaṁ]-

l.note: The continuation is very badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(B.K. No. 8 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: On a Slab built into the wall in the backyard of Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1083, 6th year of the king Bijjala, Visha, Āśvija
Pūrṇimā, Monday, lunar eclipse. These details are not regular. The tithi given
corresponds to A.D. 1161, October 6, which was Friday. There was an eclipse on
that day.

The inscription refers itself to the reign of Bijjala. It gives the genealogy
of the Western Chālukya king Nūrmaḍi Taila (i.e., Taila III). Then follows an
eulogistic account of Bijjala. It further states that while Vēsuva-nāyaka and four
other karaṇas were administering Huligere-300, Dēvarrāśi-paṇḍita, the āchārya of
God Rāmaidēva and a disciple of Amṛitarāśi-paṇḍita, purchased some land from
Raṭṭagāvuṇḍa of Beḷuvana-grāma and granted the same for the worship of Muttina-
keyya Indrēśvara. The village Beḷuvana is stated to have belonged to the temple of
Rāmaiyadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(B.K. No. 14 of 1937-38.)
BHŪYĀR, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Broken slab near the ruined Mallikārjuna temple on the
bank of the Bhīmarathī river.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 7, Chitrabhānu, Vaiśākha,
Amāvāsya, Monday. The details of the date correspond to A.D. 1162, May 16, which
was a Wednesday and not Monday.

The inscription states that Mahāpradhāna, Daṇḍanāyaka Lakshmīdēva who was
governing Mūvattāruṁ-bāḍa, visited Buyyāra for the purpose of taking bath in the
river there and made a grant of land, jointly with the four karaṇas and others,
headed by his perggaḍe Nāgyaṇa-nāyaka, to Basavayya, the servant of Śivayōgi
Sātayya of Neluvige for the benefit of the latter.

A further gift to the temple of Abhinaya-Mallikārjuna at Buyyāra, in the year
Sarvadhāri during the reign of Rāyamurāri Sōvidēva, by Kopparasa, an officer of
Maiḷeya-Nāyaka, Chikka-Chaṇḍarasa and others, is recorded at the end of the
inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(B.K. No. 33 of 1936-37.)
KAḌLĒVĀḌA, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the temple of Svāmilinga.

inscription.p:
This record is dated during the reign of Tribhuvanaika-nija-bhuja-Vīrāmalla,
Chitrabhānu, Māgha, ba. 2, Thursday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti. The details of the date
regularly correspond to A.D. 1163, January 24, Thursday. It was a day of
Kumbha-saṁkramaṇa in Uttarāyaṇa.

The inscription records the remittance of taxes on goats and sheep for the
worship and offerings to god Svayaṁbhu Sōmanātha at Kaḍalevāḍa. The grant was
made by Sōvarasa and Ayitarasa, the Sādhavergaḍe of the division of Tardavāḍi-One
Thousand.

l.note: This is apparently a mistake for da.

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(B.K. No. 57 of 1936-37.)
KANNOḶḶI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the right wall of the temple of Hanumān.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated in the year 9 of Tribhuvanaikavīradēva,
Tāraṇa, Āshāḍha, s11u. 2[4], [Thursday], Dakshiṇāyana-Saṁkrānti. The details correspond
to A.D. 1164, June 25, Thursday.

The inscription records a gift of certain incomes made to the temple of
Chikkēśvara-Nāgēs11vara constructed by Bāchiseṭṭi and Nāgiseṭṭi at Kannavaḷḷi, by
Rāmayya-daṇḍanāyaka, Brahmadēva-daṇḍanāyaka, ten prabhus of bhatta-grāma
Kannavaḷḷi and other bodies. Kannavaḷḷi is stated to be stituated in Tārdavāḍi-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(B.K. No. 51 of 1938-39.)
JAMKHAṆḌI, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Slab in the Māruti temple at Ṭakkaḷige now kept in the house of Mr.
W.T. Apte.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated Śaka 1086, Tāraṇa, Mārggasira, Puṇṇami, lunar
eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1164, November 30, Monday.

The inscription seems to registers a gift for repairs of, and worship and offerings in
a temple, the details being lost. It refers to a feudatory of the king belonging to
Beṇṇeva-kula.

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(B.K. No. 66 of 1935-36.)
BĀḶAMBĪḌ, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the temple called Bōḷadēva-guḍi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated the cyclic year Pārthiva, Kārtika, s11u. Monday, Saṁkramaṇa
Neither the Śaka nor the regnal year of the king is given. The tithi also is omitted.
The cyclic year Pārthiva which occurred during the reign of Bijjala coincided with
A.D. 1165 and the S11ukla-paksha of Kārtika fell in October. In that paksha
occurred two Mondays, one on the 11th and the other on the 18th of October. There
was no Saṁkramaṇa on either of the days. In the absence of the tithi it is not
possible to determine as to which of the two Mondays was intended.

The inscription records a grant of land made by Kēśava-daṇḍanāyaka, the officer
in charge of taxes (suṅkādhikāri) for the benefit of the temple of God
Brahmēśvaradēva, attached to that of the God Chikēśvaradēva of Baḷḷarabīḍu.

The grant was made in the presence of the karaṇas, headed by Siddhugi-
daṇḍanāyaka, Hariharagāvuṇḍa, heggaḍe Bīraṇa and others and was entrusted to
Vimalaśaktidēva, the āchārya of the temple.

The conquests of Bijjala are enumerated and the genealogy of Kēs11ava-daṇḍanāyaka
is given.

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(B.K. No. 63 of 1936-37.)
KOṆḌGUḶI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken pillar set up on the bund of the well outside the village.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated in the king's regnal year 11, Vyaya, Śrāvaṇa,
śu. 14, Thursday. The tithi given corresponds to A.D. 1166, July 13, which was a
Wednesday.

The inscription seems to register a gift made to the mahājanas of agrahāra
Koṇḍaguḷi by Nakharas and others. Mentions Kumāra Mallugi. The king is stated
to be ruling from his capital [Kaṅgāra].

l.ka: catē rāmabhadra svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(B.K. No. 52 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in Mādaragēri.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1088, Vyaya, Kārtika, Pūrṇimā, Monday, lunar eclipse.
The date is irregular. In the year cited, the tithi corresponded to A.D. 1166,
November 9, which was Wednesday. There was no eclipse on that day.

The inscription opens with a brief genealogy and eulogistic account of the
conquests of Bijjala. Then follows the genealogy of the king's general Srīdhara, who
was governing Huligere-300.

The purpose of the epigraph is to record the forming of a brahmapuri in the
capital town (rājadhāni-paṭṭaṇa) of Huligere by Śrīdhara-daṇḍanāyaka and his brother
Mācharasa-daṇḍanāyaka. The brahmapuri was donated to fifty-two persons among
whom were a number of brāhmaṇas versed in different branches of learning. The
boundaries of the brahmapuri are specified.

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(B.K. No. 10 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRAHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab in the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This damaged and mutilated inscription, the date of which is lost, begins with
the genealogy of the Chālukya dynasty, down to Taila III. Then follows a
description of the valorous deeds of Bijjala and the genealogy of his general
Rāmadēva-daṇḍanātha. The purport of the record cannot be made out.

l.ka:
ḍuge rāmadēvapṛtanādhīśaṁge saṁprītiyiṁ || jananāthāgraṇi tailanātana magaṁ satyā
śrayaṁ tatta-

nūjananēkārinṛpāḷasindhuramrigēṁdraṁ vikramōrvvīśanātana tammaṁ negaḷdayyaṇakṣi
tibhujaṁ tatsūnu vi-

krāṁtamaṇḍananaṁtā jayasiṁhabhūpanesedaṁ cāḷukyacakrēśvara || ·ene saṁdā jayasi[ṁ]ha
sūnu vimaḷō[dyatkī]-

ḷanaṁ permmāḍibhūpāḷanātana putraṁ bhuvanaikamalla[jana]nāthaṁ taṁnripāḷātmajaṁ ||
jagadēkamalla[ma]-

hipaṁ negaḷdātana tammanapagatārinripāḷaṁ mrigarājavikramaṁ rāyagaṁḍapeṁḍāranenisi
nūrmmaḍi [tai]-

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(B.K. No. 7 of 1937-38.)
ARJUṆAGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone standing near the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record refers to the reign period of king Bijjala of the Kalachuri
dynasty and mentions Amma-gāvuṇḍa of Arjuṇige the heggaḍe of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Īsarasa of Tarddavāḍi-1000.
1. The inscription is broken.

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(B.K. No. 48 of 1937-38.)
NANDARGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in Survey No. 115.

inscription.p:
This short undated inscription records a grant of land to god Dāvapēśvara of
Nandurage by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Bijjaladēva.

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(B.K. No. 51 of 1937-38.)
NIMBĀḶ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of a stone in the compound of Śankaraliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription, the date of which is lost, refers itself to the reign of
Vīra-Tribhuvanamalla. It registers a gift of land by Bammarasa, the prabhu
of Goṇavalage, Ayicha, Kalleya-nāyaka and others and the Eṇṭu-Hiṭṭus. Mentions
Tarddavāḍi-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(B.K. No. 68 of 1937-38.)
SANKH, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Three fragments of slabs built into the wall of the ruined village-chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription which appears to refer to the reign of Bijjala,
ruling from Maṁgaḷivēḍa, records a gift of land and oil-mill made for the worship
and offerings to god Mahimēśvara of Saṅka.

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(B.K. No. 61 of 1938-39.)
KANNŌḶI, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the house of Siddagauḍa Patil

inscription.p:
This worn out inscription refers itself to the reign of [Bijja]ladēva. The date
and other details are lost.

l.note: The remaining portion of the record is mutilated.

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(B.K. No. 37 of 1936-37.)
KAḌLĒVĀḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in the Temple of Svāmiliṅga.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in Śaka 1090, Sarvadhāri, Āśvija, śu. Pūrṇimā, Wednesday,
lunar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1168, September 18, Wednesday, f.d.t. ·03.

The inscription states that Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Simgidēvarasa of the Silāhāra
family who was governing Eḷamēlu, made a grant of certain taxes and incomes due
from Seṁbevāḍa under the administration of the temple of Svayaṁbhū Sōmanātha-
dēva of Kaḍalevāḍa for the benefit of the same temple. The gift was entrusted to
āchārya Sōvarāśidēva. It also registers another gift of one gadyāṇa from each of the
villages in the nāḍu by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gōmadēvarasa. ēteya-nāyaka of Haḍa-
vaḷahaṭṭi and the prabhus and gāvuṇḍas of Eḷamēla-30 and Siṁdige-12, for the benefit
of the same temple. The king is stated to have been ruling from Maṁgaḷivēḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(B.K. No. 3 of 1937-38.)
AGARKHĒḌ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab fixed into the wall of the temple of Saṅkaraliṅga.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the cyclic year Vikṛita, Jyēshṭha, Amāvāsyā, Monday.
The king's regnal year is not given but the cyclic year Vikṛita which fell in the reign
of Sōvidēva, coincided with A.D. 1170. In this year, the details of the date
correspond regularly to Monday, June 15, f.d.t. ·10.

The inscription records a gift of land to Dugga-jīya made by Daṇḍanāyaka
Basavarasa, an officer of Maiḷaya-nāyaka, his brother Kopparasa, Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Vārarasa, the prabhu and gāvuṇḍas of Khēḍa, etc., out of the gift of 325 mattars of
land which had been (formerly) made by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kaiḷāsarasa, a subordinate
of Subhatuṅgadēva to the temple of Kaiḷāsadēva at Khēḍa, the rājadhāni of the Thirty-
six-village (division) included in Tarddavāḍi-nāḍu. It also registers the gift of an
oil-mill to the same temple, by Daṇḍanāyaka Basavarasa and refers to a previous
gift, apparently to the same temple, made by Babbagāvuṇḍa and Lakshmādēvi on the
occasion of a solar eclipse.

khēḍada taṁna hesara dibyaliṁgaṁ śrīkaiḷāsēśvaradēvargge biṭṭa keyi māṇi-
ruṁ khēḍada prabhu gāvuṁḍugaḷuṁ paṁcamaṭha stāna-

rmmamaṁ mana vacana kāyadiṁ keḍisidavaru śrībhīmarathī-

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(B.K. No. 15 of 1937-38.)
BHŪYĀR, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Broken slab near the ruined Mallikārjuna temple on the bank of the
Bhīmarathi river.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 4, Khara, Phālguṇa, Amāvāsye,
Monday. The details of the date correspond to A.D. 1172, February 25, which was
Friday and not Monday as stated in the record.

The inscription registers a gift of land for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple
of god Kūḍaladēva, by Dhāyya, Māya-sāhaṇi, Rēchagāvuṇḍa and others.

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(B.K. No. 42 of 1936-37.)
KAḌLĒVĀḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the temple of Śivaliṅga.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1094, Nandana, Pushya, śu. 8, Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-
Saṁkrānti, corresponding to A.D. 1172, December 24. The week-day was, however,
Sunday.

The inscription states that Daṇḍanāyaka Kumāra Bammidēvarasa who was
administering Sagara, Hagariṭṭage and Eḷamēla, divisions and Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Siṁgidēvarasa of Eḷamēla visited the temple of Svayambhū Sōmanāthadēva at the
ghatikāsthāna Kaḍalevāḍa and made a gift of land for worship, offering and the feeding
of the ascetics in the temple. The gift was entrusted to āchārya Sōvarāśi-paṇḍitadēva
who, in his turn, made some gift of land to his son (putra) Saṁgōja who is called
an architect (sūtradhāri) and an expert in building.
The king is stated to have been ruling from Seleyahaḷḷi.

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(B.K. No. 52 of 1937-38.)
NIMBĀḶ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of stone lying in the compound of Śaṅkaraliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 8, Jaya, Chaitra, śu. 2, Monday.
The details of the date correspond to A.D. 1174, March 6, when the week-day was
Wednesday and not Monday.

The inscription records a gift of land, house-sites etc., to the temple of Kōṭi-
śaṅkaradēva of Niṁbahaḷḷa by Vārarasa, the prabhu of Taḍavalage, and others.

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(B.K. No. 40 of 1936-37.)
KAḌALĒVĀḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the temple of Svāmiliṅga.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 8, Jaya, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 8, Monday,
corresponding to A.D. 1174, July 22, Monday, f.d.t. ·39.

The inscription records a gift of taxes made by Mahāpradhāna Kumāra
Bammidēvarasa to god Svayaṁbhu Sōmanātha of Kaḍalevāḍa. The latter place was
situated in Yeḷamēla-nāḍu, which was governed by the donor. The gift was entrusted
to Sōmēs11vara-paṇḍita.

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(B.K. No. 57 of 1938-39.)
JAMKHAṆḌI, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-Stone in the Temple of Nandigappa.

inscription.p:
In this damaged and broken record only the date portion is preserved. It is
dated in the king's regnal year 8, Manmatha, Mārgasira, s11u. 8, the week-day being
lost. The given details correspond to A.D. 1175, November 23, Sunday.

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(B.K. No. 41 of 1936-37.)
KAḌALĒVĀḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the temple of Śivaliṅga.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1097, Manmatha, Pushya, ba. Amāvāsyā, Tuesday,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti, corresponding to A.D. 1176, January 13, Tuesday. The
saṁkrānti did not, however, occur on that day.

The inscription states that Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Siṁgidēvarasa of Eḷamēlu, belong-
ing to the Silāhāra family, Kasapayya-nāyaka, Mahāpradhāna, Daṇḍanāyaka Kumāra
Bammidēvarasa, śrīkaraṇa Chākaṇayya-nāyaka, the mahāprabhu of Kummasi,
Guṇḍarasa and his brother Mahādēvarasa made a gift of land and certain taxes for
the worship and offerings to the temple of Svayambhū Sōmanātha. The gift was
entrusted to āchārya Sōvarāśidēva. The king is stated to have been ruling from
Maṁgaḷivēḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(B.K. No. 53 of 1937-38.)
NIMBĀḶ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragmentary stone lying in the compound of the Śaṅkaraliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged and mutilated record is dated in the king's regnal year 10, ḍurmati,
Chaitra, śu. 5, Wednesday. In the record, only the lettersau which may stand for ṣaumyavāra, i.e., Wednesday, is visible.āpparently, the cyclic year ḍurmati is a mistake for
ḍurmukhi. The details of the date correspond to A.D. 1176, ṁarch 17, Wednesday.

The inscription appears to record a gift of land to the temple of Śaṅkaradēva
Other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(B.K. No. 4 of 1936-37.)
ĀLMĒL, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab built into the front wall of the Sāvaḷgi Basappa Temple.

inscription.p:
Only the praśasti portion of the king Rāyamurāri Bhujabalamalladēva is preserved
in this fragmentary record.

rāraṁbhamulastaṁbhāya saṁbhavē || svasti samasta śrī pṛthvī-

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(B.K. No. 27 of 1936-37.)
HAḌGINHĀḶ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in the dargā.

inscription.p:
This damaged inscription records a grant made to god [Dhru]vēśvaradēva. The
name of the donor and the details of the grant are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(B.K. No. 70 of 1938-39.)
TUṄGAḶ, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab near the Paramānanda temple outside the village.

inscription.p:
The record is badly damaged and the date-portion is lost, except the week-day,
which was Thursday. Towards the end is contained another date which also is
partially preserved, viz., the 5th year, Khara, eclipse. The regnal year is apparently
of the same king.

The inscription seems to record a gift of land for the benefit of a temple (name
lost) by Mahāpradhāna Chiddarasa, Mahāprabhu Chauḍapa-Bhaṭṭōpādhyāya of
agrahāra Tuṁguḷa, and others. The details of the gift are lost.

It also registers a gift by a Dēvarasa and his brother (name lost) made for
the benefit of the temple of Chennakēśavadēva.
The king is stated to be ruling from Modeganūru.

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(B.K. No. 84 of 1938-39.)
KUNDGŌḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab built into the window of the raṅgamaṇḍapa in the Śaṁbhuliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
The record is damaged and worn out and the date is only partially preserved,
i.e. Friday, lunar eclipse.

The inscription seems to state that the king made a gift of land for the worship
of god Svayaṁbhū and for the feeding of the ascetics, at the request of Mahāpradhāna
Lakshmidhara-daṇḍanāyaka and others.

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(B.K. No. 81 of 1937-38.)
TENIHAḶḶI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken stone built into the wall of the house of the Jahagirdar.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record is dated in the king's regnal year 2, Durmukhi,
Mārgas11ira, Amāvasyā, Thursday, Dhanu-saṁkramaṇa, corresponding to A.D. 1176
November 2. Thursday, f.d.t. ·19. Saṁkramaṇa did not ccur on that day.

The inscription registers a gift of income derived from the cess on paddy, betel
leaves and nuts in the Thirty-six villages division with Chīkka-Rūvige as the chief place.

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(B.K. No. 96 of 1936-37.)
BIJAPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab (No. 56) in the Archaeological Museum.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record is dated in Śaka 1098, Durmukhi, Mārgaśira,
ba. 8, Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1176, November 25, Thursday.

The inscription registers a gift of income derived from cess on certain articles
made by the Five-Hundred of Ayyāvoḷe, Mummuridaṇḍas and others to the temple of
Nāgēs11varadēva.
The king is stated to have been ruling from Māsaṇūru.

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(B.K. No. 63 of 1937-38.)
RŪGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Right side of a stone standing close to the front wall of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated in the second year of Mallugidēva, Durmukhi,
Monday, Saṁkrānti; the other details are not given. It is engraved in characters of
about the 12th century. During the reign-period of Mallugi, the cyclic year corres-
ponded to A.D. 1176.

The inscription registers a gift of land by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvarara Chāmuṇḍarasa
who was governing Taddavāḍi-1000 District, for the benefit of a temple (name lost)
of the agrahāra Āneya Rūvige in Mūvattāṛuṁbāḍa. The gift was entrusted into the
hands of Sarbbēśvaradēva. It also records other gifts by prabhu Brahmadēva and
others (details lost).

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(B.K. No. 20 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab in the back-yard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary and damaged record is dated in the king's regnal year 3,
Viḷambi, Māgha, s11u. 5, Monday, corresponding to A.D. 1179, January 15, Monday.

The inscription records a gift, the details of which are lost, for the repairs to the
temple of Svayaṁbhū-Lakshmaṇēśvara of Huligere. The gift was entrusted to
Trilōchana-paṇḍita.

śaṁge santō • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(B.K. No. 62 of 1938-39.)
KUNCHANŪR, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab lying in the temple of Mallikārjuna.

inscription.p:
In this badly damaged and mutilated record, only the praśasti of the king is
preserved. The extant date portion is Kārtika, Pūrṇimā. The king is stated to have
been ruling from Kalyāṇa.

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(B.K. No. 90 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone in the temple of Sōmēśvara.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's rengal year 4, Śōbhakṛit, Vais11ākha, ba. 1,
Sunday, corresponding to A.D. 1183, April 10, Sunday. The month given was
adhika-māsa.

The inscription records the death of Bammuga, a servant of Chaladaṁka-rāma
Mādaṇa-seṭṭi in a fight with Vemmaṇa at Raṭṭahaḷḷi.

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(B.K. No. 108 of 1935-36.)
BABLĒŚVAR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of a slab fixed in a niche in Virakta-maṭha.

inscription.p:
In this fragmentary record dated during the reign of Kalachuri king whose
name is lost, only the tithi and the week-day, i.e., Paṁchami, Bṛihaspativāra, are
preserved.

The inscription seems to refer to the construction of the temple Mūlasthāna by
the chief of Babbulēśvara (name lost) and mentions the tax-officer (suṁkad-adhikāri)
Tipparasa.

l.ka:
• • • carāja prabhu māḍisidaṁ [||] ma • • māḍisida dharmmamaṁ nōḍi [bā]
geya

l.note: The stone is broken away after this and lost.

inscription.head:
No. 171.
(B.K. No. 83 of 1937-38.)
UMRĀJ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of a stone built into the wall of the village school.

inscription.p:
The inscription which is partially preserved contains the praśasti of a Kalachurya
king whose name is lost.

l.note: The stone is broken after this.

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(B.K. No. 49 of 1937-38.)
NIMBĀḶ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Slab built into the wall (inner side), left of entrance into the central shrine of the
Śaṅkarliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
The record is dated in the king's regnal year 3, Plavaṅga, Bhādrapada, Amāvāsyā,
solar eclipse, probably corresponding to A.D. 1187, September 4, which was a Friday.
There was a solar eclipse on that day but the Kanyā-saṅkramaṇa had occurred on
the previous Friday.

The inscription records a gift of land and toll-income by Māyidēva and others
to Kōṭiśaṁkaradēva of Nimbahura. Other gifts of money and lands in different
villages (specified) made by Gaṁgara Kanniseṭṭi and others, for the benefit of the
same temple, are also recorded.
The inscription is published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XXVIII, pp. 94 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(B.K. No. 13 of 1936-37.)
BEKKINĀḶ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the field called Guttappanavara Hola

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 2, Plavaṅga, Kārtika, Pūrṇimā,
Monday, regularly corresponding to A.D. 1187, October 19, Monday.

The inscription records a gift of land for a feeding house (satra) by Rēchibhaṭṭ-
of agrahāra Kalakere after purchasing it from Daṇḍanāyaka Vikramāditya, the prabhu
of Kalakeṟe. See No. 174 below and Introduction.

l.ka: ya pla[vaṁga] saṁvatsarada kārttikada puṇnami sō-
vāgi krayavaṁ koṁḍu satrakke koṭṭa māṇi-
kēśvarada kōlada daśanivarttana kṣētravī dharmmavaṁ prati-

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(B.K. No. 52 of 1936-37.)
KALKĒRI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab leaning against the front wall of the temple called Mallappana-guḍi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in Śaka 1109, king's regnal year 2, Plavaṅga, Kārttika,
Pūrṇimā, Monday, regularly corresponding to A.D. 1187, October 19, Monday.

The inscription records a gift of land by Rēchi-bhaṭṭa of Kamme-vaṁśa, one of
the Three Hundred Mahājanas of Kalkere included in Hagariṭage-nāḍu, after
purchasing it from Vikramāṅka-daṇḍādhipa, the prabhu of Aśmataṭāka (i.e., Kalkeṟe),
for the feeding house (satra) of the place when Mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Virupadēvarasa
was administering Hagariṭage-nāḍu. The gift was made with the consent of the
Mahājanas of Kalkeṟe. The donor Rēchibhaṭṭa is stated to be the son of
Padmanābha-bhaṭṭōpādhyāya and grandson of Nandyaṇadvēdi.

The record refers to the regnal year of Vīra-Nārāyaṇa who is identified with the
Yādava king Bhillama on the ground that the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Viruparasa and
Vikramāṅka daṇdādhipa are mentioned in other records of this king. ARSIE, 1929-30, B.K. Nos. 18, 55 and 56.

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(B.K. No. 36 of 1937-38.)
HIRĒ BĒVINŪR, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up near the temple of Bhōgalinga.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1112 (nara-nētra-Śaśi-Sōma-Indu), Sādhāraṇa,
Śrāvaṇa, Pūrṇimā, Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1190, July 18. The week-day was
Wednesday, when the tithi ended at ·72.

The inscription states that at the request of Siridēvi, the wife of Boppaṇa-nāyaka,
to observe kshitiruha-nōṁpi, Daṇḍanāyaka Māyidēva, the minister of the king,
planted a number of trees and established a charity fair (dharma-santhe) in
Bēvinūr situated in Tardavāḍi 1000, in the presence of Mummuridaṇḍas and others of
the Thirty-six-villages division. Boppaṇa-nāyaka was governing the division of
Thirty-six-villages as a subordinate of Māyidēva.

It also records a gift of land and house for the maintenance of the above
charities made to Bandarasa, the manneya of Tarddavāḍi-1000 and to Boppaṇa-
nāyaka by Māyidēva. Further gifts of trees to the temples of Taleyakāvadēva,
Rēvaṇēśvara and Bhōgēśvara and also to Sōmanātha-paṇḍita, the composer of the
record, are registered.

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(B.K. No. 114 of 1935-36.)
MADBHĀVI, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar used as foot-step in the Kallēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged and worn out record is dated in the king's regnal year 4,
Sādhāraṇa, Kārtika śu. Pūrṇimā, Monday, Saṁkramaṇa, regularly corresponding to
A.D. 1190, October 15, Monday. There was, however, no saṁkramaṇa on that day.

The inscription seems to record a gift for the worship of god Svayaṁbhu
Bijjalēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 177.
(B.K. No. 23 of 1937-38.)
CHŪRGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: On a Nandi-pillar in front of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated in the king's regnal year • • •, Sādhāraṇa Pushya
s11u. [1]1, Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-Saṁkrānti. During the reign of Bhillama the cyclic
year Sādhāraṇa corresponded with Śaka 1112. In that year, the details correspond
to 1190 A.D., December 24, Monday. The saṁkrānti occurred on the following
Wednesday.

The inscription states that Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vīra-Bandugidēvarasa and
Mahāpradhāna Māyidēva-daṇḍanāyaka made a gift of the village Chōrahārige situated
in the division (kaṁpaṇa) of Thirty-six villages in Tardavāḍi Thousand for the
benefit of the temple of Kapilasiddha-Mallikārjuna of Sonnalige which is called
Abhin11ava Śrīśaila.

l.ka: da koneya jyōtīśvaranuṁ vriṣabhana rūpāgi yajamā-
saṁ pēḷuttaviralu yēṁ besadēvā yenalu yī sthāna-
liṁgakke hastōdakavaṁ māḍi koṭṭuduvaṁ manaṁ pēsade śeḷedukoṁ[ḍape]neṁbā-
puḷugoṁḍada narakadoḷagikki nīnāhutigoṁḍiru kaṁḍā yele pā-
pavē yī dēvana bhūmidhanakkānaṁjuveneṁdu pāvaḍarddante manaṁ
bedaṟi poḍavaṭṭana bēga śīghraṁ koṇḍu bā kaṇḍā yele pu[ṁ]-
ṇnyavē kaviḷāsakke yinteraḍara beṁge pu(ṁ)ṇya pāpaṁgaḷa • •
pārddu koṇḍirddoyvudu tappadu diṭha diṭha sa • • • • •
sarbbanamasyavāgi koṭṭa dharmma caṁdrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ salguṁ yī dharmmavaṁ
davaru yadhōgatigiḷivaru maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī ||

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(B.K. No. 30 of 1936-37.)
HIPPARAGI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab fixed into the wall in the temple of Kāśīliṅga.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1115, Paridhāvi, Bhādrapada, ba. Madhyāshṭaki, Monday,
Kanyā-saṁkramaṇa. The Śaka year given was current. The details correspond to
A.D. 1192, August 31, Monday, f.d.t., ·14. There was no Saṁkramaṇa on that day.

The inscription registers the grant of the village Iṁguṇige, at the request of
Bammeya-nāyaka, for the worship and offerings to god Kalidēva at Hipparage by the
king in conjunction with Phēyiya-sāhaṇi and Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gōṇarasa of the
Seḷaṛa (Silāhāra) family and others to Rājagurudēva of Bhujagāvaḷi-kula while
sarvādhikāri Malleya-Sāhaṇi was governing Tarddavāḍi-nāḍu.

Also records other gifts to the same god by Māyidēva-daṇḍanāyaka, the
officer in charge of taxes, and by Brahmadēva-daṇḍanāyaka headed by Dēvaparasa,
the prabhu of Hipparige and Pārīsadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(B.K. No. 66 of 1937-38.)
SĀLOṬGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up against the front wall of the Chāvaḍi in Holagēri.

inscription.p: In this damaged and worn out record, the date-portion is lost.

The inscription records a gift of the village Māvuliganūr for the benefit of the
temple of Bhōgēśvara at Pauṭhage by Baṁdarasa of the Jīmutavāhana
family who was administering Pauṭhage as a subordinate of the minister Māyidēva.
The gift was entrusted to Amarēśvaradēva, the āchārya of the temple. Mentions
Baṁdarasa's subordinate tantrapāḷa Boppaṇa.

The record gives a genealogical account of Baṁdarasa and a description of the
place Pauṭhage.

inscription.head:
No. 180.
(B.K. No. 43 of 1936-37.)
KAḌLĒVĀḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in the temple of Śivaliṅga.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1114, Paridhāvi, Pushya, ba. 10, Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-
saṁkrānti. The date is irregular. The tithi given corresponds to A.D. 1192,
December 21, when the week day was Thursday and the saṁkrānti did not occur.

The inscription states that Mahāpradhāna Sōyidēva-daṇḍanāyaka who was incharge
of mēlāḷke made a gift of income from Kuṁbiṭṭage, a village in Kummasi-30
included in Tarddavāḍi-1000 division (kampaṇa), to Sātimayya, the ācharya of
Hiriya-maṭha of Svayaṁbhu Sōmanāthadēva, for the benefit of the temple of
Māṇikēs11vara and for maintaining eight brahmapuris, etc.
The king is stated to be ruling from Dēvagiri.

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(B.K. No. 69 of 1937-38.)
SAṄKH, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken stone built into the piel of the Lingāyata maṭha.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged and effaced record is dated in Śaka 1115, Pramādi, Māgha,
Monday, Śivarātri, regularly corresponding to A.D. 1194, February 21, Monday.

The inscription begins with a vachana of Kapilasiddha Mallikārjuna and records
a gift of a village (name lost) for the worship of the god Kapilasiddha-Mallikārjuna
of Sonnalige by Māyidēva-Daṇṇāyaka (i.e. Daṇḍanāyaka).

l.ka: • • • • • gikki nīnāhutigoḷtiru gaṁḍā yele pāpave yī dēva-
pramādi saṁvatsarada māgha māsa • • • • • sōmavāra śivarā-

koṭṭa dharmma caṁdrārkka • • • • • • • • • • yīdharmmavaṁ prati
pāḷisida-

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(B.K. No. 24 of 1936-37.)
DĒVŪR, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the left wall (outer) of the Rāmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged and mutilated record is dated Śaka 1118, Nala, Monday,
solar eclipse (total), Uttarāyaṇa-sāṁkranti. The month and the tithi are not given.
The date is irregular. There was no solar eclipse in the given year. The Śaka and
the cyclic year correspond to A.D. 1196-97.

The inscription refers to a chief (name lost) of the Jīmūtavāhana family and to
Sōvaladēvi and seems to record a gift.

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(B.K. No. 47 of 1937-38.)
NĀDA (KHURD), INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab near the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 2, Piṅgala, Āsh āḍha, śu. 3, Saturday
corresponding to A.D. 1197, June 3. The weekday was, however, Friday.

The inscription records a grant of land for the benefit of the temple of god
Chaṇḍēśvara by Kētagāvuṇḍa and Mudrehasta Mallaṇṇa, a subordinate officer
of mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Lakshmīdēva and others.

l.note: This is engraved in continuation of No. 106 above.

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(B.K. No. 18 of 1936-37.)
DĒVAṆGĀV, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the temple of Kallappa.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Saka 1121 (current), Kālayukta, Māgha, ba. Amāvāsyā,
Monday, Solar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1199, January 28, Thursday, and not
Monday.

The inscription records a gift of land for the worship and offerings to the temple
of Kalidēva of Kēsarpāṭi by Mahāpradhāna, magara-murāri Sōyidēva-nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(B.K. No. 41 of 1937-38.)
KŪḌIGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Nandi pillar in front of the temple of Basavaṇṇa.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated in the king's regnal year 11, Durmati, Pushya,
śu. 11, Monday, Uttārayaṇa-saṁkrānti, corresponding to A.D. 1201, December 23. The
weekday was, however, Sunday.

The inscription begins with the vachana of Kapilasiddha-Mallikārjuna and
records a gift for the benefit of the temple of Kapilasiddha-Mallikārjuna by Daṇḍa-
nāyaka Sōvadēva, Māyidēva-nāyaka and others.

l.ka: gaḷa dhanaṁgaḷa yī liṁgake hastōdakaṁ mā-
naṁ bēga [sī]ghraṁ koṁḍu bā kaṇḍā ya-

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(B.K. No. 44 of 1936-37.)
KAḌLĒVĀḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab in the temple of Svāmiliṅga.

inscription.p:
This record in Nāgari characters and Kannaḍa language is dated Śaka 1127,
Raktākshi Ās11vija, s11u. Pūrṇimā, Sunday, lunar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1204,
October 10, Sunday, taking the Śaka year to be current. There was a lunar eclipse
on that day.

The inscription states that the king renewed the grant of certain villages for
the worship and offerings of the god Svayaṁbhu Sōmanātha at Kaḍalēvāḍa, in the
name and for the merit of Chilādēvi, probably the queen of Siṅghaṇa. The earlier
grant seems to be the same as the one referred to in the latter part of the inscription.
It records a gift for the benefit of the same temple, by Rāyamurāri Jammugidēva,
Bijjaladēva and others. It appears that this grant was discontinued and was
renewed during the time of Siṅghaṇadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(B.K. No. 106 of 1935-36.)
UDAGAṬṬI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying in Survey No. 8.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 1, Prabhava, Chaitra, śu. 1, Sunday.
The tithi corresponds to A.D. 1207, March 1. The weekday was, however, Thursday.

The inscription records a gift made by Sōvagauḍa and Haṁḍe gauḍa to Maseya-
nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 188.
(B.K. No. 75 of 1936-37.)
PAḌAGĀNŪR, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the temple of Hanumān.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 113[1], Vibhava, Kārtika, s11u. 13,
Thursday. Taking the S11aka year to be current, the details correspond to A.D. 1208,
October 23, Thursday.

The inscription seems to record a gift of land to the temple of Acahalēśvara at
Paḍa[ti]ganūru entrusted to the āchārya (name lost) of Svayaṁbhu Kēdārēśvara at
Vijayāpura, called Dakshiṇa-Vāraṇāsi, when Kapiladēva-daṇdanāyaka was governing
Tarddavāḍi-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(B.K. No. 85 of 1936-37.)
YĀRGAL (BHUJRUKH), SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab in the Village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 10, Vibhava, corresponding to
A.D. 1208-09. Other details of the date are not specified.

The inscription records a gift of land made by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Bommidēvarasa
of the Siḷāra family to the temple of Hayimēśvaradēva, constructed by him in the
southern part of Viriṁgal.

l.ka: ge biṭṭa keyi mattaru 6 nivēsaṇa geyi

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(B.K. No. 66 of 1936-37.)
KŌRVĀR, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar built into the stone kaṭṭe (raised platform) in front of Daṇḍappa Gauḍa's house.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged and effaced record is dated in Śaka 1133, Prajāpati,
Kārtika, Amāvāsyā, Sunday, Saṁkramaṇa, corresponding to A.D. 1211, November 6
Sunday, f.d.t. ·27.

This inscription seems to record a gift (of land) made to the learned mahājanas
of Santheya Kō[rā]vūr, for the merit of a certain Boṁmarasa.

l.note: The letter ka is written below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 191.
(B.K. No. 25 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This damaged and worn out record is dated in the cyclic year Yuva, Āshāḍha,
Amāvāsyā, Monday, Pushya-nakshatra. In the reign of Siṅghaṇa, Yuva fell in A.D.
1215 and in that year the tithi corresponded to June 28 which was a Sunday and not
Monday. Pushya-nakshatra coincided with the latter day.

The inscription records a gift of oil for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple
of god [Kēdāra]dēva, by the oil men of Purikaranagara. It appears that Māyi-paṇḍita
was administering Purikaranagara at the time as a subordinate of the king.
The writer of the inscription was a son of Rudrabhaṭṭōpādhyāya.

inscription.head:
No. 192.
(B.K. No. 112 of 1935-36.)
KUMAṬGI, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Slab built into the front wall of the deserted Hanumān temple in the
old site of the village.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year, 16, Yuva, Bhādrapada, śu
Pūrṇimā, Wednesday, lunar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1215, September 9, Wednes-
day. There was an eclipse on that day.

The inscription records the renewal of a gift to the temple of Bhōgēśvara by
Mahadēvarasa and others, of Kuṁbiṭage. The āchārya of the temple was Sōvarāsi.
The details of the gift are not specified.

l.ka: yuva śaṁvatsarada bhādrapada śudha hu(ṁ)ṇnave budhavā-

inscription.head:
No. 193.
(B.K. No. 111 of 1938-39.)
GUḌIGĒRI, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in Kalmaṭha.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 7, Īśvara, Śrāvaṇa, Amāvāsyā,
Thursday, Solar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1217, August 3, Thursday, f.d.t.
14. The eclipse occurred on the following day, i.e. Friday.

The inscription contains a praise of the Twelve (mahājanas) and the Fifty
Families of oil-men (Telligar-Aivattokkalu) of Guḍigere and registers a gift of oil
for burning a perpetual lamp to the temple of Kāḷēsvara. The gift was entrusted to
Tējōnidhidēva, the āchārya of the temple.

l.ka: vartti vīrasiṁhaṇadēvavarṣada 7 neya yīśvara saṁvatsarada śrā-

inscription.head:
No. 194.
(B.K. No. 46 of 1936-37.)
KAḌṆI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in Survey No. 24 near the temple of Kāśīviśvēśvara at old Kaḍṇi.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated in the cyclic year Vishu, Pushya, s11u. Pūrṇimā,
Thursday, regularly corresponding to A.D. 1221, December 30, Thursday.

The inscription seems to record a gift of land made by a certain Kaḍa[ṇa]
[Vā]sitara Nāgadēva.

l.ka: [jyaṁ] visu saṁvatsarada puṣya suddha puṇnami bra-
sēnabhō[ga] dēkaṇna kūḍi mūḍi koḷaga biṭaru dēka-

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(B.K. No. 89 of 1938-39.)
KUYIBĀḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone in front of the temple close to the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 14, Svabhānu, Mārgasira, ba.
Amāvāsyā, Sunday. The details correspond to A.D. 1223, December 23, which was
a Saturday, not Sunday and the tithi ended at ·59 on that day.
The inscription records the death of a hero (name lost) probably of Huligere.

inscription.head:
No. 196.
(B.K. No. 79 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the temple of Sōmēśvara.

inscription.p:
The inscription is dated in the king's regnal year 16, Tāraṇa, Jyēshṭha, śu. 5
Sunday. The date is irregular. The tithi corresponded to A.D. 1224, May 24, which
was Friday and not Sunday.

The inscription records a gift of certain taxes and provisions for the offerings
to God Svayaṁbhu Sōmanātha and the feeding of the ascetics by Kaḍuva
[Ma]hanta, the tax-officer (Suṁkada Hegaḍe) of Lakshmīpāḷadēva.

l.ka: ṟeya śrī svayaṁbhu sōmanāthadēvara nivēdya tapō-
biṭṭaru yī dharmmavanu ·ā staḷada samasta praje muṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 197.
(B.K. No. 20 of 1937-38.)
CHAḌCHAṆ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment built into the village-gate.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record is dated in Śaka 1148, [Vyaya], ba. Amāvāsyā,
Monday, Saṁkramaṇa. The names of the month and the saṁkramaṇa are lost. The
year given corresponds to A.D. 1226-27.

Only a part of the praśasti of a subordinate (name lost) of the king is preserved
in this inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 198.
(B.K. No. 26 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒS11VAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built to the wall of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 11[49], Sarvajit, Kartika, s11u, Ashṭami, Monday,
corresponding to A.D. 1227, October 18, Monday, on which day the tithi commenced
at ·93 and continued on the next day upto ·84.

The inscription records a gift of land and house-site, after purchase from
Goggiyāchārya (?), by Mūrujāvidēva, disciple of Kēdāradēva for the srīkārya of the
gods Kēdāradēva and Chikkēśvaradēva at Purikara-nagara and for the feeding of
the ascetics, when Malliseṭṭi was governing the Karṇāṭa-vishaya. It also records a
gift, made after purchase from Tīkiseṭṭi, son of Bharatiseṭṭi, by the same Mūrujā-
vidēva to Jñānasōma-paṇḍita, the āchārya of the Sōmanāthadēva temple for the
śrīkārya of god Kēdārēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 199.
(B.K. No. 78 of 1938-39.)
KUNDGŌḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab in front of the Durgā temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 32, Śārvari, Chaitra, ba. 11.
Wednesday, corresponding to A.D. 1240, March 20, which was a Tuesday and not
Wednesday. The tithi ended on that day at ·92.

The inscription is in two parts. The first part, in Sanskrit language, records
the gift of six nivartanas of land for the benefit of the temple of Durgā at Kundaṁguḷa
by Mallaśarma, son of Basavaṇa and grandson of Mādhavārya, with the consent of
the king's minister Vāsudēva. The record was composed by Vaijanātha.

The latter part of the inscription, written in Kannaḍa language, apparently refers
to the same gift mentioned above. The donor is called here Taḷiyara Mallayya.
It also records a gift of oil made by the Fifty-Families (Aivattokkalu) of
Kuṁduṁgoḷa, for burning a perpetual lamp in the same temple. The Thousand
Mahājanas of Kuṁduṁguḷa were to protect the gifts.

inscription.head:
No. 200.
(B.K. No. 13 of 1937-38.)
BHAṬGUṆKI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the pial in front of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated in the year Śārvari, Jyēshṭha śu. Monday, Solar
eclipse. The Śaka year is lost except the last digit which is 2. Śārvari corresponds
with Śaka 1162 but the existing details are irregular. The paksha given, viz., S11uddha
is apparently wrong because of the mention of the solar eclipse. Again, in the month
of Jyēshṭha of the given year there was no eclipse at all. The tithi also is lost. In
the previous month however, i.e. Nija-Vais'ākha, both the lunar and solar eclipses
occurred on Monday, the 7th and Wednesday, the 23rd respectively in May, A.D.
1240. The intended date seems to be A.D. 1240, May 23.
The inscription mentions Mahāpradhāna, Sēnādhipati Bommeyanāyaka.

l.note: The letter vē is written above the line.
The letters me and nā are written above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(B.K. No. 39 of 1937-38.)
KAPPANIṀBARGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken Nandi pillar lying in front of the temple of Hanumān.

inscription.p:
This damaged and fragmentary record is dated Śaka [116]5, Śōbhakṛit, Āshāḍha,
Monday, Dakshiṇāyana—Saṁkrānti. The tithi is lost. The details correspond to
A.D. 1243, July 27, Monday. The Saṁkramaṇa occurred on the following day.

The inscription seems to record the gift of a village (name lost) made for the benefit
of the temple of Kapilasiddha [Mallikārjunadēva] of Sonnalige by Mahāpradhāna
Mādhavadēva-nāyaka and his son Kēśavadēva-nāyaka governing from rājadhāni Uṁmaḍe.

l.ka: • • • śrī soṁnalageyapurada śrīmanu mahāmahima śrī kapilasi-
• • • • • saṁyuktaṁ sulka sāda daṁḍa maulika kāru-

inscription.head:
No. 202.
(B.K. No. 53 of 1936-37.)
KALKĒRI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab in the temple called Mallayyana Guḍi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1167, Krōdhi, Bhādrapada, śu. 14, Sunday. The date
is irregular. Taking the Śaka year to be current (Krōdhin), the tithi corresponds to
1244 A.D., August 18. The week day was Thursday and not Sunday as stated in
the record.

This long inscription gives the genealogy of Siṅghaṇa and records gifts of lands and
house-site by pradhāni Malla, Bīchaṇa and his son Pāyiseṭṭi, for the benefit of the
temple of Ananta-Tīrthakara at Kalukere in Hagariṭage-nāḍu constructed by Pāyi-
seṭṭi, at the instance of his guru Kamaḷasēna-bhaṭṭāraka. Pāyiseṭṭi, who is stated to
be the son of Nākiseṭṭi, was given in adoption to Bīchaṇa.

Hagariṭage-nāḍu, Kalukere, Bīchaṇa and his exploits, Pāyisetti and his
brothers are all described at length.

l.ka:
pōddhatōddāmōdyadvijayī parākramavanāṁtaṁ vastupāpastamastōmānaṁtananaṁta
tīrtthakananaṁtasvāmilakṣakkatiprēmaṁ nākana sūnu vaiśyaku-

ḷakaḷpasthāyyanaṁ pāyyanaṁ || vikasāṁbhōruhadaṁte ka(ṁ)ṇgesegu jaṁbūdvīpa
vaṁbhōjakarṇnikeyaṁtippu-

du mēruvaṁbudhiyaṁbhōjakarōruha • • karaṁ • • • • • bharatakṣōṇi
raṁjikuṁ taddakṣiṇabhāgadoḷu viṣada-

davakuḷāgraṇi bhillamabhūmipāḷanā narapatinaṁdanaṁ negaḷda jaitugidēvanavaṁge puṭṭidaṁ
varasutanurvvarābharitabhāradhuraṁdhara-

na(ṁ)nyarāyasiṁdhuramadamānasaṁharaṇa siṁhaparākrama cakri siṁghaṇaṁ || ·ātana
parākramaveṁteṁdaḍe || mattēbhavikrīḍi[taṁ*]

harihēśā[ra][va]diṁ virōdhinriparaṁ beṁnnaṭṭi vidyuṣṭa rāyara saṁ[bhāga] maneyde
koṁdu tanagēkachatravāgalke dikkaribhōgīkamaṭhaśramāpaha-
l.note: The correct reading seems to besaṁghāta.

l.ka:
raṇaṁ kāmēccheyaṁ tāḷdidaṁtire tāḷdaṁ nibiḍōru bhūribhujadoḷu bhūbhāramaṁ
siṁghaṇaṁ || va || ·ā pratāpacakravarti siṁ-

ghaṇarāyaneṁba yādavanārāyaṇana ·avatārakke tārābalaṁ ·ayiśvaryyakke yāditya
balaṁ caturōpāyakke caṁdrabalaṁ bhōgakke bhūbalaṁ digu-

vijayōdyōgakke yōgiṇībalaṁ bhaṁḍārada bhāgyavōlagada mayisiridaśakke dīkṣāguru
rājyakke rakṣāmaṇiyenisida pradhānacūḍāma-

ṇigaḷāreṁdaḍe || vri || ·amaḷacaritradiṁ kulahitārtha ·udārateyiṁ dharātaḷakkamama hitārtha
va(ṁ)nvitacaturṇnayadiṁ pati siṁha-

ṇāvanīramaṇahitārtthavāgi paḍedaṁ naralōkada cikkadēvanuttama suta mallanaṁ śaciva
bīceyaneṁdaḍe cakramallanaṁ || dhareyaṁ nāṁṭirevattu durggata-

tiyiṁ bhaṁḍāradiṁdiṁbugoṁḍurucāturbbaḷadiṁda pallavisi rāṇīvāsavistāradiṁ
caritaṁ puṣpitavāytu cikkana sutaṁ mallāṁkaniṁ bīcaniṁ paramā-

rtthaṁ phalavāytu siṁghaṇanarēṁdrayiśvaryyakalpadrumaṁ || va || ·aṁtavarirvvaroḷu
piriyaṁ malliseṭṭi pradhānōttamana pradhānaśaktiyeṁ-

teṁdaḍe || nyāyamane niṟisi caturōpāyamanodavisi nijādhipati yādavanārāyaṇana
rāmarājyaśrīyaṁ peccisuvaja[ṁ]-

ge nallaṁ mallaṁ || ·ātana magaṁ cāvuṁḍana mahimeyeṁteṁdaḍe || yādavarāyana
rājyaśrīdarppaṇanamama bīcanabhivriddhikarōdvartta-

nacaritra cauṁḍaṁ mēdinigesedaṁ pradhānakuvaranidānaṁ || ·ā pradhānakuḷatiḻaka(ṁ)
napratimallaṁ mallana sahōdaraṁ yādavanārā-

yaṇaṁge voṁdeneya kumārakaṁṭhīravaṁ yaraḍaneya sauparṇnanuṁ mūṟeneya lalāṭa
lōcanaṁ nālkaneya karaṇōdayanuṁ ·ayidaneya vijayabhujādaṁḍa-

nuṁ yāṟaneya cētāṁgabhūtanuṁ yēḻaneya maladhānurūpanuṁ yeṁṭaneya vibhavāṁga
maṁgaḻanuṁ voṁbhattaneya yōgaprabhāvanuṁ hattane-

ya bhāvarasāḻaṁkāranuṁ haṁnoṁdaneya prathamāvatāranumenisida rāyadaṁḍanātha
gaṁdhavāraṇa bīcana patihitakaraprabhāvanāda [viva]-

raveṁteṁdaḍe || negaḷdugrāhitarāyaraṁ kadanadoḷu beṁkoṁḍu saptāṁgamaṁ bage
dōraṁtire taṁdu koṭṭu patigēkādriṣṭitaṁ māḍidaṁdigaḻī kāṁte[ya]

siṁghaṇaṁ sobaganōrvvaṁ gaṁḍaneṁtoṁde sattige voṁdāṇa ·enalke cikkana tanūjaṁ
bīcadaṁḍādhipaṁ || ·ādalladeyuṁ || ·āmarddu bāgidalaṁ • • • • •

dhūḻīpaṭa [māḍidaṁ] samarāḻōkadavā[gni]yeṁduripidaṁ pāṁḍyōgravāgāsiyaṁ
nimirdu dōrasamudramaṁ manade goṁḍāpōśanaṁ geydanapramadiṁ

[niṁ] rūḍhi pāṁḍyāvanīśaśirōjriṁbhitasāṇadolu • • • lupu gharṇneṁbina
• • manidaṁ cikkana bīcigaṁ digibharaṇyōdbhīkaradhvānadiṁ || ·avanipa siṁha-

ṇa besasalaṁ cikkana sūnu bīcanēṟuva turaga prabhūtada • • • tadagraturu[®ka]
pāṁḍya • • • maghadhāṁgarāyarumaṁ cama-

rījamanāgadāgaḷikkuva nevadiṁ sadā tāṁḍavarā mahipāḻa[karāḻa]dhūḻiyaṁ || biruda
virōdhirāyagajamastakavaṁ miduḻuṁ poyva niṣṭhuranaṁ • • • • •

vāṁpaḍe ·ugidettuva dāḍi bisurvva bālavāṁtara rudhirāṁbuvaṁ kuḍiva nālage siṁha-
ṇaneṁba rāyakēsariya gabhīraghōraghana • • • cikkana [sū]nu bī-

[ca]yaṁ || ·antu siṁhaṇana sarvvāṁgasaktiyuṁ svāmibhaktiyuṁ tanaga[ḷa*]ṁkritamāge
dvitīyanārāyaṇanuṁ caturōpāyaparāyaṇanuvenisida

edition.p: 1) The letter ta is written above the line.

l.ka:
bīcidēvadaṁḍanāthacūḍāmaṇi saptāṁgarājyalakṣmīnivāpadaṁteseva dakṣiṇarāyarāja
dhāni huligeṟeya nelevīḍinoḷu [sama]stasukhasaṁkathāvi-

nōdadiṁ rājyaṁ geyyuttamire || bhāvanidhi dharmmasēnatraividyadēvara[khi]ḷajanamunijana
rājīvavikāsadineseva trividhadaggaḷipa tējadiṁ tribhuvanamaṁ ||

dviti mūlasaṁghalakṣmīpuḻakōdamaṁ pogarigaccha mahōtsavamaṁgaḷōdakaṁ śrī
purapāriśvanāthacaraṇānvayadunnativettanī tapa-

[śrī]paripāḻaka kamaḷasēnajinabrativaṁśabhūśaṇaṁ || ·antu negaḻteyaṁ pogaḻteyaṁ paḍeda
kamaḷasēnabhaṭṭārakadēvara tapaścaraṇaparijānñanasamādhānameṁteṁdo-

ḍe || vri || nāḍe guṇabratābharaṇasayyamatuṁgataraṁgadiṁde kaigūḍida sīlaphēna pari
pāṁḍuravrittiyanānta [sō]bheyaṁ sōḍi tapōviḷāsavatiyuṁ sale bhārati-

yuṁ sadā jaḷakrīḍeyanāḍuva[r]kamaḷasēnana śāṁtarasapravāhadoḷu || ·antu
malladeyū || [su]ravallīlatikālavitranatuḻaṁ krōḍācaḷānīkabhīkaravajraṁ

ka || neṭṭane jaṁbūdvipakkiṭṭaḷa hagaraṭagenāḍu nosalā nosaliṁgiṭṭeseva tiḻakadaṁtire paṭṭaṇa
vidu rāyamaḻige kalukeṟeyaseguṁ || ·adalladevuṁ ||

da[ma]diravidu dānada tavarmmane dharmmada janmabhūmi nītiya nijadāgara
mahiyāgara[kuttama] • • [bhabya]kōṭiya manameccu maccuvolanneṁbanne-

gaṁ sogayippudī dharitriyoḻatiśōbheyiṁ sobaganāntu kalukeṟe ·eṁba paṭṭaṇaṁ ||
[aṁteseva] dibyatīrtthasthaḻa kalukeṟeyoḷaśēśabhavyajana-

cūḍāmaṇi śrīdharmmarakṣāmaṇi pāyiseṭṭiyarggaṁ ·avana tīrtthakapratiṣṭheya
caityālayamaṁ sumuhūrttadiṁ nirmmisuvudeṁdu besase

tadgurūpadēśavākyamanuttamāṁgadoḷāṁta mahimanātana kulānvayaprabhāva[meṁ]
teṁdoḍe || vri || vadanaviśōgranalladahivaṁśajanalladavaṁ dvijihva-

nallada gativakranallada gataśri[ta]nallada śūnyakarṇnanallada jalavāsanalladaniḷāsananallada
nāgadēvanabhyudayaviḷāsi roḍḍama[ne]yanvayadanvita[bō]si-

huṭvidaṁ || kaṁ || ·antudayisida budhajanacintāmaṇiyāgi nāgiseṭṭiya[ra] • • kānta
nene vaiśya • • guṇaśrēṣṭhagōtranamaḷacaritraṁ || ·ātana pi-

riya maga hrijjātōpamarūpanakhiḻabhavyōttama vikhyātanātanaruhatpādāṁbujātō
rjjitadharmmanenisida [guṇavarmmaṁ] || kuladabhivṛddhi māṁtanada

buddhi manōrathasiddhiyaṁ mūruṁ tolagada peṁpuṁ • • • • • bāgyada
• • • dharmmadoḷava nalaviṁda • • • • • • • • • • sa-

le taledūgisito guṇavarmmana sarvvaguṇaṁ dharitriyaṁ || ·ā guṇavammanēṁ maṁtrige
tavarmmaneyenippa pu(ṁ)ṇyōdaya • • • doḻurbbareyaṁ sa-

le harisidaṁ pāṣanamaḻasamyagdha[rmmaṁ] || ·avarirvvaroḻage piriyaṁ vividhaguṇābharaṇaguṇa
manātana sati bhūbhuvanavinute sati • • • • •

• bhavanalakṣmi sōmaleyeṁbaḻu || vitataguṇavarmmaneṁnaya pati nākaṇanāci • • •
• • kaḻparu sutarudayisu[vaṁdacaḻavara] • guṇacāritre

nāmanabhirāmaṁ nēmaneṁbarmahātaneyarppuṭpidaralte seṭṭi guṇavarmma • • •
• • • • • || ·antanibariṁ piriyaṁ nākiseṭṭiyara mahimeyeṁteṁdaḍe ||

[ta]nage parōpakāracarita tanuvāge janāṁghribhaktibhāvaneyaṁ nijavacanaṁ vineyavritti
pitrārjjitavritti satya • • • • • • • • • • nija-

priyeyeṁba negaḻte lōkamōhanilenisittu vaiśyakasikhāmaṇi nākanadēṁ guṇāḍhyanō
|| va || ·ātana kaḻatranidhāneyu patihitakaraṇa • • • yuṁ

paramajinacaraṇakamaḷārccita[pā]rijā[te]yuṁ • • • satpātradānasanmāneyumenisida
bācauvveya guṇakathanaveṁteṁdaḍe || vri || ·ā guṇavarmmanagrasuta nā-

(na)kana vallabheyīke saṁpadakkāgaravīke • • matiyīke patibrateyīke peṁpiniṁ
śrīgeṇeyīke dānanidhiyīke dayāpareyīke yeṁdiḷābhāgadoḻirddu • • •

[dē]varīke bācabeyaṁte nalvāṁta • • • • • na sudhājaladhiyiṁdudayisida
• • tyudāra pārijāta harīśvadēvasaṁtānōpamānāgaṇyapuṇya-

caritraka pāyyanatyaṁtaśāntateyaṁ tāldidanātana kiṟiya tamma śāntaneṁbayvaraṁ sutaraṁ
pettanidēṁ kritārtthano jagaprakhyātaroḻu [nāka]ṇaṁ || va || ·antu

na guṇāvaḷiyeṁteṁdaḍe || vri || sakaḷaguṇabratātiśayaneṁba caritranidhānaneṁba tīrtthakara
kumāraneṁba ratunatrayabhūṣaṇaneṁba martyamāṇi-

kanivaneṁba bhabyamukhadarppaṇaneṁba kuvaravaṁśadīpakanivaneṁba peṁpu • • nākana
sūnu śāntanoḻu || va || ·ataniṁ piriyaḻa pūrvvaguṇābharaṇe yāreṁdaḍe ||

|| ka || durmmatavirakte [sati] sāsirmmadharmānukūle subrataguṇade nirmmaḻacaritreyeṁ
bī permmege sōmavveyaṁte nōṁtavaroḷare || ta || ·antirvvariṁda piriyananantaji-

nakramasarōjamadhukaranenalatyaṁtasubhacarita pāyyanane tānuṁ paḍeda pitana janmave saphaḻaṁ ||
vri || vodavida bhaktiyiṁ [janaka]narccisi dibyajināṁghriyugaḷapaduḷa-

dinetti jainamunituṣṭigaḻaṁ [neṟe] māḍi dharmmadoḷā sadamaḻanāgi vartisi
purārjjitapuṇyadoḻemma nākanallada manujarparārtthanidhi pāyiganaṁ pa-

ḍeyalu samartthare || [va] || ·ātana kulānvayapavitrīkaraṇakāraṇa kaḷatrabharaṇe yāreṁdaḍe ||
ka || muṁnave jaṁbūdvīpaṁ tānenisugu pāyyanamama pāyya-

leyamritāṁbōnidhi surataru pādadānāvrite kalpalatike pāyaleyalte || vri || guṇa
voṁdirvara rūpadoṁdeṟakavoṁdāḷāpavādaṁge lakṣaṇavoṁdāṁ[ta]ca-

ritra • • • • • vādaudāryavoṁdoṁde sayiraṇavoṁdoṁdida dharmmavoṁdu pesaroṁ
deṁto vidhātraṁ maṁtaṇeyaṁ māḍidanayya pāyyaleyoḷātmasthāyyana pāyya-

naṁ || va || ·antagaṇyapuṇyōdayanenisida pāyyanaṁ paḍedu nākiseṭṭi rāyadaṁḍanātha
bīcanudarasthāyyanaṁ māḍaloḍaṁ magaṁ baḍedaṁte rāja-

śrēṣṭhi paṭṭama kaṭṭidaṁ || vri || rāyārūḍhacaritra bīcanudarōtpattitvamaṁ dharmma
doṁdāyuṁ śrīyenipēḷgeya kamaḻasēnānugrahōdyaduyaśaśrīyaṁ bhabyajana-

prasaṁgaphaḷa[nēṁ] māḍittu kaḷvāṁtarasthāyi kīrtti[gavā]ryyanabhyudaya sāmūhika
kautūhaḻa || ·ā nuta sārṅgapāṇiparamēśavarasthirarāyarājyasa-

ntānavadā pratāpanidhi siṁghaṇanā[ta]na bāhuśauryyasantānavadā negaḷda kali bīceya
nātana kūrppu dharmma santānavadārkkubērakuḷapāvana pāyanadēṁ kritā-

rtthanō || sutanī pāyanoḍarcci māḍuva • • • • • bīcanuṁtana
bīcanasauryyadu[ṁ]natiyeṁteṁdu siṁghaṇaṁganvitāyatasiṁhāsanacāmarapra-

bhūte yēkachatravā chatradiṁ sale • • • • • dharitri gaḍaniṁnēvaṇnipaṁ baṇnipaṁ
|| vri || ·uṟi[paḻi]vādudī kamaḷasēnamunīṁdragurūpadēśavaṁ baḻikeya ka-

ḷpabhūja ·uditōditanirmmaḷadharmmalakṣmiya(iṁ)neṟadudu nityavāyi tama satya
siḷākṣaramūrttikīrtti kaṇdeṟadu nōḍa bīcana tanūbhava pāyana puṇyadēḷgeyoḷu ||

[amaḷa]napārgge niṁdapudu nilvaḍe kīrttiye nilkumeṁdu neṭṭane tanuva jinēṁdrapathadoḷu
janamaṁ mige dharmmadoḷu jagajjana taledūge nirmmisi negaḷte pogaḷtege nōṁte[se]-

yipā bīcana suta pāyyanallada manuśyarigī yugadali tī[rvvu]dē || va || ·antā dharmma
viraktanuṁ saddharmmayuktanāgi || ka || guru kamaḷasēna jinamunivaranolaviṁ besa-

se taṁde bīcaṁgāyuṁ siriyanodavisuva matiyiṁ paramātthavanaṁtatīrtthakasthāpanavaṁ
māḍi || vri || yidu saṁsārasamudrasētuvidu nirvvāṇaikaniśrēṇiyiṁtidu • •

rmmaṁgaḷa kaṇge • • • • • hadharmmārtthabhaṁḍāraviṁtidu bhavyavaha sēbyadarppaṇavenippā
rūḍhiya māḍidaṁ viditānantajinēṁdramaṁdiraviḷāsasthāiyaṁpāyiyaṁ || ·antu

dharmapratipāḻakaṁ nijabhujaparākramōṁnatanāreṁdaḍe || vri || baḷavadarātimaṁḍaḷikamastaka
sūlanudagravairimaṁḍaḷikamadēbhakēsari madōddhatamaṁḍaḷikā-

caḷēṁdrasaṁkuḷakuḷiśāyudhaṁ birudamaṁḍaḷikārṇnavaghōrabāḍavānaḷanahitōgramaṁḍa
ḷikabhairava vēsugidēva bhūbhujaṁ || © || svasti yamaniyama svādhyāya dhyā-

nadhāraṇamōnuṣṭhānaparāyaṇaruṁ japatapasamādhisīlādyanēkaguṇasaṁpannaru yaupāsa
nāgnihōtra dvijagurudēvatāpūjātatpararuṁ mākaṁdō-

rṇnakuṁḍaḷābharaṇaruṁ ·āśritabudhajanakaḷpavrikṣarumappa kāḷimayyadaṁḍanāyakara dati
śrīmatu sarvvanamasyaduttamadagrahāra kalukeṟeya mahā-

prabhugaḷu mukhyavāgi mūnūrvvaru mahājanaṁgaḷuṁ ·ā maṁneya • • • ·ī
nāḍa seṭṭigutta cauḍiseṭṭiyaru mukhyavāgi muṁmuridaṁḍaṁgaḷu samucitē-

kasthānarāgidda samayadalu rāyapradhāni mallanuṁ bīcaṇanuṁ magaṁ pāyiseṭṭi mukhya
vāgi sakaḷa bhabyajanaṁgaḷu nōṁpi nimittavāgi māḍisida śrīmadananta-

tīrtthadēvara ·aṁgabhōga raṁgabhōga nityanaimittikamahābhiśēka pūjādi caturvvidhi
dāna sanmāna khaṁḍaspuṭita jīrṇnōddhārakyeṁdu śakavarṣada 1167 neya krōdhi

saṁvatsarada bhādrapada śuddha 14 ·ādivāra byatīpāta puṇyadinadalā prathamasthaḷada
madhyakada bhūmiyoḷage ·ā sthaḷada prabhugaḷmukhyavāgi mahā-

janaṁgaḷige pūjeya koṭṭu sakaḷānumatiyiṁda mūlasthānadēvara dattiyōpādiyāgi
sarvvanamasyaveṁdu dhārāpūrvvakavāgi paḍeda kayya sthaḷanirddē-

śavā[vu*]vudeṁdaḍe vūriṁ teṁkalu tuṁbigeya baṭṭeyiṁda paḍuvalu narasiṁhadēvara-
keyiṁdaṁ baḍagalā mallināthadēvara keyiṁdaṁ mūḍalu ·aṟavaṭigeya ke·iṁ-

daṁ teṁkalu ·aḷadikkida māṇikēsvarada [kō]la mattaru haṁneraḍu 12 nivēśaṇa
mallināthadēvara dānasāleyiṁ cikkakāceyana maneyiṁdaṁ paḍuvalu gavaniyiṁdaṁ
mūḍa-

lu yekhyeyagala yippateṁṭu kayya nīḷada dānasāle nivēśaṇavoṁdu gāṇa gāṇakkarddharddha
yaṇne || © || śrīmadiṁguḷigeya mahāprabhu muṭṭarasanu taṁna ·ananta-

dēvara nōṁpi ·udyāpane nimittavāgi koṭṭa mattaruṟu pāyiseṭṭiyaru koṭṭu
haḍada mattarāṟu sthaḷanirddēśavāvudeṁdaḍe kalukeṟeya baṭṭe-

yiṁdaṁ teṁkalu bhōgiseṭṭiya keyiṁdaṁ mūḍalaḷadikkida māṇikēśvarada kola mattaru
12 tiḷuguḷada prabhuviṁge koṭṭu haḍada hūdōṁṭa

mattaroṁdu 1 haṁnikayya nivēśaṇavoṁdu || kramadinasaṁkhyāta nālku mahāmana
sahita jōgavaddigeya caṁdramahītaḷauḷḷanegavamaḷaguṇarkkamaḷasēnadēva-

rggoṁḍaru || ·amarddire paṁcamahāśabdamanī caityālayakkasaṁkhyātarnnālku mahāmane
verasi sthaḷadamaḷatapōdhanaruvittu pūjisikoṁḍaru || © || kṣōṇī-

madhyadoḷaṁkuraṁ negarddudurbbīlōkavidvajjanaśrēṇīhastataḷaṁgaḷalli phaḷamāytā
śādhināṁthāṁganāvēṇībhārada mēle pūtudamamā kāilli hūva-

llidēṁ jāṇme bīcana sūnu pāyyana yēśōvalli latāḍaṁbaraṁ || ka || dhareyaṟeye
bīcidēvana varasuta pāyigana dharmmasā(ṁ)mrājyada meysiri karuvina kavi
kannaḍasa-

rasati viracisidaṁ dēvanī śāsanamaṁ || kalyāṇada rāyarūvāri ballōja kaṁḍarisida
sāsana maṁgaḷa mahā śrī || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁdharā
śaṣṭirvvaruṣasa-

inscription.head:
No. 203.
(B.K. No. 91 of 1936-37.)
BIJĀPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab (No. 46) in the Archaeological Museum.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged and effaced record is dated Śaka 1166, Krōdhi, Māgha, ba,
the tithi being lost. It corresponds to A.D. 1245. It refers to a certain
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara. All other details are lost.

l.ka: vaccharada māgha ba • svasti śrī mahāmaṁḍaḷē-

inscription.head:
No. 204.
(B.K. No. 47 of 1938-39.)
ĀLGŪR, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the Kalamēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 41, Viśvāvasu, Vaiśākha, śu. 14
Wednesday, corresponding to A.D. 1245, April 12, Wednesday.

The inscription records a gift of provisions to god Mallikārjuna by the
Mahājanas and others• The name of the place is partially preserved as • •
gurava, which is in all probability Alagurava.

inscription.head:
No. 205.
(B.K. No. 9 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall in the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated Śaka 1169, Plavaṅga, Jyēshṭha, Amāvāsyā, corres-
ponding to A.D. 1247, June 4, which was a Tuesday.

This inscription states that Rājaladēvi, the daughter of Mahāpradhāna Bīchirāja,
renovated the Vijaya-Jinālaya at Purikara-nagara and made a gift of land and
money for the same, on the advice of her teacher rājaguru Padmasēna and with the
consent of her father.

inscription.head:
No. 206.
(B.K. No. 13 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
The inscription records a gift of land, garden, etc., made, after purchase, by
Saṅgayya, for the benefit of the temple of Remmēśvara, constructed by him in the
premises of the temple of Sōmanātha at Purikara-nagara and named after his grand
preceptor Remmarasa, when Malliseṭṭi was governing Karṇāṭa-vishaya. Malliseṭṭi's
son Rēva is mentioned. It states that Saṅgayya was the pupil of Aichidēva, a
disciple of Remmarasa who was a disciple of Kēśava-daṇḍanātha.

inscription.head:
No. 207.
(B.K. No. 19 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary and damaged record mentions Ārya Malliseṭṭi, a subordinate of
the king.

l.note: Some lines in the beginning are worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 208.
(B.K. No. 19 of 1936-37.)
DĒVAṆGĀV, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the temple of Kallappa.

inscription.p:
This undated record registers a gift of land to god Kalidēva of Kēsarpaṭi by
Magara-murāri Sōyidēva-nāyaka who was a subordinate of king Siṁghaṇa. It also
records other gifts of land, oil and taxes to the same deity by the Mahājanas,
Mummuridaṇḍas and others. This gift was made over to [Śiva]dēva, the āchārya
of the temple. Cf. No. 184 above.
1) The continuation is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 209.
(B.K. No. 87 of 1936-37.)
YĀRGAL (BHUJRUKH), SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab near the Vīrabhadra temple near the temple of Rāmalinga.

inscription.p:
Except the name of the king, nothing else can be made out in this mutilated
record.

inscription.head:
No. 210.
(B.K. No. 78 of 1937-38.)
TADDĒVĀḌI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in the old village site on the bank of the Bhīmarathi.

inscription.p:
The inscription records a gift of some land made to the temple of Chandēśvara-
dēva at Taddavāḍige, by Chandarasa, the manneya of Taddavāḍi-nāḍu, on the occasion
of a total solar eclipse (kattaleya-grahaṇa). The gift was entrusted to Bijja-jīya, the
āchārya of the temple of Sōmanāthadēva at Ṭakkaḷige.

l.ka: [ṭu] hiṭṭu sthaḷa māṁnyada voṁde • • • • • ya baḷiya bā-
