nyā balludu || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vvā yō • • •

inscription.head:
No. 211.
(B.K. No. 53 of 1938-39.)
JAMAKHAṆḌI, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Slab from the Ī0s11vara temple at Madarkhaṇḍi, now kept in the house of
Mr. W. T. Apte.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary and damaged inscription seems to record some gifts, the
details of which are lost. It mentions Madurakhaṇḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(B.K. No. 53A of 1938-39.)
JAMAKHAṆḌI, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Slab from the Īśvara temple at Madarkhaṇḍi, now kept in the house of
Mr. W.T. Apte.

inscription.p:
No details of the date except Śrāvaṇa, ba. Tuesday are preserved in this frag-
mentary record.

It seems to record a grant of oil for some religious purpose, in the presence of
the Mahājanas who assembled in the Sabhāmaṇṭapa for the purpose.

edition.p:
1) This record is found in continuation of No. 211 above (ARS, IE 1938-39, B.K. No. 53). Since
it contains a different date, it has been separately given here as No 53-A.

inscription.head:
No. 213.
(B.K. No. 86 of 1938-39.)
KUNDGŌḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab built into the wall of the Gaṅgādharēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
In this badly damaged and effaced inscription, only the pras11asti of the king is
preserved.

l.ka:
svasti samadhigatapaṁcamahāśabdaṁ dvāravatīpuravarādhīśvaraṁ viṣṇuvaṁśōdbhavaṁ suvarṇna
garu-

inscription.head:
No. 214.
(B.K. No. 90 of 1938-39.)
KUYIBĀḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone close to the temple near the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
The details of the date preserved in this damaged record are only Chaitra śu. 3,
Thursday.
Except the name of the king, nothing can be made out in this inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 215.
(B.K. No. 44 of 1937-38.)
AGARKHĒḌ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab fixed into the wall of the temple of Hanumān.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated Śaka 1170, Kīlaka, Jyēshtha, Pū0rṇimā,
Monday, lunar eclipse. In the given year there were two Jyēshṭhas, adhika and
nija, and the lunar eclipse fell on the Pūrṇimā of nija-Jyēshṭha, which was a Sunday
and not Monday. The latter tithi corresponds to A.D. 1248, June 7. The inscription
seems to record a gift of land to Jaina ascetic of Dēsi-gaṇa, made in the presence
of the Mahājanas of agrahāra Khēḍa and others of that place. The details of the
gift are lost.

l.ka: laka saṁvatsarada jēṣṭa sudha paurṇnamāse sōmavāra
svarada baḷi pāriśvadēvarige taṁma teṁka • • • • • • • • • • dali

inscription.head:
No. 216.
(B.K. No. 54 of 1936-37.)
KALKĒRI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab in the temple called Mallayyana-guḍi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 4, Sādhāraṇa, 8, Monday. The
month is not given. The year Sādhāraṇa in Kannara's reign corresponded with A.D.
1250-51. In the absence of the month, the Christian equivalent of the other
details cannot be found out.

The inscription records a gift of land made to the temple of Ananta-Tīrthadēva
by the Mahājanas of Kalukere on the occasion of the birth of a son in the house of
one Raṅgarasa.

Another gift of income derived from taxes, made to the same temple in the
month of Pushva of the same year by Sarbadhara-nāyaka, is also recorded.

inscription.head:
No. 217.
(B.K. No. 22 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone built into the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1181, (expressed by the chronogram Indu-kari-Rudra)
Siddhārthi, Jyēshtha, ba. 10, Monday, corresponding to A.D. 1259, June 16, Monday, f.d.t. 10.

The inscription commemorates the death of Khaiya (also called Khaila) and
Māyiga who voluntarily lost their lives following the death of their ‘mother’ Lakkhile,
the wife of Āchidēva, a servant of Chāmuṇḍa-Daṇḍēśa.

inscription.head:
No. 218.
(B.K. No. 79 of 1937-38.)
ṬĀKAḶI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab built into the plinth of the local mosque.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription in Nāgari script and Sanskrit language opens with
the Vachana of Kapilasiddha-Mallikārjuna and gives the genealogy of the Yādava
kings upto Kannara.

inscription.head:
No. 219.
(B.K. No. 65 of 1938-39.)
TADALBĀGI, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone-beam in the Ī1śvara temple, called Dēgula, outside the village.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated in the king's regnal year 10, Śukla, Śrāvaṇa, Purṇima
Wednesday. The weekday is wrong. The tithi corresponds to A.D. 1269, July 15,
which was Monday and not Wednesday.

The inscription records a gift of land by Bommarāsi, son of Sōvarāsidēva
to a mason (name-lost) as remuneration for the construction of the śikhara of the
temple of Sōmanātha with brick and mortar. The donor was the āchārya of the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(B.K. No. 81 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the temple of Sōmeśvara.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 12, Pramōdūta, Jyēshṭha, śu. 5,
Monday, Saṁkramaṇa, corresponding to A.D. 1270, May 26, Monday.

This inscription records a remission of certain taxes for the benefit of the temple
of Svayaṁbhu Sōmanātha of Kalukere and for the feeding of the ascetics, by Sitāḻa
Māyidēva, the officer of taxes, at the instance of Sarvādhikāri Viṭṭarasa.

l.ka: ka sarvvamānya yiṁtī dharmmamaṁ staḻada ga-
vuḍu samasta praje stāna mānya muṁmu-

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(B.K. No. 102 of 1935-36.)
HIRĒBIDRI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the gate-way of the Kallēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1205, Svabhānu, Āshāḍha, ba. 30, Sunday, Mithuna-
saṁkramaṇa. The tithi corresponds to A.D. 1283, June 26, Saturday, instead of
Sunday. The month was Adhika-Āshāḍha.

The inscription records a gift of land by Padmaladēvi, wife of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Vikramādityadēva of the Gutta family, for worship and offerings to god Svayaṁbhu
Kalināthadēva of Bidirahaḷḷi, after leaving the feet of Tripurāntakadēva, son of
Mallikārjunadēva. The record gives a short genealogy of Vikramāditya.

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(B.K. No. 23 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone in the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated in the king's regnal year 17, Sarvajit, Kārtika,
s11u. 10, Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1287 October 16, Thursday, f.d.t. ·93.

The inscription seems to commemorate the death of Dāmanāyaka, a hero who
died in a fight with Mummuḍi Siṅgeya of Kummaṭa.

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(B.K. No. 72 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone on the tank bund.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 18, Sarvadhāri, Pushya, s11u. 8,
Saturday, corresponding to A.D. 1289, January 1, Saturday.

The inscription records the death, by Jaina rite, of a certain Mādayya, the son
of Sāyiseṭṭi and Mādavve, and a disciple of Samantabhadradēva, a Jaina ascetic
belonging to Mūla-saṁgha.

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(B.K. No. 28 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing in the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This mutilated record is dated Śaka 1217 (in chronogram), Bhādrapada, śu. 5.
Monday. It corresponds to A.D. 1295, August 16, the weekday being Tuesday and
not Monday.

The inscription seems to belong to the reign of Rāmadēva of the Yādava dynasty.
It registers a gift of land for worship and offerings to god Sāntināthadēva, by Sōmaya
and Padmaya who purchased the land for the purpose. It refers to an officer who
was governing Huligere-300 division. The record mentions certain Jaina preceptors
of Mūla-saṁgha, Sūrastha-gaṇa and Chitrakūṭa-anvaya.

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(B.K. No. 11 of 1937-38.)
BARḌŌL, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the temple called Kumbhāra-dēgula.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1226, Krōdhin, Jyēshṭha. The tithi and the weekday are
not given. The year and the month correspond to A.D. 1304, May-June.

It records a grant of land made to Chalava of Kauśika-gōtra by Kaṁchagāra
Rēvisetti and others, at the instance of Sarvādhikāri Viṭha adēva-nāyaka, an officer
under Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Jaitapāḷadēva-rāṇe.
The donee is stated to have been born in the royal family (rāja-vaṁśa).

daṁ mane 9 koḷake yōgya bhōga caṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(B.K. No. 52 of 1938-39.)
JAMKHAṆḌI, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab kept in the house of Mr. W.T. Apte (originally at Is11vara temple at Kāyibiḷigi).

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1228, Parābhava, Srāvaṇa, ba. 30, Monday. The tithi
corresponds to A.D. 1306, August 10, Wednesday and not Monday.

The inscription records a gift of land for the purpose of offerings to god
Kallinātha of Beḷḷalige, in Kaṇaṁbāde-bāda of Karahaḍa Four Thousand. The gift
was made by Bhāyigauḍa and others, under the orders of Mahāpradhāna, sarvādhikāri
Raṅganātha.

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(B.K. No. 146 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab in the Jaina basti.

inscription.p:
In this fragmentary and damaged record the available details of the date are
Īśvara, Vais11ākha, śu 3, Sunday.

The inscription refers to a Yādava king (name lost) and mentions Kāḷagāvuṇḍa
the son of Boppagāvuṇḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(B.K. No. 77 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the temple of Sōmēśvara.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the king's regnal year 17, Prabhava, Mārgaśira, śu. 11
Monday. The details of the date are irregular. The year Prabhava coincides with
A.D. 1207, but the details do not agree with that year. But in the previous year, i.e.
Kshaya, the details regularly correspond to A.D. 1206, November 13, Monday, ·06,
which seems to be the intended date. According to this reckoning, the commence-
ment of his reign would be A.D. 1190-91, one year earlier than the date suggested by
Fleet (Bom. Gaz., Vol. I, Pt. II, p. 503).

The inscription records a gift of land for the offerings to god Svayambhū
Sōmanāthadēva and for the feeding of ascetics, by Hariyama-śeṭṭi, the nāḻprabhu of
Kalukeṟe, in the presence of the Mummuridaṇḍas, Kācheya-sāhaṇi and Amiteya-
sāhaṇi. The gift was entrusted to Mūrujāvidēva.

Supplementary gifts for the benefit of the same temple on Śivarātri and other
occasions, are also recorded.

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(B.K. No. 130 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i:
Slab set up to the left of Vīrabhadra shrine in the compound of the
Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1290, Kīlaka, Vaiśākha, ba. 30, Monday, Solar eclipse.
The date is irregular. In the given year the tithi corresponded to A.D. 1368, May 17,
Wednesday (not Monday) and there was no eclipse on that day. It may be noted,
however, that on Jyeshṭha ba. 30 of the following year i.e., Saumya, there occurred a
solar eclipse when the tithi commenced on Monday at, ·14 and lasted till ·11 of
Tuesday, the 5th June, A.D. 1369, when the solar eclipse occurred.

The inscription states that when Mahāpradhāna Mādhavāṅka was governing
Banavāsi 12000 province, Nāgappa, the son of Prabhu Śivadēva of Raṭṭakallu installed
an image of god Vīrēśvara in the vicinity of Madhukanātha at Banavāsi, and
made a grant of a village and money to the god. The grant was entrusted to Bikki-
dēva-oḍeya, the son of Lākuḷēśvaradēva-oḍeya. The latter is described as Kādaṁba-
rāya-kul-āchārya.
The king is stated to be ruling from Hastināvatipura.

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(B.K. No. 142 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Tirumaladēva temple outside the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1290, Kīlaka, Āshāḍha śu. 1, Sunday. The date is
irregular. The tithi corresponds to A.D. 1368, June 16, which was a Friday.

The inscription states that when Bukkaraāya's subordinate Mādhavāṅka was
governing Banāvasi 12000, the latter's servant Aubhaḷanātha (i.e. Ahōbalanātha)
effected repairs to the temple of Gōpīnātha, in order to fulfil the desire of his master.
Ahōbalanātha also requested the gauḍas and the subjects (praje) of eighteen
kaṁpaṇas of Gutti whereupon the latter assembling at Banavāsi, made a gift of
land for the benefit of the temple, and entrusted the gift to Bikkidēva, the son (i.e.
the disciple) of Lakuḷīśvaradēva.
The king is stated to be ruling from Hastināvati.

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(B.K. No. 127 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Three faces of a pillar in the temple of Madhukēśvara.

inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 1[3]09, Prabhava, Mārga[śira], lunar eclipse, Monday.
The tithi which is lost was apparently Pūrṇimā. The details correspond to A.D. 1387,
November 25, Monday. There was a lunar eclipse on that day.

The inscription seems to record a gift of land to some deity, whose name is
lost. The donor of the grant, whose name also is lost, seems to have been the
governor of Gōve.

The king is stated to be ruling from Hastināpuri. The writing on the second and
third faces has been erased.

l.ka: radattāṁ nā yō harēta vasuṁdharāṁ śaṣṭivarṣasahaśrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(B.K. No. 33 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Three sides of a pillar in Śankhabasti in Basti-baṇa.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated Śaka 1334, Nandana, Mārgaśira, śu. 2, Sunday, corres-
ponding to A.D. 1412, November 2, Sunday.

This refers to a dispute between Hēmadēvāchārya of Śaṅkhabasti at Huligere
and Sivarāmayya, the achārya of Sōmadēva temple of the same place regarding the
land belonging to the respective temples. The dispute was referred to Mahāpradhāna
Nāgaṇṇa-daṇḍanāyaka, at whose command purōhita Saṅgamadēva and the learned
people of the place studied the documents (śāsana) of both the parties, and settled
it in favour of Hēmadēvāchārya.

inscription.head:
No. 233.
(B.K. No. 119 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab fixed in the south prākāra in the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated Śaka 1354, Pramādi, the other details being lost.
The cyclic year Pramādi coincided not with Śaka 1354 but 1355. Śaka 1354
corresponded to A.D. 1432-33.

The inscription seems to record a gift of land made to Mallaṇṇa, son of
Rāyaṇṇa who died in a fight with the robbers when the latter looted the Madhu-
kēśvara temple, when Mallaṇṇa, son of Chikkidēva, called Tribhuvana-Bharatāchārya
was holding some office at Banavase and Mahāpradhāna Bayicha-daṇṇāyaka was
governing Gōve and Guttiya-Vēṇṭhe.

inscription.head:
No. 234.
(B.K. No. 82 of 1938-39.)
KUNDGŌḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone built into the front wall of the Hanumantadēva temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1366, Raktākshi, Kārtika, s11u. 5. The weekday is not
given. The tithi corresponded with A.D. 1444, October 16, which was Friday.

The inscription states that Vallabharāja and Yiḷamadhirāja installed the image
of Hanumanta at Kundhāgal (Kundagōḷ). Vallabharāja is stated to be the son of
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Singarāja, a subordinate of the king. The sculptor was Kallōja,
son of Bommavōja.

hārāyarugaḷa bhajā pratāpa yinakulatilaka gaṁḍara ga-
ru kuṁdhāgalladalu śrī hanumāṁ[nti]na pratiṣṭheyanu māḍisi

inscription.head:
No. 235.
(B.K. No. 63 of 1935-36.)
SAUNDATTI, PARASGAD TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i:
Pillar in the western entrance into the Yellamma temple on the
Yellamma hill near Saundatti.

inscription.p: This record is dated Śaka 1436, Bhāva, corresponding to A.D. 1514-15.

The inscription states that the work of the upper storey of the stone maṇḍapa
of the western gate was completed on the specified date.

l.ka: ṇa bāgila kalla maṁṭapa mēlaṇa ·upparigeya kelasa samāpta

inscription.head:
No. 236.
(B.K. No. 120 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i:
Slab set up behind the shrine of Narasiṁha, in the compound of the
temple of Madhukēśvara.

inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 1474, Paridhāvi, Srāvaṇa, s11u. 1, Friday, corresponding
to A.D. 1552, July 22, Friday.

The inscription states that when Mahāmaṇḍalēśvaras Rāmārajayya, Tirumalarājayya
and Venkatādrirājayya, the sons of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rangarājayya of the Āravīṭi
family, were govering the fort of Chandragutti, Padumappa, the attendant in the
service of Rāmarāja and Venkaṭādri constructed two raṅga-maṇṭapas in the temple of
Madhukēśvara, one for Pārvatādēvi and the other for Narasimhadēva. Padumappa
acted at the instance of Rāmarāja and Venkaṭādrirāja and for their prosperity.
The king is stated to be ruling from Vidyānagari.

rAjanu tirumalarAjaya venkaTAdriya ULigada padumappanu.

inscription.head:
No. 237.
(B.K. No. 15 of 1938-39.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Mālegitti Śivālaya.

inscription.p:
This record is dated the cyclic year Śōbhakṛit, Āshāḍha, śu. 15, corresponding
to A.D. 1543, June 16. The date is not verifiable.

The inscription states that the bastion was constructed by Haḍapada Eṟa-
kṛishṇappa-nāyaka.
Published in 1nd. Ant., Vol. X, p. 64.

inscription.head:
No. 238.
(B.K. No. 17 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṅga, Āśvija, s11u. 12, Monday,
corresponding to A.D. 1547, September 12, Monday.

The inscription states that at the request of Suḍugāḍa Liṅgiseṭṭi, the king
exempted the agrahāra Huligere from the levy of marriage tax. Suḍugāḍa Lingiseṭṭi
was deputed by the nāḍagauḍas and others of Huligeṟe to request the king for the
exemption.

l.ka: kaikoṁḍu ma • • • • • ru naṁmma kāryyake kartta-

inscription.head:
No. 239.
(B.K. No. 7 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒS11VAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall in the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṅga, Mārgasira, s11u. 10,
corresponding to A.D. 1547 November 22, which was a Tuesday.

The inscription records the gifts, by the king, of taxes made to the barbers
Timmōja, Raṁgōja and Bhadrōja who were brothers. The gift was made at the
request of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja, to whom the barbers had made their request.
The king is stated to be ruling from Vidyānagari.

inscription.head:
No. 240.
(B.K. No. 62 of 1935-36.)
SAUNDATTI, PARASGAD TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i:
Pillar in the Raṅga-maṇḍapa of the Yellamma temple on the Yellamma
hill near Saundatti

inscription.p:
This badly damaged and worn out record is dated Śaka 1482, Dundubhi, Chaitra
śu 1. The date is irregular. The year Dundubhi coincided with Śaka 1484 and
not 1482. Since no weekday is given, the date is not verifiable. Taking the Śaka
year to be correct, it may be equated with A.D. 1560, March 27, Wednesday.

The inscription seems to record a gift of land by Viṭhapa, the secretary
(kāryakartta) of Venkaṭādrirājayya, who probably obtained the gift from Mahāmaṇḍa-
lēśvara Rāmarājayya.

l.ka: © śubhamastu svasti samastabhuvanāśraya śrī pruthvīvallabha mahā-
hana śakavaruśa 1482 neya duṁdubhi saṁvacharada cayi-

inscription.head:
No. 241.
(B.K. No. 121 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab leaning against the west prākāra wall in the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1494, Prajāpati, Vais11ākha, ba. 14, Tuesday. The
details of the date correspond to 1571 A.D., May 22, Tuesday, the Śaka year being
current.

The inscription records a gift of land by Arasādēvi and the son (not named) of
Immaḍi Arasappa Oḍeya, the Mahāprabhu of Sōde, who belonged to Kauśikagōtra
and Āpastambha-sūtra. The gift was made for the benefit of the temple of
Narasiṁhadēva at Banavase, at the instance of Raṁgapparājayya, son of Mahā-
maṇḍalēśvara Rāmarājayya-Venkaṭādrirājayya. The latter is referred to as the
kāryakarta of Sadāśivarāya, while Immaḍi Arasappanāyaka-Oḍeya wasa subordi-
nate of Raṁgapparāja.

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(B.K. No. 41 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the northern entrance into the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This undated epigraph records the remission of marriage-tax in Huligere-nāḍu,
by the king at the instance of Liṁgiseṭṭi. This may be assigned to the reign of
Sādās11ivarāya of the Vijayanagara empire.

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(B.K. No. 71 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone set up in the Basavaṇṇa temple in the village.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the cyclic year Prabhava, Āśvayuja, śu. 10. The Śaka
year and the week-day are not given. The year Prabhava that fell in the regime
of Rāmarāya, who is referred to in the record, coincided with A.D. 1567 and Chaitra
śu. 10 fell on March 20, Thursday.

The inscription records a gift of land made by Vopaṇa to Parvata-oḍeya, for the
merit of Kāmapanāyaka, the secretary (kāryakartta) of Māhāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja
and Veṁkaṭarājayadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(B.K. No. 36 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒS11VAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the right wall in Śankha-basti in Basti baṇa.

inscription.p:
This inscription in Sanskrit language refers itself to the reign of Mārasiṁha
and is dated in Śaka 890, Vibhava, corresponding to A.D. 968-69.

It registers a grant, by the king, of the village Siggala, to Gaṅga-kandarpa-
jinālaya, a Jaina monastery, apparently called after the king. The grant seems
to have been entrusted to Jayadēva-paṇḍita.

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(B.K. No. 38 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: On a slab fixed in the left wall of the Sankha-basti in Basti-baṇa.

inscription.p:
This Sanskrit inscription referring itself to the reign of Mārasiṁha Satyavākya
Koṇguṇivarma is dated in Śaka 890, Vibhava, corresponding to A.D. 968-69.

It records a grant of some lands to Gaṅgakandarpa-Jinālaya, named after the
king. The grant was made over to Jayadēva-paṇḍita, a Jaina priest. The genealogy
of the Gaṅga king is given.
The record is published in Ind. Ant. Vol. VII, pp. 101 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 246.
(B.K. No. 30 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒS11VAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up near the Brahmadēva temple in Basti Baṇa.

inscription.p:
The inscription which is very badly damaged is dated in Śaka 893, Prajāpati,
Kārtika śu. Pūrṇimā, corresponding to A.D. 971, November 5, Sunday.

It appears to record a grant for effecting repairs to Śankha-Jinālaya and for
feeding the ascetics. The grant was made by Mārasiṁha jointly with another
individual whose name is lost. The details of the grant are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 247.
(B.K. Nos. 54 and 55 of 1935-36.)
SAUNDATTI, PARASGAD TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab fixed in the right wall of the Aṅkēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
There are two dates in this record. The first is dated Śaka 970, Sarvadhāri,
Pushya, ba. 7, Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti. The details given are irregular. The
said tithi occurred on A.D. 1048, December 30, Friday when there was no saṁkrānti.
In the previous year Sarvajit, the tithi corresponded to A.D. 1048, January 10. But
there was no saṁkrānti on that day. The second date is Śaka 1009, Prabhava,
Śrāvaṇa, Amāvāsya, sunday, solar eclipse, regularly corresponding to 1087 A.D.,
August 1.

The inscription records the renewal of a former gift of land, which had lapsed,
by the Raṭṭa Chief Kārtavīrya in conjunction with his wife Bhāgaladēvi to Jñāna-
śakti-vratīndra, disciple of Sōmēśvara-paṇḍitadēva of Puliyarasara-maṭha. It is

1) This inscription is noticed in JBBRAS, Vol. X, pp. 172-73.
stated that the former gift was made by Mahāsāmanta Aṅkarasa, grandfather
(actually grandfather's brother) of Kārtavīrya, to Sōmēśvara-Panḍitadēva for the
benefit of the temple of Aṅkēśvara built by him at Sugandhavarti. It is also stated
that Aṅkarasa was a feudatory of Trailōky amalla (Sōmēśvara I).
The record is damaged and worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 248.
(B.K. No. 60 of 1935-36.)
SAUNDATTI, PARASGAD TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1151, Sarvvadhāri, Āshāḍha, Amāvāsyā, Monday, Solar
eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1228, July 3. The Śaka year was current.

The inscription records various grants of lands by Rājaguru Munichandradēva
and others for the worship of and offerings to god Mallinātha who was installed by
Kēsirāja, the prabhu of Kōlāra, when Mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Lakshmīdēvarasa was
governing the Sugandhavartti-12 division from Vēṇugrāma. The record contains the
genealogical account of both Kēsirāja and Lakshmīdēvarasa.
The record is published in J.B.B.R.A.S., Vol. X, pp. 260 ff.

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 249.
(B.K. No. 143 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in the Jaina Basti.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in the 12th regnal year of Kādamba Chakravarti Vīra-
[ma], Durmati, Kārtika ba. 5, Monday. This Vīra[ma] may be a mistake for
Vikrama. In that case Vikrama will have to be identified with the son of Jayasimha.
It may be noted however that no inscription of this Vikrama has as yet been found
and it is doubtful if he ruled at all. The details of the date may be equated with
A.D. 1081, October 25, Monday. It may also be noted that Kīrtidēva who came
after him has for him a date as early as 1068 A.D.

It records the death, by the rite of samādhi, of Bhōgave, the wife of Tippiseṭṭi
Sātaya. She is stated to be the lay disciple of Sakalachandra-bhaṭṭāraka belonging
to Dēsi-gaṇa, Pustaka-gachchha, and Koṇḍakund-ānvaya.

l.ka: © svasti śrīmatu kādaṁbacakravartti kaligaḷaṁkusa gaṁḍara dāvaṇi vīra-
[ma] varṣada 12 durmmati saṁvatsarada kārttika bahuḷa paṁchami sōma-
drabhaṭṭārakara guḍḍi tippiseṭṭi sāteyana heṁḍati bhōgave

inscription.head:
No. 250.
(B.K. No. 65 of 1935-36.)
BĀḶAMBĪḌ, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero stone set up in the temple of Rāmēśvara.

inscription.p:
The inscription which refers itself to Tailahadēva ruling from Naraymagal is
dated in the 2nd year of his reign, Prajāpati, Kārtika, śu. Pūrṇimā, Monday.

The name Tailaha suggests that he belonged to the family of the Kadaṁbas of
Hāngal. But the 2nd year of none of the Tailahas of this family coincided with the
cyclic year Prajāpati.

It records a grant of land at Kalkere made by Bammagauḍa, in honour of his
father who died in a raid made by Tailaha on Baḷḷarabīḍu in course of his attack on
Huriṭigi-nāḍu, the fort of which was in charge of a certain Sōvarasa.

l.ka: svasti śrīmatu 2 neya prajepati saṁvatsarada kārttika su puṇnami sōmavā-
parisagara basavaṁ haritandu hebbāgilalli ·aḷaṁ taṟibi kādi sattu svarggakke [pō]da
·ātana maga kaḷiga bammagauḍanu prajeyu kalakeṟeya [mū]ḍalēriya keḷage gadde
kamma 12 hakkalu gauḍaruṁbaḷiyalli maraḍeya baḍiyalli kamma 20

inscription.head:
No. 251.
(B.K. No. 145 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab set up in Tirumaladēva temple.

inscription.p:
The date of this inscription is the 1st regnal year of [Kāva]dēva, Pingaḷa, Māgha
śu. 5, Monday. But this date does not correspond to the 1st year of Kāvadēva.
Since the record is much damaged, it is difficult to make out the exact name of the
king.

It records the death, apparently by a rite, of a certain Marakave, a lay disciple
of a Jaina ascetic.

inscription.head:
No. 252.
(B.K. No. 121 of 1938-39.)
KĀMADHĒNU, KALGHATGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in front of the temple of Kallēśvara, outside the village.

inscription.p:
This inscription referring to the rule of Śivachitta-Permāḍi is dated in his
25th regnal year, Khara, Pushya śu. 14, Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-Saṁkrānti. The given
Pushya s11u. 14 fell on Monday but there was no Saṁkrānti on that day. Pushya ba.
14 also fell on Monday but the Saṁkrānti occurred on the previous day. Taking
the latter to be the intended date, the details may be equated to A.D. 1171, December
27, Monday.

It records a grant of land to god Kalidēva of Sāsala in Palasige-nāḍu. A grant
made by a certain Kumāra Mallayya for the worship and offerings to Toṟeyadēva, is
also recorded.

The record states that Śivachitta-Permāḍi was governing Koṅkaṇa 900 and
Palasige 12,000, from his capital at Chandrāpura. Eulogistic account of the exploits
of Śivachitta's son Permāḍi and Vijayāditya is given. Kumāra Mallayya is described
as the favourite disciple of Vijayāditya.

inscription.head:
No. 253.
(B.K. No. 124 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing between the shrines of Madhukēśvara and Pārvatīdēvi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1521, Viḷambi, Śrāvaṇa, s11u. 15, [Sunday], lunar eclipse,
corresponding to A.D. 1598, August 6. The Śaka year was current.

The inscription records a gift of land to the temple of Madhukēśvaradēva of
Jayantīpura by Mahāprabhu Immaḍi Arasappa-nāyaka of Sōde.

l.ka:
kyanagarāraṁbhamūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē | harērlīlāvarāhasya daṁṣṭrādaṁḍaḥ

inscription.head:
No. 254.
(B.K. No. 117 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone couch kept in a room in the compound of Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the cyclic year Vibhava, Māgha, Śu, Śivarātri, Wednesday.
On palaeographical grounds, the record may be assigned to the 16th century.

The inscription in Sanskrit verse, states that the stone couch was presented to
god Madhukēśvara of Jayantipura, for use in the spring festival, by Raghu, the king
of Sōdā.

l.ka:
śrī varṣē vibhavē rutau śiśirē mūghākhyamūsē sitē pakṣē sachivarātri saumya
divasē sōdā raghakṣmābhṛtā | pa[ryya]ṁkōśmamayō vasaṁtakutukāyā
sthānikē maṁṭṭapē

inscription.head:
No. 255.
(B.K. No. 125 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Pārvati shrine in the compound of Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the cyclic year Nandana, Vasanta, Monday, the details being
not verifiable. It is in characters of about the 16th century.

The inscription records that the stone maṇḍapa in the Pārvati shrine
was an offering of Sadās11iva-rājēndra of Sōde.

l.ka: ṁdrara biṁnāha śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī ||

inscription.head:
No. 256.
(B.K. No. 132 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Vīrabhadradēva shrine in the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of the 16th century A.D., is dated the cyclic year
Jaya.

The record state that (this is) the service of Sadās11ivarājēndra of Sōde. It
mentions Kanakāvati which was an other name of Banāvasi.

inscription.head:
No. 257.
(B.K. No. 53 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the Mallikārjuna temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1497, Dhātu, Māgha, ba. 30, Monday. The details are
irregular. Śaka 1497 corresponded to the cyclic year Yuva, when the details regularly
correspond to A.D. 1576, January 30. In the cyclic year Dhātu corresponding to Śaka
1498, the tithi corresponds to A.D. 1577, February 17, when the weekday was
Sunday. The former might be the intended date.

The inscription records a gift made by Mahārāja Barigidēva-nāyaka of the village
Gauḍagere, situated in Lakshmaṇēśvara-sīme of Huligeṟe-nāḍu which territory had
been received by him as Amara-Mokhāsi-Nāyakatana from Alli Edlikhāna-Pātaśāhi.
The gift was made over to Nāgaṁṇa-voḍeya for the benefit of the temple of
Mallikārjuna.

group.h3:
No. 258-270.
(B.K. Nos. 1 to 13 of 1938-39.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
group.p:
These label records, in characters of about the 7th Century A.D., contain names
of individuals, possibly of pilgrims and other visitors.

p.i: Pillar in the double-storeyed maṇḍapa on the northern fort-wall.
Another pillar in the same maṇḍapa.
Slab built into the back wall of the Īśvara temple in the Northern fort.
Rock wall to the left of the passage leading upto the same fort.
Cliff overhanging the Baṭṭerappa temple.

inscription.head:
No. 271-275.
(B.K. Nos. 16-20 of 1938-39.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
These label records, in characters of about the 7th Century A.D. contain
names of certain individuals.

p.i: Slab built into the right wall of the gate leading to cave No. III.
Slab built into the Left Wall.
Outer rock wall to the right of the above cave.

edition.p: These two inscriptions are engraved in shell characters.
edition.h4:
276.
(B.K. No. 35 of 1938-39.)
MAHĀKŪṬA, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Saṅgamēs11vara temple in the compound of Mahākūṭēśvara temple.

edition.p:
This record, in characters of about the 7th century A.D., contains the name of
an individual. Cf. No. 257 above.

inscription.head:
No. 277.
(B.K. No. 84 of 1936-37.)
YĀRGAL (BHUJRUK), SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up in the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka [7]00, Durmati, Chaitra, Amāvāsyā, [Satur]day. If
the Śaka year was current, the details given correspond to A.D. 777, April 12, when
the cyclic year was Piṅgaḷa and not Durmati. In the cyclic year Durmati corres-
ponding to Śaka 703 (781 A.D.), the tithi fell on March 29, Thursday.

This badly damaged inscription records the death of an individual in a fight
It refers to Kanna and a perggaḍe.

l.ka: svasti śakanṛpakālātīta sabhacchira sataṁga ·e[ḷu]nūri durmmati sa[mba]-
• • yadā bha(ṁ)ṇḍaṇade kōcaḍi caṭṭara[kā]va kannaṁ nida • • •

inscription.head:
No. 278.
(B.K. No. 99 of 1935-36.)
SIḌĒNŪR, HIREKERUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of a stone set up near the Basavaṇṇa temple.

inscription.p:
This incomplete record, in characters of about the 8th Century A.D., mentions
Saḷuki-rājya, and refers to Dōsi and Pogilli.

l.ka: © śaḷuki rājyadā keḍuvalla dōsiya pogilliya sā

inscription.head:
No. 279.
(B.K. No. 95 of 1935-36.)
SIḌĒNŪR, HIREKERUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken hero-stone set up near the Kallēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record, in characters of about the 8th-9th Century A.D., seems
to record the death of a hero. It refers to a certain Aridhīra.

l.ka: valli muduvāka koṇṇindara kudure parivisi biḻdha

inscription.head:
No. 280.
(B.K. No. 36 of 1938-39.)
MAHĀKŪṬA, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Pillar inside the temple of Saṅgamēśvara in the compound of the
Mahākūṭēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 856, Jaya, Kārtika śu. 5, Wednesday, corresponding to
A.D. 933, October 15, Wednesday, f.d.t. ·03.

The inscription records a grant by Mahāsāmanta Bappuvarasa. It is published
in Ind. Ant., Vol. X, pp. 104 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 281.
(B.K. No. 41 of 1938-39.)
MUḶḶŪR, RAMDURG TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i:
Pedestal of a hero-stone standing near the Iśvara temple at Rāmatīrtha
about two miles from the village.

inscription.p:
This incomplete record is dated Śaka 895, Śrīmukha, Vaiśākha, Puṇṇame, Monday
corresponding to A.D. 973, April 20, the weekday being, however, Sunday.

The inscription seems to record the death of Nāḻchuga, younger brother of
Kāḷamma.

inscription.head:
No. 282.
(B.K. No. 75 of 1937-38.)
TAḌAVALGĀ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Bottom side of the stone now used as door frame of the
central shrine of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated Śaka 9 • • • Amāvāsyā, the other details being lost.
The date cannot be verified. The characters are of about 10th Century A.D.

The inscription mentions Āychapayya and Niṁbadēva-Bhaṭṭa. The details of
gift cannot be made out.

inscription.head:
No. 283.
(B.K. No. 49 of 1938-39.)
ĀLGŪR, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken stone lying in the Kalamēśvara temple.

inscription.p: This damaged record is dated Śaka 939, Piṁgaḷa, corresponding to 1017-18 A.D.

The inscription seems to record the setting up of a stone pillar by Vaijapayya
and three others at the instance of Kaḍitaverggaḍe Poḻalamayya. It refers also to
some gifts made by the gāvuṇḍas of [A]lguṟuva.

inscription.head:
No. 284.
(B.K. No. 33 of 1937-38.)
HIRĒ-BEVINŪR, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of a slab standing near the Siddharāmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record is dated Śaka 955, Śrīmukha, ba. 2, Wednesday, Visākhā-
nakshatra. The name of the month is not given. The intended date seems to be
A.D. 1033, March 21, Wednesday.
The inscription is broken away after the date portion.

inscription.head:
No. 285.
(B.K. No. 118 of 1938-39.)
AḌŪR, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing near the Kaḷamēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated Śaka 989, Plavaṅga, Pushya, ba, 1. Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa
saṁkrānti, corresponding to A.D. 1067, December 23, Sunday, f.d.t. 54. The saṁkrānti
occurred on the next day.

The inscription records a gift of 300 plam-trees for the benefit of the temple of
Kāḷēśvara, by gāvuṇḍa Āytavarmayya and others. The gift was made over to
Bāḻachandrapaṇḍita of Śakti-parshe. It refers to [Poṭṭi]yūru and Pāṇḍi[yūru].

l.ka: • • pakṣapāti ḷa sāma • • • baḷajaḷadhi baḍavānaḷa cōḷa-

inscription.head:
No. 286.
(B.K. No. 15 of 1936-37.)
CHĀNDAKAVAṬE, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar lying in the well called Basavaṇṇona bhāvi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 989, Plavaṅga, Phālguṇa, ba. 3, Sunday, corresponding
to 1068 A.D., February 24.

The inscription states that the memorial-stone of Māghaṇandi-Bhaṭṭāraka of
Sūrastha-gaṇa was set up by Jākiyabbe of Sindige. Koppaseṭṭi and Chandiseṭṭi
were responsible for its erection.

l.ka: © svasti sakavarṣa 989 plavaṁga saṁvatsarada phālguṇa ba-

inscription.head:
No. 287.
(B.K. No. 56 of 1938-39.)
JAMKHAṆḌI JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab from Olyappa's Maṭha now kept in the house of Mr. W.T. Apte.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the cyclic year [Dundubhi], Chaitra, ba. 5, Monday,
Vishuva-saṁkrānti, Solar eclipse. Obviously the eclipse could not have occurred on
the given tithi. On grounds of palaeography the date may be equated with A.D. 1082
March 21, Monday. The saṁkrānti did not occur on that day.

It registers a gift of land to god Uttarēśvara of Jaṁbukhaṇḍi. The beginning of
the record is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 288.
(B.K. No. 46 of 1938-39.)
SŌRĒBĀN, RAMDURG TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i:
Stone window in the left outer wall of the temple of Śabari (Sōravva) in
a valley about two miles from the village.

inscription.p:
This record, in Sanskrit language and Nāgari characters, is dated Śaka 1031
Śukla, Vaisākha, the other details being lost. The cyclic year corresponded to Śaka
1011 (A.D. 1089-90). The details are insufficient for verification.

The inscription records the commencement of the car festival on the specified
date.

inscription.head:
No. 289.
(B.K. No. 94 of 1938-39.)
SAṀSI, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab leaning on the wall near the Śaṅkaraliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record, in characters of about the 11th Century A.D.
registers gifts of land, oil-mill and shops. Other details of the gift and the name of
the donor are lost. It mentions Seṭṭi Mārajayya.

l.note: A few lines in the beginning are worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 290.
(B.K. No. 60 of 1937-38.)
RŪGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing near the front wall of Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This incomplete record, in characters of about the 11th-12th Century A.D.,
mentions the Five Hundred mahājanas headed by the Uroḍeyas Āchimayya,
Dāmaṇa-Bhaṭṭa-Sōmayāji and Aṇṇamayya.

inscription.head:
No. 291.
(B.K. No. 144 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken stone set up in the Jaina basti.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary and damaged record is dated Śaka 103[6], Sādhāraṇa, Kārtika,
the other details being lost. The details cannot be verified.
It seems to mention some officer. All details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 292.
(B.K. No. 25 of 1937-38.)
HALSANGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of stone lying in the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
The available details of the date in this damaged record are Śaka 1090, and
Ās11vayuja, Paurṇamāsi, Thursday, lunar eclipse. The date corresponds to
A.D. 1168, September 19, Thursday it the Śaka year is 1090 itself. The eclipse
occurred on the previous day when the tithi commenced after ·04.

The inscription seems to record a gift on the first date to Dōra Jinā[laya]. It
also registers a gift of land and income from taxes to god Svayaṁbhudēva, by
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Chauṇḍarasa of the Silāhāra family on the occasion of a lunar
eclipse.

edition.p: 1. The record is broken after this.

inscription.head:
No. 293.
(B.K. No. 106 of 1938-39.)
YELIVĀḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in a ruined well near the Kalamēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1115 (given in words), Virōdhikṛit, Uttarāyaṇa. The
cyclic year given corresponds to Śaka 1113 and not 1115. The intended date may
be A.D. 1191, December 26, Thursday.

The inscription registers a gift of land by the One Thousand of the mahāgrahāra
(name lost) for a well caused to be constructed by Ermmeyara Mādiyaṇa. It contains a
genealogical account of the latter, and mentions Beḷvola-nāḍu. The beginning of the
record is lost.

edition.p: 1. The stone is broken after this.

inscription.head:
No. 294.
(B.K. No. 21 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone built into the wall of the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This undated inscription in Kannaḍa verse records that people mourned over
the death of Kaṁpila and Siḍila-Bomma. It refers to Dhavaḷaṁka-Bhīma, perhaps
a title of Kaṁpila. In characters of about the 12th Century.

l.ka: nōrppanu sattude[ṁdarā] ballida kaṁpilaṁ siḍila bo-

inscription.head:
No. 295.
(B.K. No. 50 of 1935-36.)
LAKS11HMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pedestal now bearing a Basava image near the Sahasraliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
It records that the image of Vāgdēvī was installed by Bāchirāja. In characters
of about the 12th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 296.
(B.K. No. 51 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the well known as Jōḍu Kappali-bhāvi.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record mentions Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Boppadēva, the manneya
of Huligeṟe-nāḍu, Jayakēsidēva and a son of the latter. In characters of about the
12th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 297.
(B.K. No. 82 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated the cyclic year Sādhāraṇa, Tadige, Friday. The details of
the date cannot be verified.

The inscription records the death of a certain Maṇḍalika of Uddhare, in a fight
at the fort, during the reign of Kāvadēva. This latter person might have been the
same as Kāmadēva of the Kadaṁba family, who was a feudatory of Sōmēs11vara IV,
the Western Chālukya king. In characters of about the 12th Century A.D.

l.ka: ya kōṭeyalli kādi satta tadige śukravāra
l.note: The second panel is completely worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 298.
(B.K. No. 8 of 1936-37.)
ĀLMĒL, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying on the road to Kaḍṇi.

inscription.p:
The inscription states that it was the boundary-stone of the field belonging to
god Uttarēśvara of Eḷemēla. In characters of about the 12th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 299.
(B.K. No. 39 of 1936-37.)
KAḌLĒVĀḌ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in the Svāmiliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th Century A.D., registers a gift
of tolls on certain articles of merchandise for the benefit of the temple of
Svayaṁbhu-Sōmanātha of Kaḍalevāḍa, by the Mummuridaṇḍas from thirty-six centres,
who had assembled as mahā-nāḍu. It also registers a gift by Dūḷarasa who was
the nāḍa-bīravaṇiga.

inscription.head:
No. 300.
(B.K. No. 47 of 1936-37.)
KALKĒRI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab lying near the well called Ūra-bhāvi.

inscription.p:
The record in characters of about the 12th Century A.D., seems to register
gifts of land as bhaṭṭa-vṛitti for teaching Nyāsa, Prabhākara, Vēdānta, Ṛigvēda,
Purāṇa, etc., and also gifts for dancing girls, drummers and others. The beginning
and the end are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 301.
(B.K. No. 50 of 1936-37.)
KALKĒRI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken Slab built into the raised platform of the mosque near the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription, in characters of about the 12th Century A.D.,
seems to record a gift to god Kēśavadēva. It mentions Kāḷimarasa who was probably
Mahāpradhāna and Kaḍitaverggaḍe.

inscription.head:
No. 302.
(B.K. No. 60 of 1936-37.)
KOṆḌGŪḶI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab fixed into the right outer wall of Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription records a gift of land measured by the Bandhu-
chintāmaṇi pole, house-site, garden, etc., to a god and two hundred Vājasanēya
Brāhmaṇas of the place. All other details are lost. In characters of about the
12th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 303.
(B.K. No. 82 of 1936-37.)
SINDGI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab placed in a niche in the courtyard of Paḍaseṭṭi Siddarāmappa's house.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of land and garden, after purchase, to a
temple by the Mahājanas of Sindige headed by Ūroḍeya Sivanātha. In characters
of about the 12th Century A.D.

l.ka: ṇḍu koṭṭa kaiyi mattaru pattu tō-

inscription.head:
No. 304.
(B.K. No. 98 of 1936-37.)
AṆṆIGERI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Base of the Vināyaka image set up on the roof of the maṇḍapa in the Amṛitēśvara
temple.

inscription.p:
The record states that the image was got prepared by Mallaṇṇa and Nākimayya,
son of Dhuṇḍadēva described as the son of the Thousand Sameya who were the
dēvaputrakas of god Amṛitēśvara of Aṇṇigere. In characters of about the 12th
Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 305.
(B.K. No. 6 of 1937-38.)
ARJUṆGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone standing near the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th Century A.D., seems to praise
Rannavīra as the ‘fortress of his lord’ and the ‘store-house for his father’.

inscription.head:
No. 306.
(B.K. No. 37 of 1937-38.)
HORTI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of stone built into the platform of the mosque.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record, in characters of about the 12th Century A.D., seems to
register a gift of land to a deity (name lost). It mentions the village Horati.

inscription.head:
No. 307.
(B.K. No. 64 of 1937-38.)
RŪGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Left side of a stone standing close to the front wall of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record is in characters of about the 12th Century A.D.
Only the imprecatory portion can be made out.

inscription.head:
No. 308.
(B.K. No. 67 of 1937-38.)
SĀLOṬGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of a stone lying in the Mallayyana-guḍi.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record, in characters of about the 12th Century A.D., contains
a verse describing a village, probably Sāloṭgi.

inscription.head:
No. 309.
(B.K. No. 74 of 1937-38.)
TAḌAVALGĀ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Front side of the stone now used as door frame of the
central shrine of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th Century A.D., records a gift of land
and money. It refers to Kailāsa-nṛipa and his son Dhanasaṅgraha who are praised.

inscription.head:
No. 310.
(B.K. No. 80 of 1937-38.)
TĀMBE, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab built into the front wall of the Police Quarters.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record, in characters of about the 12th Century A.D., refers
to a Sāmantādhipati and Daṇḍanāyaka. All other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 311.
(B.K. No. 84 of 1937-38.)
UMRĀJ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of a slab lying in the village chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th Century A.D., registers a gift of
income from toll on ten cattle for the benefit of the god Yōgēśvara, by
Basavarasa with the permission of Suṁkaveggaḍe Vāmarasa at the instance of
Bijjagāvuṇḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 312.
(B.K. No. 73 of 1938-39.)
KAṀḌOḶḶI, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone built into the ceiling of the Rāmēśvara temple.

inscription.p: This record, in characters of about the 12th Century A.D., describes Nākibhūpa.

l.ka:
rāmapratiṣṭitaṁ liṁgadarśanādipaṁ janmanisvarśanādanyajanmani • • • • • • • • • •

māḍidoḷpudutthinutamāytu dhāriṇi kuḷācaḷa caṁdra dinēśaruḷḷina[ṁ] || ©

inscription.head:
No. 313.
(B.K. No. 74 of 1938-39.)
KAMḌOḶḶI, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Lintel of the door-frame in the Rāmēśvara Temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th Century A.D., contains verses in
praise of Nākabhūpa who was the son of Sōvarasa. Cf. No. 312 above.

l.ka: nē ·ammamma tānantakaṁ vilasaddhāriṇimadhyadoḷ budhanuta śrī nākabhūpāḷakaṁ ©

inscription.head:
No. 314.
(B.K. No. 112 of 1938-39.)
GUḌIGĒRI, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Kalmaṭha.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th Century A.D., registers a gift of land
made after purchase, by the twelve gāvuṇḍas of Guḍigere for the worship of the
Śiva-liṅga named Kalidēva installed by Kallimayya who also donated money with
which the gift land was purchased.

inscription.head:
No. 315.
(B.K. No. 32 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒS11VAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pedestal of the image of Vīrabhadra in the Vīrabhadra temple.

inscription.p:
The record states that Chauṇḍarāya had the image of Lakshmīnārāyaṇa
installed in memory of Malla and Mallāṁbā. In characters of about the 12th-13th
Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 316.
(B.K. No. 89 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up to the right of the Sōmēs11vara temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th-13th Century A.D., records the death
of Hiriya Nāgaiya, son of Nāḷprabhu Sōyaseṭṭi, in a fight.

l.ka: bhōgamaṁ || hiriya nagaiyana kalu || maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī ||

inscription.head:
No. 317.
(B.K. No. 91 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAI, TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up to the right of the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th-13th Century A.D., records the
death of Chika-Nāgaiya, son of Naḷprabhu Sōyaseṭṭi, in a fight. Cf. No. 316 above.

l.ka: rggābhōgamaniddu nāganeradaṁ svarggōtthasōbhōgamaṁ || cika nagaiyana kalu maṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 318.
(B.K. No. 22 of 1936-37.)
DĒVŪR, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in the Dyāmavva temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th-13th Century A.D., states that this is
the niśidhi of Siṅgiseṭṭi, Dēviseṭṭi, Padumavva and Siṅgeya who were the disciples of
Nēmidēva of the Iṅgaḷēśvara lineage belonging to Mūla-saṁgha, Dēsi-gaṇa, Koṇḍakunda
anvaya and Pustaka-gachchha.

l.ka: hā śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 319.
(B.K. No. 64 of 1936-37.)
KOṆḌGUḶI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
First fragment of the third face of a broken pillar set up on the bund of the well
outside the village.

inscription.p:
This damaged record, in characters of about the 12th-13th Century A.D., seems to
register a gift of land, income from taxes and oil to god Sōmēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 320.
(B.K. No. 64-A of 1936-37.)
KOṆḌGUḶI, SINDGI, TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Second fragment of the third face of a broken pillar set up on the bund of the well
outside the village.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th-13th Century A.D., seems to register
a gift of land and money to god Sōmanātha.

inscription.head:
No. 321.
(B.K. No. 40 of 1938-39.)
MUDENŪR, RAMDURG TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone standing in the Sōmaliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th-13th Century A.D., refers to the
death of a hero during a cattle raid, at Mudunīru. It mentions Bamma-nṛipāḷa.

inscription.head:
No. 322.
(B.K. No. 42 of 1938-39.)
PANCHGĀV, RAMDURG TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing near a dharmaśālā.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged and worn out record, in characters of about the 12th-13th
Century A.D., seems to register a gift of land to a deity. It refers to Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Brahma-bhūpa who is eulogised at great length.
1) The rest is worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 323.
(B.K. No. 63 of 1938-39.)
MADARKHAṆḌI, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone standing in the Rāmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th-13th Century A.D., registers the death
of Māchaya Boppeyya of Hipparage.

inscription.head:
No. 324.
(B.K. No. 67 of 1938-39.)
TAKKŌḌ, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing in Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record, in characters of about the 12th-13th Century A.D.,
seems to register a gift of land. The extant portion of the date refers to Puṇṇami,
Monday, all other details being lost.

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 325.
(B.K. No. 98 of 1938-39.)
SHIRŪR, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pedestal of a Jaina Tīrthaṅkara image.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th-13th Century A.D., states that the
image of Pārśva-Bhaṭṭāraka was presented to the Kusuva-jīnālaya of Yāpanīya-
saṁgha and Vṛksha-mūla[gaṇa], by Kāḷiseṭṭi of Oṛeyūr (?).

inscription.head:
No. 326.
(B.K. No. 109 of 1938-39.)
GUḌIGĒRI, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Jaina Basti.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th-13th Century A.D., registers the gift
of oil from all the oil-mills of the village by Kētaiyya, son of Javaniseṭṭi, to
Mu[re]ya-basadi of Guḍigere.

l.note: Here the is written and struck off and ḍi is written above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 327.
(B.K. No. 119 of 1938-39.)
KĀMADHĒNU, KALGHATGI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Arch-stone at the top of the door-way in Kallēśvara temple outside the village.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 12th-13th Century A.D., states that this
(i.e. the arch-stone) is the workmanship of Maṇḍaḷika Mādiga.

inscription.head:
No. 328.
(B.K. No. 102 of 1938-39.)
YELIVĀḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone built into the wall of Kalamēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated Śaka 1171, Kīlaka, Chaitra, ba. 3, Sunday
corresponding to A.D. 1248, March 14. The weekday, however, was Saturday
Śaka year being current.

The inscription seems to record the death of a person (name lost) during a fight
(cattle raid) against Jōyidēva of Gutoḷa at the instance of Chaṭṭayadēva. It refers
to anādi-agrahāra Kundugoḷa. Other details are lost. Cf. No. 329 below.

geldu tuṟuvaṁ maraḷci svarggake hōdanintī bīragallu

inscription.head:
No. 329.
(B.K. No. 103 of 1938-39.)
YELIVĀḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone built into the wall of the Kalamēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1171, Kīlaka, Chaitra, ba. 3, Sunday, corresponding to
A.D. 1248, March 14, the weekday being Saturday and Śaka year current.

The inscription records the death of an individual (name lost), in a fight
against Jōyidēva of Gutoḷalu at the instance of Chaṭṭayadēva of Halasige-nāḍu, during a
cattle-raid. It mentions the One Thousand of agrahāra Kuṁdugoḷa.

l.ka: © svasti sakavaruṣa 1171 neya kīlaka saṁvatsarada cai-

inscription.head:
No. 330.
(B.K. No. 88 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up to the right of the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 14th century A.D., is dated the cyclic
year Īsvara, Dvitīya-Phālguṇa, śu. 14, Tuesday. In this century the details of the date
correspond to A.D. 1278, March 8, Tuesday f.d.t. ·75.

The inscription records the death of Chika, son of Gōyanāyaka, who was the
household officer (mane-adhikāri) of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Śāḷuveya Ballugidēva-
Rāṇeya, in a fight with a certain rāhuta.

l.ka: taru gōiyanāyakana maga cikana kalu vritēna la-
kṣaṇa kāyē kā ciṁtā maraṇē raṇē

inscription.head:
No. 331.
(B.K. No. 45 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Left pillar at the eastern entrance into the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record in characters of about the 13th century is dated the cyclic year Ānanda,
Mārgaśira, dvitīya-paksha. The details cannot be verified.

The inscription records the renovation of the temple of Sōmanātha by Guru Nāgē-
śvara who is said to have hailed from Saurāshṭra. The record is in faulty Sanskrit
language.

inscription.head:
No. 332.
(B.K. No. 78 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
The inscription which is engraved in continuation of No. 228 above, records
gifts of land by nāḷprabhu, Chaladaṁkarāma, Hariyama-Seṭṭi and others to Mūṟujāvi-
dēva at the time of Śivarātri. It also records gifts of land and money-income, to
god Svayaṁbhudēva of Kalukere by the eight Hiṭṭus and others. In characters of
about the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 333.
(B.K. No. 103 of 1935-36.)
KŌṆANTAMBIGE, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Kallēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated the cyclic year Prabhava, Sunday, Amāvāsya, Solar eclipse,
Saṁkramaṇa, the details being not enough for verification. It may be assigned to
about the 13th Century A.D. on palaeographical grounds.

The inscription records a gift of a charity-house for the benefit of the temple of
Kalidēva at Hosavura by pradhāni Chaṭṭarasa, Kūcharsa and sāhaṇi Nāraṇadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 334.
(B.K. No. 115 of 1935-36.)
MADBHĀVI, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab built into the plinth of Kallēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record, in characters of about the 13th Century A.D., registers
a gift of land by several individuals of whom the names of Bhīma-Bhaṭṭa and
Nīlakaṇṭha-Bhaṭṭa are preserved. It records further gifts by the sons of Ayaṁgaḷu.
The details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 335.
(B.K. No. 7 of 1936-37.)
ĀLMĒL, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying on the road to Kaḍṇi (about 2 miles from Ālmēl).

inscription.p:
The inscription states that (the stone) was the north-western boundary of the
gift-picotta. In characters of about the 13th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 336.
(B.K. No. 9 of 1936-37.)
ĀLMĒL, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying on the road to Kaḍṇi (about 2 miles from Ālmēl).

inscription.p:
This damaged record, in characters of about the 13th Century A.D., seems to
register a gift of land to god Lōkēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 337.
(B.K. No. 16 of 1936-37.)
CHAṬṬARKI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Base of a soulpture on the outer wall of Dattātrēya temple.

inscription.p:
The purport of this record in characters of 13th Century A.D., is not clear. It
refers to Rudra-lōka.

inscription.head:
No. 338.
(B.K. No. 70 of 1936-37.)
MALGHĀṆ, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Side of the slab fixed in the front wall of Mallikārjuna temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record contains verses in praise of Mādirāja and Ēchiyakka. In
characters of about the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 339.
(B.K. No. 76 of 1936-37.)
PAḌAGĀNŪR, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
The record is badly damaged and it seems to register a gift of land and oil to a
deity (name lost) by the Seṭṭi, Karaṇas and Telligas. All other details are lost. In
characters of about the 13th Century A.D.

l.ka: ḷu koṭṭa mattaṁ telligaru • • • gāṇadalu paḷige yaṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 340.
(B.K. No. 12 of 1937-38.)
BENAKANHAḶḶI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone leaning on the wall of the temple called Hiroḍeya.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 13th Century A.D., registers a gift of land
for a charitable picotta.

inscription.head:
No. 341.
(B.K. No. 18 of 1937-38.)
CHAḌCHAṆ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Third face of a rectangular stone standing in front of the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record, in characters of about the 13th Century A.D., seems
to register a gift land.

inscription.head:
No. 342.
(B.K. No. 19 of 1937-38.)
CHAḌCHAṆ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragmentary stone built into the basement of the village gate.

inscription.p:
This damaged record, in characters of about the 13th Century A.D., contains a
portion of Siddarāma-Vachana dedicated to Kapilasiddha-Mallikārjuna.

l.ka: © śrī rāmanātha[nē śaraṇu] ·ōṁ jaya paramēsvara paramā-

l.note: The record is broken after this.

inscription.head:
No. 343.
(B.K. No. 34 of 1937-38.)
HIRĒ-BEVINŪR, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing near the Siddharāmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription, in characters of about the 13th century A.D., records the impre-
cation against the misappropriation of the gifts (?) made to the temples of Bhōga-
nātha, Rēvaṇasiddha, Mallikārjuna, Taleyakāva-Rāmēśvara, Brahmēśvara and
Mailāradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 344.
(B.K. No. 45 of 1937-38.)
NĀDA (KHURD), INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing near the village-gate.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 13th Century A.D., states that the stone
indicates the boundary of the field to the west of the village Arjunige and east of the
village Nada.

inscription.head:
No. 345.
(B.K. No. 50 of 1937-38.)
NIMBĀḶ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Fragment of stone built into the wall (inner-side), left of entrance into the
raṅga-maṇḍapa of the Śaṅkaraliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 13th Century A.D., seems to register a
gift of toll incomes on certain articles of merchandise.

inscription.head:
No. 346.
(B.K. No. 54 of 1937-38.)
NIMBĀḶ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Right face of a fragment of stone lying in the compound of Śaṅkaraliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record, in characters of about the 13th Century A.D., registers
a gift of land and house-site to Barmayya, by Sōmayya, devotee of Kōṭiśaṁkaradēva,
for the maintenance of a charity picotta.

inscription.head:
No. 347.
(B.K. No. 55 of 1937-38.)
NIMBĀḶ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Fragment of a slab built into the wall (outside), left of entrance into
the raṅga-maṇḍapa of Śaṅkaraliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record is in characters of about the 13th century A.D. It seems
to register gifts of land, house-site and income from certain taxes probably to the
Śaṅkaraliṅga temple. The details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 348.
(B.K. No. 56 of 1937-38.)
NIMBĀḶ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone fixed as arch-stone at the top of the front gate way of the Śaṅkaraliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record, in characters of about the 13th Century A.D., refers to
year 20. It mentions the boundaries of a gift land. It also registers a gift of land
to god Paṅchaliṅgadēva. The details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 349.
(B.K. No. 57 of 1937-38.)
NIMBĀḶ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone fixed as arch-stone at the top of the front gate-way of the Saṅkaraliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 13th Century A.D., contains an impreca-
tion against the destruction of some gift.

inscription.head:
No. 350.
(B.K. No. 82 of 1937-38.)
UMRĀJ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of stone built into the back-wall of the village School.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 13th Century A.D., registers a gift of
land by Muṭṭarasa to god Trikūṭadēva and for the upkeep of the sabhā-maṇṭapa of
the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 351.
(B.K. No. 39 of 1938-39.)
MUDĒNŪR, RAMDURG TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying in front of Rāmaliṅgasvāmi temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 13th Century, A.D., seems to register
a gift of land by Sileyaṇagavuṇḍa. The language is corrupt.

l.note: The letter la is written above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 352.
(B.K. No. 45 of 1938-39.)
SŌPAḌLA, RAMDURG TALUK, BELGAUM DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in the Saṅgamēśvara temple in Survey No. 12.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 13th Century A.D., states that it is the
oil-mill belonging to god Mūlasthāna-Mallikārjunadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 353.
(B.K. No. 54 of 1938-39.)
JAMKHAṆḌI, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Fragment of stone at Chennabasappa Arakēri's house, now kept in the house
of Mr. W.T. Apte.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged and mutilated record in Sanskrit language is in characters
of about the 13th Century A.D.

Only a portion of the invocatory verses at the beginning is preserved, the rest
of the record being worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 354.
(B.K. No. 79 of 1938-39.)
KUNDGŌḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pedestal of the Tīrthaṅkara image built into the wall of the A.V. School.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record in characters of about the 13th Century A.D.,
refers to Mūla-saṁgha. All other details being lost.

inscription.head:
No. 355.
(B.K. No. 80 of 1935-36.)
KALKĒRI, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record which is engraved below No. 196, in characters of about the
13th-14th Century A.D., is dated in the cyclic year Sarvadhāri, Māghā, ba. 30, Saturday,
Solar eclipse. The details cannot be verified.

The inscription records a gift, at the instance of Pradhāna Bākenāyaka, of certain
toll income by Immadi Siṅganāyaka-Voḍeya, Dēmarasa and Tipparasa, for the
benefit of the Sōmanātha temple.

inscription.head:
No. 356.
(B.K. No. 98 of 1935-36.)
SIDĒNŪR, HIREKERUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Māsti-stone set up in the open site of Hirē-matha.

inscription.p:
This damaged record states that the bīra-gallu for Channappa and the māsti-
kallu for his wife Nāgaundi were installed. It is in characters of about the 13th-14th
Century A.D.

l.note: On two side are ent two figures, one

inscription.head:
No. 357.
(B.K. No. 128 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record states that Vibhūti Gauraya disciple of Panditārādhya of Orugaṇṭi,
seeks refuge in god. In characters of about the 13th-14th century.

inscription.head:
No. 358.
(B.K. No. 51 of 1936-37.)
KALKĒRI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone set up near the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This damaged record refers to a Hegaḍe-mānya, in characters of about the
13th-14th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 359.
(B.K. No. 24 of 1937-38.)
HALSAṄGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of a stone lying in the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record, in characters of about the 13th-14th Century A.D.,
seems to register a gift of land and house-site by the mūligas and several persons to
god Svayaṁbhudēva.

inscription.head:
No. 360.
(B.K. No. 64 of 1938-39.)
SĀVAḶIGE, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone set up behind the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 13th-14th Century A.D., states that this
is the mānya of the Brāhmaṇas.

inscription.head:
No. 361.
(B.K. No. 68 of 1938-39.)
TUṄGAḶ, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of stone built into the wall of Mallayya temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record in characters of about the 13th-14th Century A.D., registers
gifts of land, oil-mill, money, vegetables and corn for the benefit of a temple (name
lost). Other details are also lost.

inscription.head:
No. 362.
(B.K. No. 92 of 1938-39.)
KUYIBĀḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone standing near the mosque close to the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 13th-14th Century A.D., is dated Vais11ākha
s11u. 10, Monday, the other details being not given.
The inscription records the death of a hero whose name is not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 363.
(B.K. No. 105 of 1938-39.)
YELIVĀḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in a ruined well near the Kalamēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 13th-14th Century A.D., is dated in the
cyclic year Śārvvari, Māgha, śu. 1, Sunday.
It mentions Lakhumeya-nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 364.
(B.K. No. 22 of 1937-38.)
CHIKKA-MASAḶI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing in front of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1229, Plavaṅga, Bhādrapada, Amāvāsyā, Monday,
corresponding to A.D. 1307, August 28, the month being adhika-Bhādrapada.

This inscription records a gift of land to mahāprabhu Kēśavadēva by Haridēva
Rāṇeya.

l.ka: ṁ koṭṭaru maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 365.
(B.K. No. 5 of 1937-38.)
AGARKHĒḌ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in the field called Kāḍappanavara hola, about three miles from the village.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated Śaka 1273, Khara, Bhādrapad Paurṇami,
Wednesday, Lunar eclipse. The tithi corresponds to A.D. 1351, September 5, when the
weekday was Tuesday. The eclipse did not occur on that day.
The inscription seems to record a gift of land to god Sōmanātha of Agrā[khēḍa].

inscription.head:
No. 366.
(B.K. No. 43 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar near the southern entrance into the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in the cyclic year Prajōtpatti (Prajāpati), Phālguṇa, ba. 5,
Thursday. On palaeographical grounds, the record, may be assigned to the 14th
Century A.D. The intended date might be A.D. 1392 March 14.

The inscription records that Bhairavadēva, son of Kheyidēva, who was a
servant of Kumāra Sivarāma Ayya, renovated the southern gate (teṁkaṇa dāraṭa),
obviously of the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.head:
No. 367.
(B.K. No. 126 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 14th Century A.D., registers a gift to
Bikkidēva of Kādaṁba-kula, a pupil of Lākulēśvaradēva, for the benefit of the temple
of Madhukanātha.

l.ka: lajyātarumappa śrī bikkidēvayyagaḷa kayyalu śrī madhukanāthadēvari-
gi koṭṭudu yī dharmavanū ·āvanānobbhanu pālisidātanū gaṁgā-
(ya)nū vadhisidātanū || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁ-
dharā ṣaṣṭirvvaruṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi || dāna-
sētuṁ nṟupāṇāṁ kālē kālē pālanīyō bhavadbhiḥ sarvānnētā-

inscription.head:
No. 368.
(B.K. No. 1 of 1936-37.)
ĀHĒRI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing near the Rāmaliṅga temple locally called dēgula.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 14th Century A.D., seems to record a gift
of land. It mentions Permāḍidēva.

inscription.head:
No. 369.
(B.K. No. 2 of 1936-37.)
ĀLMĒL, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in front of the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 14th Century A.D., states that he who
destroys this (gift of) land commits the sin of killing the cows.

inscription.head:
No. 370.
(B.K. No. 3 of 1936-37.)
ĀLMĒL, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in front of the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
The inscription, in characters of about the 14th Century A.D., records a gift of
land to god Traipurushadēva by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Sōvadēvarasa for the merit of
Chandaladēvi. Mentions Nāgadēvabhaṭa.

l.note: The letterva is written above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 371.
(B.K. No. 11 of 1936-37.)
BAMMADĒVANAMAḌU, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone set up in a field of Sangappa about a mile to the west of the village.

inscription.p:
It states that the land was the mānya of Jakeyanāyaka. In characters of about
the 14th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 372.
(B.K. No. 77 of 1936-37.)
RĀMANHAḶḶI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing in Survey No. 23 on the way to Almēl.

inscription.p:
The record states that this is the boundary stone of the land belonging to god
Paṁkabbēśvara. In characters of about the 14th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 373.
(B.K. No. 86 of 1936-37.)
YĀRGAL (BHUJRUKH), SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of land by manneya Bōyarasa. In characters of
about the 14th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 374.
(B.K. No. 85 of 1937-38.)
JHALKI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken stone built into the wall of the Hirēmatha.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record, in characters of about the 14th Century A.D., contains
only the imprecatory portion.

inscription.head:
No. 375.
(B.K. No. 86 of 1937-38.)
TŌṆṬĀPŪR, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing near the bastion.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 14th Century A.D., states that the stone
marks the north-western boundary of the land belonging to Bhilladēva-nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 376.
(B.K. No. 87 of 1937-38.)
TŌṆṬĀPŪR, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying in Hullappana hola.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 14th Century A.D., states that the stone
marks the boundary of the land belonging to Nāyimēśvaradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 377.
(B.K. No. 69 of 1938-39.)
TUṄGAḶ, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing near the Paramānanda temple outside the village.

inscription.p:
This damaged record, in characters of about the 14th Century A.D., refers to
some gift by the residents of the village Tuṁguḷa.

inscription.head:
No. 378.
(B.K. No. 83 of 1938-39.)
KUNDGŌḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pedestal of the māsti stone near the Hanumantadēva temple.

inscription.p:
The purport of this damaged record, in characters of about the 14th Century A.D.
is not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 379.
(B.K. No. 99 of 1938-39.)
SHIRŪR, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying in the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This damaged and worn out record, in characters of about the 14th Century A.D.,
mentions Hulīgere.

inscription.head:
No. 380.
(B.K. No. 44 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Right pillar at the eastern entrance into the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1354, Virōdhikṛit, Māgha, ba. 30, Monday, Solar
eclipse. The details are a little irregular. They correspond to A.D. 1432, February
2. But the week-day was Saturday and the Śaka year current when there was a
solar eclipse on that day.

The inscription records a gift of toll-income to the temple of Sōmanātha of
Purikara-nagara, by Aḷiya Viṭharasa at the instance of Ārādhyadēva of ubhayamārga.

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 381.
(B.K. No. 10 of 1937-38.)
BABLĀD, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone built into the platform in front of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged and effaced record is dated Śaka 1362, Raudri, Bhādrapada,
śu. 11, Tuesday, corresponding to A.D. 1440, September 6, Tuesday, f.d.t. ·92.
The inscription seems to record a gift of land by certain individuals.

inscription.head:
No. 382.
(B.K. No. 48 of 1938-39.)
ĀLGŪR, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pedestal of the image of Ādinātha-tīrthaṅkara.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated Śaka [13]66, Raktākshi, Māgha, ba., Sunday, the
tithi being not given. The intended date may be A.D. 1445, January 24 or 31, Sunday.
The inscription seems to record the installation of the image of Ādinātha.

l.ka:
svasti śrī nṛpaśālivāha[na] saka [13]66 varṣa ratākṣināma saṁ • • • • •
mā[ghē] kṛṣṇapakṣē ravivā[ra*] ·alagurada

inscription.head:
No. 383.
(B.K. No. 70 of 1937-38.)
SĀTULGĀV, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Top of the door frame of Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1[40]3, Plava, Mārgas11ira, śu. [7], Saturday, corresponding
to A.D. 1481, November 27, the week day being Tuesday and not Saturday.

This inscription records the completion of a hall (sabhā) evidently in the temple
of Hanumān. It mentions Rāmarasa who perhaps was responsible for this act.

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 384.
(B.K. No. 47 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Fragment of a broken slab lying near the Dharmanta's house
near the Sōmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
Only the concluding portion of the record is preserved. It seems to record the
gift of a picotta. It refers to the boundaries of Lakśhumaṇēśvara written at the
instance of Mallika[Nā]datta-Vaḍeya, and to a gift to Liṁgaṇagauḍa son of
Sōmaṇagauḍa, of Huligere. In characters of about the 15th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 385.
(B.K. No. 97 of 1935-36.)
SIḌĒNŪR, HIREKERUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab lying in Survey No. 2.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 15th Century A.D., is dated the cyclic year
Raudra [Pausha, śu.] 5, Tuesday. The details cannot be verified. It records the death
of Mallayya, son of Bhayiraya-nāyaka, and younger brother Māchaya.

inscription.head:
No. 386.
(B.K. No. 78 of 1936-37.)
RĀMANHAḶḶI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing in Survey No. 22.

inscription.p:
This record, in Nāgari characters and Sanskrit language, is damaged after the
date portion. The details of the date are the cyclic year Pradhāvi (Paridhāvi),
Vaiśākha. In characters of about the 15th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 387.
(B.K. No. 72 of 1937-38.)
TAḌAVALGĀ, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing in the site of Hirēmaṭha near the Dharmaśāla.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 15th Century A.D. refers to the gift of
50 mattar of field at Iṇḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 388.
(B.K. No. 14 of 1938-39.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Mālegitti Sivālaya.

inscription.p: This record, in characters of about the 15th Century A.D., mentions Bādāmi.

inscription.head:
No. 389.
(B.K. No. 133 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Outer side of the wall of the Madhukēśvara shrine in the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 15th-16th Century A.D., gives the names
of Bhūtanātha-Yogēśvara-Voḍeya, Sadāsiva-Voḍeya, Hire-Sarvēśvara-Voḍeya and
Channapa-Voḍeya.

l.ka:
© staḷada bhūtanāta yōgēśvaravaḍēru | sadāsivavaḍērū | hiresarvēsvaravaḍērū
caṁnāpavaḍērū

inscription.head:
No. 390.
(B.K. No. 31 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of Vīrabhadra temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1435, Śrīmukha, Bhādrapada, śu. 5, Sunday correspon-
ding to A.D. 1513, September 4, the month being Nija Bhādrapada.

The inscription records a gift in the presence of god Dakshiṇa-Sōmēśvara
of flower-garden by Singiseṭṭi-arasa of Kūḍalige to the temple of Maruḷa-Siddhēśvara,
named after the Śaiva preceptor Maruḷasiddha who had come to Huligere. The
record is damaged towards the end.
