inscription.head:
No. 391.
(B.K. No. 18 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the wall of the backyard of the Taluk Office.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated Śaka 14 • •, Vilambi, Prathama-Jyēshṭha, Śu• 1,
Monday. The details regularly correspond to A.D. 1538, April 29, if the Śaka year
was 1460.

The inscription registers a deed of settlement of dispute between the Jainas
headed by Saṁkhaṇāchārya and Hēmaṇāchārya of the Śaṅkha-basti and Kāḷahastidēva
and Śivarāmadēva of the temple of Dakshiṇa-Sōmēśvaradēva regarding the use of
certain honours which had been misappropriated by the Jainas for their deity.

inscription.head:
No. 392.
(B.K. No. 122 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing in the South-West corner of the compound of Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record, in characters of about the 16th Century A.D., seems
to register a gift of land, the details of which are all lost. It refers to some nāyakas
as witnesses of the gift.

l.ka: ṇaṁ pu(ṁ)ṇyaṁ paradattānupā[la]na • • • ttāpahārēṇa svadataṁ ni-
ttu svargamāpnōti pālanā • • • padaṁ || sākṣigaḷa vivarā || śrī

inscription.head:
No. 393.
(B.K. No. 134 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Outer side of the wall of the Madhukēśvara shrine in the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p: The record mentions god Madhuliṅga. In characters of about the 16th Century A.D.

l.ka: © [lū] vāra mommakkaḷa saṁtatti || śrī madhuliṁgadēvara pādavē gati

inscription.head:
No. 394.
(B.K. No. 135 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Outer side of the wall of Madhukēśvara shrine in Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
The inscription refers to the family of the former Voḍeyas of Banavase-sthaḷa.
In characters of about the 16th Century A.D.

l.ka:
© banavase sthaḷada pūrvada vaḍērugaḷa sa[ṁ]tatti | [yivarū] | śrīmadhulliṁgadēvara
pādavē gati ||

inscription.head:
No. 395.
(B.K. No. 136 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Outer side of the wall of the Madhukēśvara shrine in the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 16th Century A.D., refers to the three
aṅkaṇas of the Voḍeyas of Banavāse-sthaḷa.

inscription.head:
No. 396.
(B.K. No. 137 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Outer side of the wall of the Madhukēśvara shrine in the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
The inscription mentions Channappa-Vaḍeya, Liṅgappa-Vaḍeya and Snivappa-
Vaḍeya of the former family of Vaḍeyas of Banavase-sthaḷa. In characters of
about the 16th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 397.
(B.K. No. 138 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Outer side of the wall of the Madhukēśvara shrine in the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
The inscription mentions Śankara-Vaḍeya, Madhukappa-Vaḍeya and Vīrappa-
Vaḍeya of Kaḍugōḍa-sthaḷa. In characters of about the 16th Century A.D.

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 398.
(B.K. No. 139 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Outer side of the wall of the Madhukēśvara shrine in the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 16th Century A.D., mentions Madhukappa-
Vaḍeya, Māllapa-Vaḍeya and Sivappa-Vaḍeya.

l.ka: śrīmatu | staḷada madhukapavaḍēru | māllapa vaḍēru śivapavaḍēru |

inscription.head:
No. 399.
(B.K. No. 140 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Outer side of the wall of the Madhukēśvara shrine in the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
The record mentions Channappa-Vaḍeya of Bijaraṇe-sthaḷa, Keṅchappa-Vaḍeya
and Madhukappa-Vaḍeya. In characters of about the 16th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 400.
(B.K. No. 141 of 1935-36.)
BANAVĀSI, SIRSI TALUK, NORTH KANARA DISTRICT.

p.i: Outer side of the wall of the Madhukēśvara shrine in the Madhukēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
The inscription mentions Paṅchāgai Beṁki-Vaḍeya, Chigare-Vaḍeya,
Kālāntaka-Vaḍeya, Mṛityuṅjaya-Vaḍeya and Honna-Vadeya. In characters of
about the 16th Century A.D.

l.ka:
sthaḷada paṁcāgni beṁ[kki]vaḍeru | cigare vaḍeru | kālāṁ[tta]kavaḍēru | mrutyuṁja
vaḍērū • • •

inscription.head:
No. 401.
(B.K. No. 28 of 1936-37.)
HIPPARAGI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone fixed into the wall of the well called Kuṁbāra-bhāvi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated cyclic years Śārvari, Chaitra, ba. (mistake for s11u.)
Paurṇami, Chandrahōre, Pūrvā-nakshatra. The details cannot be verified. It is in
characters of about the 16th Century A.D.
The inscription records the construction of a well by Guṇḍayya (?) of Hipparige.

inscription.head:
No. 402.
(B.K. No. 97 of 1936-37.)
BIJĀPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab No. 51 in the Archaeological Museum.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record, in Nāgarī characters and Marāṭhi language, seems
to refer to Sahāji and Sumanashāha. In characters of about the 16th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 403.
(B.K. No. 21 of 1938-39.)
BĀDĀMI, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Boulder to the left of the house of Appaṇa-bhaṭṭara Aṇṇappa.

inscription.p:
This inscription, in characters of about the 16th Century A.D., records the obei-
sance of a person (name lost) to Jagadīśvara, Gaṇapati and Nārāyaṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 404.
(B.K. No. 72 of 1938-39.)
BEṬADŪR, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying near the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 16th Century A.D., states that the field was
the mānya of Mādapa, son of Gaṁgapa, belonging to Āśvalāyana-sūtra, Atrēya-gōtra
and Ṛk-śākhā.

inscription.head:
No. 405.
(B.K. No. 77 of 1938-39.)
KAṀḌOḶḶI, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone leaning against the front wall inside the Basavaṇṇa temple.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record, in characters of about the 16th Century A.D., men-
tions Kuṁdagoḷa and refers to a maṭha.

inscription.head:
No. 406.
(B.K. No. 8 of 1937-38.)
ARJUṆGI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in the field called Ākaḷakallu-hola.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 16th-17th Century A.D., states that this is
the S11āsana-kallu marking the boundary of the field to the south of Arjuṇige and to
the north of Mārisiṅgana-haḷi.

inscription.head:
No. 407.
(B.K. No. 108 of 1938-39.)
GUḌIGĒRI, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into the back-wall of Lakumēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in characters of about the 16th-17th Century A.D., is dated in the
cyclic year Pārthiva, Kārttika, s11u. 7. The details cannot be verified.
It records a gift of mānya to Chauṇḍarasa by Bammanāsana Khāna-sāhēba (?).

inscription.head:
No. 408.
(B.K. No. 48 of 1936-37.)
KALKĒRI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in Hirēmaṭha.

inscription.p:
The extant portion of the date of this damaged record is Saumya, Pushya, śu.
13, Wednesday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkramaṇa. On palaeographical grounds the date may be
probably equated with A.D. 1609, December 27, Wednesday, f.d.t. ·66. The saṁkra-
maṇa occurred on the next day.

The inscription registers a gift of oil for a lamp to a deity by some trade-guilds
(sameya) at Kalkere.

inscription.head:
No. 409.
(B.K. No. 20 of 1936-37.)
DĒVAṆGĀV, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab standing in Survey No. 539.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1557, Yuva, Āshāḍa, ba. 13, Thursday, corresponding to
1635 A.D., July 2.

The inscription, in Nāgari characters and Marāṭḥī language, seems to record a
fight between Pādas11āhi Suḷatāna Mahamada Arakā[ḍa] and rājādhirāja Murāri Paṇḍit-
[ēs11vara] on the bank of the Bhīmarathī near Dēvaṇagāu.

inscription.head:
No. 410.
(B.K. No. 29 of 1936-37.)
HIPPARAGI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone fixed into the front wall of the Hanumān temple near the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1566, Tāraṇa, Srāvaṇa, śu. 1 Friday. The tithi corresponds
to A.D. 1644, July 24, when the weekday was Wednesday instead of Friday.

The inscription, in Nāgari characters and Marāṭhī language, seems to record the
construction of Hanumān temple, in stone, and a gift of land made for it.

l.de: da sāmasta praja mai jehī paragaṇa
ḷa ghaghuva sadīḷavāra gaunara gunadeve yā-
maḷā to sīdīḷa se kīdīṁ mabhu

inscription.head:
No. 411.
(B.K. No. 107 of 1935-36.)
YELLĀPUR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone fixed into the raised platform in the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1597, Rākshasa, Pushya, ba. 15, corresponding to A.D.,
1676 January 5, Wednesday.

The inscription records that Dēśāyi Liṅgapagauḍa of Gutaḷa made grants (of
land) in Maḍatira-sīme which was his uṁbaḷi to several individuals mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 412.
(B.K. No. 109 of 1935-36.)
JUMNĀḶ, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone fixed into a wall of the Dēyavva temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1621, Pramāthi, Dakshiṇāyana, Bhādrapada, Paurṇami.
Wednesday, Pūrvā-Bhādrapada-nakshatra, corresponding to 1699 A.D., August 30.
There was, however, no Saṁkrānti on that day.

The inscription, in Sanskrit language and Nāgari characters, states that a well
was constructed by Rāya-Paṇḍita, son of Rāmachandra who was the son of
Tīrthēs11a.

inscription.head:
No. 413.
(B.K. No. 81 of 1936-37.)
SINDGI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Top of the stone door leading into the interior of the Raṅgamaṭha.

inscription.p:
The first ten lines of this inscription, in characters of the 17th Century A.D.,
are engraved negatively. It seems to register a gift of some Vṛittis to Siddha-
swāmi of the maṭha.

inscription.head:
No. 414.
(B.K. No. 44 of 1937-38.)
MARASANAHAḶḶI, INDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing in front of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record, in characters of about the 17th Century A.D., seems to
record a gift of land.

inscription.head:
No. 415.
(B.K. No. 80 of 1938-39.)
KUNDGŌḶ, KUNDGOL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Stone standing in the compound of the District Bungalow
(brought from Kuyibāḷ.)

inscription.p:
This damaged record, in characters of about the 17th Century A.D.,
mentions the village Kuyibāḷa and refers to a nāyaka (name lost).

inscription.head:
No. 416.
(B.K. No. 104 of 1935-36.)
UDAGAṬṬI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab fixed in a wall of Kūḍali S11ṛiṅgēri-maṭha.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1635, Jaya, Mārgas11ira, śu. Champā-shashṭhi, correspond-
ing to A.D. 1714, December 1, the Śaka year being 1636.

The inscription records a gift of land in the village Udagaṭṭi, in Meḍalēri which
was the uṁbaḷi of the donor, Bāhadara Hanumantagauḍa, son of Liṅgapagauḍa and
grandson of Dēs11āyi Hanumantagauḍa of Gutaḷala, to the maṭha of Śrī Narasimha-
Bhārati-Swāmi of Śṛiṅgēri.

l.ka: neya jaya saṁvatsarada margasira śudha caṁpāṣaṣṭipa-

inscription.head:
No. 417.
(B.K. No. 105 of 1935-36.)
UDAGAṬṬI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab fixed in a wall of Kūḍali S11ṛiṅgēri-maṭha.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1646, Vis11vāvasu, Pushya, ba. 6, corresponding to
A.D. 1726, January 12, when the weekday was Wednesday. The Śaka year was 1647.

The inscription records that Srī Śankara-Bhāratī-Svāṁi got constructed the
to of Śrī Narasiṁha-Bhāratī-Svāmi, disciple of Vṛiddha-Sankara-Bharatī-Svāmi
of Sṛingēri, who passed away at Udugaṭi on the above-mentioned date. He also had a
temple of Śiva built and made a gift of land at Hirēbidare for the benefit of this
temple.

inscription.head:
No. 418.
(B.K. No. 59 of 1938-39.)
KALHAḶḶI, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone built into the left wall of the Varāha shrine in the Veṅkaṭēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record, in Sanskrit language and Nāgarī characters, is dated Śaka 1729,
Prabhava, phālguṇa, ba. 12, Wednesday, corresponding to A.D. 1808, March 23.
The inscription states that a certain S11ēsha got constructed the Varāha temple.

inscription.head:
No. 419.
(B.K. No. 60 of 1938-39.)
KALHAḶḶI, JAMKHANDI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone built into the right wall of the Varāha shrine in the Veṅkaṭēs11vara temple.

inscription.p:
This record is dated Śaka 1730, Vibhava, Māgha, śu. 12, Monday. The tithi
corresponds to A.D. 1809, January 16.

The inscription states that the image of Varāha was installed on the hill
of Kallaḷī by Śēsha.

inscription.head:
No. 420.
(B.K. No. 49 of 1935-36.)
LAKSHMĒS11VAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone set up near the Agastya-tīrtha.

inscription.p:
It records the installation of the image of god Rāmachandradēva at Agāttipura,
by Timmaṇa, son of Chavuḍada-Rāsaya. In late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 421.
(B.K. No. 68 of 1935-36.)
BĀḶAṀBĪḌ, HANGAL TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the village Chāvḍi.

inscription.p: This record, in late characters, registers the death of Elle-Nāyaka at Hommāḍi.
